and writes to it before closing out.

## ACTIVE TASK

### What Session 228 Did

**Deliverable:** **Slice 1 of issue \#95 option C – the script-core
targeted suppression.**
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
now suppresses the issue-#9 `+ sexMean / 2` unknown-parent correction
*targeted by override side*: a one-missing-parent animal KEEPS the
correction for a known-side override (case b – previously wrongly
dropped by D11 blanket-A) and has it suppressed only when an override
stands in for its *missing* side (case a), via a new optional
`missingSideFor` column. No `missingSideFor` column =\> byte-identical
to today (blanket-A, D10). **DONE** on branch `issue95-optionC-slice1`.
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-28 / 2026-06-28 **Status:** **DONE.**
**Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED `5448a1a8` -\> GREEN `4cd3e509`;
REFACTOR offered, owner chose skip).** Phase declared each response;
**all three phase gates (PRE-RED-\>RED, RED-\>GREEN, GREEN-\>REFACTOR)
via `AskUserQuestion`**. **0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner
overrides.** - **PRE-RED recon (firsthand):** read
`checkKinshipOverrides.R` / `applyKinshipOverrides.R` (the column rides
through name-based access – no matrix-apply change),
`test_checkKinshipOverrides.R` +
`test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` (the existing suites to
extend), the `i13_correctBlanketA` oracle (`test_reportGV.R:506-525`),
and confirmed `overriddenIds` is used ONLY at the correction call
(`reportGV.R:140,159,179`) so a `suppressIds` threads cleanly.
**Load-bearing finding:** `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`’s `:164`
guard ALREADY suppresses whatever set it is passed, so option C needs NO
code change there – only the CALLER computes a narrower set (C3). The
genuine RED therefore lives in the new classifier + the validator’s new
check + the caller’s flipped case, NOT the unchanged core. - **RED
(`5448a1a8`):** NEW `test_classifyOverrideMissingSide.R` (7 cases -\>
error, helper absent); `test_checkKinshipOverrides.R` (+4: a
`missingSideFor` not naming id1/id2 -\> stop \[FAILS at HEAD – no
validation\]; accept/NA-blank/dedup-C1.2 stay green); `test_reportGV.R`
(+ the discriminating case-(b) test: blank `missingSideFor` KEEPS the
prior \[FAILS at HEAD – blanket-A drops it; only 1/280 animals
differ\]; + an independent `i13_correctOptionC` oracle + a case-(a)
suppress test that stays green);
`test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` (+2 redefined-contract
characterization, pass at HEAD). Confirmed clean-for-the-right-reason: 7
helper errors + 1 validator failure + 3 case-(b) assertions fail; all
else green. - **GREEN (`4cd3e509`, minimal):** `checkKinshipOverrides`
validates the optional `missingSideFor` column (NA-\>““; each non-blank
cell must name id1/id2 of its row, else stop; dedup key UNCHANGED,
C1.2) + roxygen. NEW `R/classifyOverrideMissingSide.R` (`@noRd`, C5: the
one-unknown focals named by a non-blank side, via `isU(ped$sire/dam)`;
NULL/zero-row/absent-column -\> `character(0L)`). `reportGV` computes
`suppressIds` (present column -\> `classifyOverrideMissingSide(...)`;
absent -\> the full set = blanket-A / D10) and passes it as
`overriddenIds` (C3). `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`
`@param overriddenIds` docstring redefined to”the set to suppress”
(guard unchanged – NO behavior change). `gvaConvergence` untouched
(still blanket-A via the full set – Slice 2). - **Verify (all clean):**
the 4 touched test files green; clean regression read **0 failed / 0
error** (1172 real files, incl. & excl. `test-app-`/`test-e2e-`,

### What Session 227 Did

**Deliverable:** **Phase 0 for issue \#95 option C – ratified C1 + C2
(and confirmed C3/C4/C5/C6) via `/grill-me`, grounded in a firsthand
numeric check on real `qcPed`.** Updated
`docs/planning/issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md`: STATUS
flipped DRAFT -\> **RATIFIED (S227)**, §3 header updated, §7 checklist
filled with the decided values, and a NEW **§8 ratification record**
added (firsthand numeric evidence + a re-runnable reproduction recipe +
the load-bearing reframing). **DONE.** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-28 / 2026-06-28 **Status:** **DONE.** **Planning / Phase-0
decision session – TDD code-phases N/A** (the deliverable is the
ratified DECISIONS; Slice-1 implementation is a SEPARATE later session –
FM \#18). Phase declared PRE-RED / Planning each response. **0
stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** – every recommendation
ratified as written (“Confirm all as recommended” at the consolidated
gate). - **Firsthand numeric check BEFORE the grill (the D11/S214 bar),
on real `qcPed`** (`scratchpad/c2_numeric_check.R`; recipe preserved in
plan §8B): replicated the `reportGV.R:118-180` mean-kinship path
(`getGVPopulation` -\> `kinship` -\> `filterKinMatrix` -\> `meanKinship`
-\> `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`). Found: the `+ sexMean/2` prior =
**median ~1.34 SD** of the mean-kinship spread (280 probands, 43
one-unknown); a single half-sib override justifies only **~10%** of that
prior (prior/raw ~9.9x); dropping one animal’s prior swings its rank
**49-121 of 280**. An Explore agent recovered S214’s recorded numbers
(“~1 SD”, “8.4x”, rank \#6-\>#179) – mine **independently reproduce
them**, which IS the adversarial verification for the load-bearing
number. - **The decisive reframing (verified logically) that made C2 a
clean call:** rule (i) suppresses the prior iff an override informs the
focal’s MISSING side; blanket-A suppresses for ANY override -\> rule (i)
suppresses a **strict SUBSET** of blanket-A -\> it is a **Pareto
improvement over today** (newly fixes case b – known-side overrides;
leaves case a exactly as today). So the alarming ~1.2 SD case-(a)
residual is **pre-existing, not introduced by option C**; (ii) (the
genetically complete fix) is safely deferrable. - **Grilled in
dependency order:** Q1 = the upstream domain judgment “are real
overrides mostly known-side / missing-side / a mix?” (owner: **genuine
mix** -\> option C worth building) -\> C2 (genetics) -\> C1 (schema) -\>
C1.2 (the one genuinely-open sub-decision) -\> a consolidated confirm of
C3/C4/C5/C6 + order + D10. C1.1 + C1.3 are FORCED by the D10
byte-identical invariant -\> stated as settled in prose, not asked
(reserve `AskUserQuestion` for genuinely-open / genetics calls).
Grounded C4 firsthand (`test_reportGV.R:545-555`) before recommending
it.

**Deliverable:** **Plan-mode / architecture design for issue \#95
“option C”** – targeted suppression of the kinship-override /

**missing** parent side (case a), and KEEP it for known-side-only
overrides (case b), which the shipped v1 blanket-A (D11) wrongly
discards. **Wrote one plan document:**
`docs/planning/issue95-optionC-targeted-suppression-plan.md` – with a
firsthand grep-based consumer inventory (current `file:line`), C1-C6
design decisions (UNRATIFIED), vertical slices with per-phase
DONE/Verify/session-boundary/dragons, and the §7 owner-ratification
checklist. **DONE.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-28 / 2026-06-28
**Status:** **DONE.** **Planning / architecture session – TDD
code-phases N/A** (the plan IS the deliverable; implementation is a
SEPARATE later session, gated on Phase-0 ratification; phase declared
PRE-RED / Planning each response, like the S213 issue-13 plan session).

option C design for issue \#95” – mapped cleanly to the ARCHITECTURE
workstream; no scope `AskUserQuestion` needed because the deliverable
was unambiguous: a plan doc, no code). - **Two read-only workflows + a

(`wf_4012d83a-551`) captured current `file:line` across the ~12 affected
files, correcting the parent plan’s drifted citations (the
`+ sexMean / 2` add is `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R:190`, NOT the
plan’s stale `:175`); plus I read the genetics core
`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` firsthand. (2) a **completeness
grep** added `appServer.R:293,316` (app wiring) + confirmed the two
behavior-bearing call sites are ONLY `reportGV` + `gvaConvergence`. (3)
a **3-agent adversarial VERIFICATION** (`wf_38c97263-a2e`: citation /
design red-team / completeness) – the project’s S213 bar for a planning
deliverable. - **The verification earned its keep – it found 6
substantive issues, all folded in:** (1) **HIGH-severity D10
contradiction** – the draft’s C1 absent-column default said “keep the
prior”, but D10 requires absent-column =\> blanket-A =\> SUPPRESS
(opposite outcomes for any pre-existing override on a one-unknown
animal); fixed as TWO opposite defaults (whole-file-absent =\>
blanket-A; per-row-blank =\> known-side, C1.1). (2) **WRONG directional
claim** – my FIRST workflow’s agent said gvaConvergence computes
parentage BEFORE its correction (`:169`); the LINE was right but `:169`
is AFTER `:157-163` (same as reportGV `:258`); **I confirmed firsthand**
and corrected (both callers symmetric; no asymmetry to exploit). (3) the
side classification needs `isU(ped$sire/dam)`, NOT a `classifyParentage`
hoist (which returns the parentage CLASS, not the side – confirmed
firsthand; `classifyParentage.R:24-26` + it reuses the same `isU`). (4)
cleaner C3 design – the red-team pushed me from “add a NULL-default
param” (a NULL-vs-`character(0L)` opposite-semantics trap) to “caller
computes the suppress set, function stays dumb/unchanged” (no new param,
no ambiguity, no signature change). (5) honest C2 framing – rule (i)
trades the case-(b) over-credit for a smaller SCOPED missing-side one.
(6) THREE missed doc/test surfaces – `genetic_value.html:50-61`,
`summary_stats.html`, and `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R:21-78` (a
doc-consistency test that pins the exact “current limitation” phrasing
and BREAKS when the text is rewritten) – all verified firsthand to
exist. - **Surfaced the gates, did NOT ratify them (FM \#19 – the plan
is a DRAFT):** option C’s two prerequisites are owner/geneticist calls –
**C1** (the irreversible 4th-column schema encoding + C1.1/C1.2/C1.3
sub-decisions) and **C2** (the residual genetics-modeling rule:
full-drop-on-any-missing-side vs partial-residual). The plan RECOMMENDS
for each but the §7 checklist is UNRATIFIED; Phase 0 = ratify via
`/grill-me` against a firsthand numeric check (the way D11 was settled).
No Slice-1 RED until Phase 0 closes.

the DESIGN, not just catches errors (the
caller-computes-the-suppress-set design emerged from the red-team); a
schema/UI change’s blast radius includes phrase-pinning doc-consistency
tests that symbol-greps miss; the plan is a DRAFT – surface genetics +
schema decisions as owner/geneticist gates, recommend but do not ratify
(FM \#19). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],

### What Session 223 Did

**Deliverable:** **Implemented issue \#13 item-3 follow-up R13 – the
Summary Statistics relationship table now FLAGS overridden pairs.** A
new display-layer helper `flagOverriddenRelationships()` appends a
logical `overridden` column to the relationship output (the “Export All
Relationships” CSV + the returned reactive) marking the pairs whose
kinship VALUE came from an outside-information override, so a user can
see which rows carry an override even though the `relation` LABEL stays
pedigree-derived (label and value can disagree). `convertRelationships`
is UNTOUCHED (owner chose display-layer; flag, not relabel). No override
=\> output byte-identical (D10). **DONE.** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-28 / 2026-06-28 **Status:** **DONE.** **Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT
session (RED `7d8998e2` -\> GREEN `9fee8620`; REFACTOR offered, owner
chose skip).** Phase declared each response; **all three phase gates
(PRE-RED-\>RED, RED-\>GREEN, GREEN-\>REFACTOR) via `AskUserQuestion`**,
plus ONE pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` (two questions: flag-vs-relabel
= Flag; blast radius = Display-layer). **0 stakeholder corrections / 0
owner overrides** (every scope/phase decision accepted as written). -

verification before RED):** mapped the relationship display (it is NOT
an on-screen table – the per-pair output reaches the user only through

`convertRelationships` (the `relation` LABEL is pedigree-derived

overridden pairs, OR narrow & document”; S217 chose **narrow +
document**, shipped S219). **Load-bearing finding:** the remaining R13
work is the OTHER fork (flag); and a single kinship coefficient does NOT
identify a unique relationship, so relabel-from-value is genetically
unsound -\> surfaced flag-vs-relabel as the owner’s genetics call (FM
\#13 avoided). - **RED (`7d8998e2`):** NEW
`test_flagOverriddenRelationships.R` (6 blocks: NULL/zero-row return

TRUE on the pair, value==0.25, label==“Other”; no-override -\> 4 cols,

app; (7) **stayed strictly in the one slice** – did not implement D11,

corrections / 0 overrides. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **a JSON typo on
the FIRST `AskUserQuestion` call** (a stray unquoted `description:` key)

sloppy first attempt; (b) Phase-3E confirms the served TEXT + the
integration tests cover the reactive, but no browser visual render (GUI
driver unavailable headlessly) – residual gap, stated; (c) the recon

**Learnings:** **Learning 209** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` – adding
a DISPLAY-LAYER FLAG for a documented divergence as the narrow
alternative to a wider core change: the remaining work after a shipped
“narrow + document” fork is the OTHER fork; surface flag-vs-relabel
(relabel-from-value is genetically unsound) and blast radius
(display-layer vs the shared core) as pre-RED scope decisions; D10 = add
the column only when overrides are supplied; TEST THE REAL APP PATH
(kinshipMatrix=NULL, not the provided-matrix branch that bypasses
overrides); match pairs unordered; fold the doc forward with a new
distinct-word doc-test without disturbing pinned phrases; `@noRd` helper
= no NAMESPACE/man change; pre-existing lints are not yours to fix in a
tight slice. Carried as applied:

\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 205/206 (the
issue \#13 item-3 arc). **This was a strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session.**

### What Session 222 Did

**Deliverable:** **Branch hygiene – deleted the 5 merged issue-13
feature branches (local + remote).** Verified each was fully merged into
BOTH `master` and `origin/master`, then deleted
`issue13-slice1-kinship-overrides`, `issue13-slice2-shiny-upload`,
`issue13-slice3-fallbacks`, `issue13-item3-inapp-docs`,
`issue13-item3-gvaconv-overrides` – 5 local + 5 remote. **DONE.** Owner
picked “All 5 issue-13 branches” at the one pre-execution
`AskUserQuestion`. **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-28 / 2026-06-28
**Status:** **DONE.** **Admin / branch-hygiene session – TDD code-phases
N/A** (no `R/`/tests/`NAMESPACE`/`man/`/`data`/`DESCRIPTION` change –
branch deletions + close-out docs only; phase declared PRE-RED / N/A
each response, like the S218/S221 admin sessions). **0 stakeholder
corrections / 0 owner overrides.** - **Verify-before-delete (firsthand,
SAFEGUARDS “never delete without verifying it’s committed/merged”):**
`git branch --merged master` and `git branch -r --merged origin/master`
confirmed all 5 issue-13 branches merged into BOTH refs. Their commits
all live in `master`’s history, so deletion loses nothing (recoverable
via the merge commits / reflog) – which is what makes it safe. - **Found
the handoff under-counted the set.** S221’s branch-hygiene thread named
only 4 (slices 2/3, inapp-docs, gvaconv-overrides);
`git branch --merged` surfaced a 5th, `issue13-slice1-kinship-overrides`
(merged long ago via PR \#89, never re-listed in recent prose). SURFACED
slice1’s inclusion to the owner rather than silently following the
4-list or silently expanding scope (FM \#13 /
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]); owner chose all 5. - **Confirmed the
outward-facing set before touching origin.** `git push origin --delete`
modifies origin, so the exact set (5 vs 4; local + remote; whether to
also sweep the unrelated stale merged branches) was one pre-execution
`AskUserQuestion`. Left `module`/`rlabkey-version-floor` (stale but
out-of-scope) and `dev`/`gh-pages` (keepers) alone. - **Local deletes:**
safe `git branch -d` deleted 4 cleanly; the 5th
(`issue13-item3-gvaconv-overrides`) REFUSED – “not yet merged to
refs/remotes/origin/… even though it is merged to HEAD” – because `-d`
checks the branch’s UPSTREAM and S221’s `reset --hard` had moved its
local tip to merge commit `daa7728b` (the “ahead 1” quirk, Learning
207). Proved containment with
`git merge-base --is-ancestor daa7728b master` (exit 0) THEN
`git branch -D` – a VERIFIED force-delete, not a blind one. - **Remote
deletes:** `git push origin --delete` removed all 5 remote branches in
one push. **Post-state verified:** `git branch -a | grep issue13` -\>
NONE; local branches now `dev`, `master`, `module`,
`rlabkey-version-floor`; `master` unchanged at `92cd34bd`, in sync with
`origin/master`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** This session
authored no `R/`/runtime code – it deleted git branches, which changes
no application behavior. **Build-equivalent (`devtools::check`) not
triggered:** nothing in
`R/`/tests/`NAMESPACE`/`man/`/`data`/`DESCRIPTION` changed (only
markdown close-out docs), same logic as the S214/S218/S221 docs/admin
sessions.

**Session 221 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 222): Score 9/10.** S221’s
handoff set this hygiene session up very well. **What helped:** (1)
**the “Branch hygiene (owner’s call)” live thread pre-warned the exact
`-d` trap** – “note the LOCAL feature branch was inadvertently moved to
the merge commit by this session’s `reset --hard` – it is still fully
merged into master, so deleting it is safe” – which is EXACTLY the `-d`
refusal I hit on `issue13-item3-gvaconv-overrides`; the handoff told me
in advance why `-d` would balk and that the force-delete was safe
(extremely high ROI); (2) it named the earlier slice branches as
deletable, seeding the set; (3) the **clean-state anchor held** (HEAD
`92cd34bd` == documented S221; tree clean except the standing untracked
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`); (4) standing keeps held firsthand
(`gh issue` Projects-classic deprecation -\> `--json`; `fetch`+`reset`
not `pull`; `lint` red non-blocking). **The -1 (minor):** the
branch-hygiene list named only 4 branches and OMITTED

**Self-assessment (Session 222): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read IN FULL; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check clean – HEAD == documented S221),
reported, STOPPED for the owner; wrote the **1B stub BEFORE technical
work**. **Strengths:** (1) **verify-before-delete on BOTH refs
firsthand** – confirmed all 5 merged into `master` AND `origin/master`
before any deletion, with the “commits live in master -\> nothing lost”
reasoning explicit (SAFEGUARDS applied to branches); (2) **caught the
handoff’s under-counted set** (`git branch --merged` showed slice1 the
prose omitted) and surfaced it to the owner rather than silently
following or silently expanding (FM \#13 /
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]); (3) **handled the `-d` refusal
correctly** – did NOT blindly `-D` on the first balk; proved containment
with `git merge-base --is-ancestor daa7728b master` first, then
force-deleted (a verified force-delete); (4) **confirmed the
outward-facing set via `AskUserQuestion` before touching origin**
(remote deletion modifies origin); (5) **stayed strictly in scope** –
left the unrelated stale merged branches (`module`,
`rlabkey-version-floor`) and the origin-only relics alone, did not touch
the item-3 follow-ups / refactor / \#37 / 2.0.0; (6) **verified the
final state** (no issue-13 branches remain local or remote; `master`
unchanged). **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **minor batching inelegance** –
I ran `git branch -d` on all 5 at once even though the handoff had
warned `gvaconv-overrides` was the moved-to-merge-commit one likely to
balk; I could have separated it from the start. Low/zero harm (`-d` is
non-destructive and the refusal is informative – it confirmed the 4
trivially and flagged the 1 I then verified), arguably even good
practice (let the safe tool gate), but a tidier plan would have
force-handled it separately; (b) no runtime/build verification –
correctly N/A (no `R/` change), stated not skipped. Capped at 9 only by
(a), a cosmetic point.

**Learnings:** **Learning 208** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` –
branch-hygiene deletion of merged feature branches as careful
outward-facing admin: verify each branch is merged into BOTH `master`
and `origin/master` firsthand (deletion loses nothing -\> that is what
makes it safe); `git branch -d` compares against the branch’s UPSTREAM
not only HEAD, so a branch whose local tip is a merge commit ahead of
its remote feature tip refuses `-d` even when merged to master – prove
containment with `git merge-base --is-ancestor <tip> master` THEN `-D`
(verified force-delete, not blind); enumerate the deletion set from
`git branch --merged`, not the handoff prose (it under-counted by one);
and confirm the outward-facing set via `AskUserQuestion` before
`git push origin --delete`. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]; extends Learning 207 (the “ahead
1” quirk this deletion had to handle). **This was an
admin/branch-hygiene session – TDD code-phases N/A.**

2.0.0 question. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **the git-sync ordering
slip – the main ding:** I chained `git checkout master` +
`git reset --hard origin/master` in one command while my own 1B stub was
uncommitted; the `checkout` aborted and the `reset --hard` reset the
FEATURE branch to the merge commit (discarding the stub, moving the
wrong pointer). Low harm (the stub is always superseded by this handoff;
untracked files untouched), recovered cleanly with `switch master` +
`merge --ff-only`, and memorialized (Learning 207), but it was a
preventable git-state fumble – the tree should have been clean before
the sync; (b) no independent build-equivalent run – defensible (doc-only
changes; merged R/ code covered by the green CI matrix), stated not
skipped. Capped at 8 by (a).

**Learnings:** **Learning 207** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` –
completing the push-\>PR-\>CI-\>merge arc for an already-finished slice
as careful admin: verify-then-merge (full matrix green + lone red
surfaced) rather than merge-on-`MERGEABLE`; when `gh run view --log`
returns empty for a failed job, get per-file findings from the check-run
ANNOTATIONS API to prove the lint red is pre-existing and not from the
PR’s changed files; syncing local master with an uncommitted 1B stub is
a git-state trap (`checkout` aborts -\> chained `reset --hard` hits the
wrong branch) so clean the tree FIRST then `switch` + `merge --ff-only`
and verify the branch + sync; and PR-body hygiene for a CLOSED tracking
issue (Relates-to, grep the rendered body, verify state before/after).
Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\]; extends Learning
204 (careful-admin merge) and closes out the S220 slice (Learning 206).
**This was an admin/merge session – TDD code-phases N/A.**

**Gotchas:** (1) **PR \#93 is MERGED to `master`** (merge commit

hygiene); the LOCAL feature branch was moved to the merge commit by this
session’s `reset --hard` (still merged -\> safe to delete). (2) **When
`gh run view <run_id> --log`/`--log-failed` returns EMPTY for a failed
job, use the check-run ANNOTATIONS API**
(`gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/check-runs/<JOB_ID>/annotations`) to get the
per-file findings – that is how I proved the `lint` red is pre-existing
whole-package noise, not from this PR. (3) **Sync local master in the
RIGHT order:** with an uncommitted 1B stub in the tree,
`git checkout master` ABORTS and a chained
`git reset --hard origin/master` resets the CURRENT (feature) branch
instead – clean the tree FIRST (the stub is disposable), THEN
`git switch master` + `git merge --ff-only origin/master`, and verify
`rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` + local-master == origin/master. (4) **The
CI `lint` check is long-standing red and non-blocking** (whole-package
noise in `data-raw/fgSEValidation.R` + a Node.js-20 workflow-deprecation
warning; `mergeStateStatus` UNSTABLE not BLOCKED); your EDITS must still
be lint-clean (\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]). (5) Carried standing
keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `gh issue view <n>` errors on a
Projects-classic deprecation -\> use `--json`; build-equivalent is
`devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)`=0/0/0 (NOT run this session – no
`R/`/test change); a 0/0/0 check does NOT imply spelling-clean -\>
`spell_check_package`; `NEWS.md` is GENERATED from `NEWS.Rmd`;
module/E2E tests need `NOT_CRAN=true`; `git pull` is rebase + chokes on
`.DS_Store` -\> use `fetch`+`reset`.

### What Session 220 Did

**Deliverable:** **Implemented issue \#13 item-3 follow-up –
[`gvaConvergence()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/gvaConvergence.md)
now honors outside-information kinship overrides** (the most
self-contained item-3 slice). Added a `kinshipOverrides = NULL`
argument; it applies the override to the proband kinship matrix via the
existing `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix()` helper AND threads the
surviving overridden ids into `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship()` so the
convergence diagnostic ranks on the SAME mean kinship
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
uses (owner chose “Match reportGV” fidelity). Also corrected the two
now-false S219 in-app doc statements (`modGeneticValue.R` helpText +
`genetic_value.html`) that said the convergence check ignores overrides,
and the matching `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` assertions. **DONE.**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-27 / 2026-06-28 **Status:** **DONE.**
**Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT session (RED `a9e89027` -\> GREEN `87eae950`;
REFACTOR offered – cross-module de-dup – owner chose skip).** Phase
declared each response; **all three phase gates (PRE-RED-\>RED,
RED-\>GREEN, GREEN-\>REFACTOR) via `AskUserQuestion`**, plus TWO pre-RED
scope `AskUserQuestion`s (slice pick = `gvaConvergence`; fidelity =
Match reportGV + update S219 docs now). **1 owner mid-session QUALITY
correction (lint standards – see weakness (a); addressed +
memorialized); 0 technical/scope corrections; 0 owner overrides** (every
fidelity/docs/phase decision accepted as written). - **Recon (firsthand,
before RED):** mapped `gvaConvergence`’s ranking pipeline (`kmat` at
`:126` -\> `meanKinship` -\> `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` -\>
`zScores` -\> `buildOrder`/half-split metrics); read
`applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix` (returns matrix only, warn-drops absent
ids = D5), `reportGV`’s override block (`:140-180`, computes
`overriddenIds` + threads into the \#9 correction), and
`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`’s `overriddenIds` branch (`:164-170`,
skips `+sexMean/2` for overridden one-unknown animals = D11
supersession). **Two load-bearing findings:** (1) `gvaConvergence` is a
**script-level exported diagnostic the Shiny app never calls** (only
call-sites: its docstring, tests, vignettes) -\> not a Shiny-wiring
change; (2) it calls the SAME \#9 correction as `reportGV`, so
matrix-only application would make a one-unknown overridden animal’s
rank DIVERGE from `reportGV` -\> surfaced “matrix-only vs
match-reportGV” as a pre-RED owner decision (FM \#13 literal-minimum
avoided). - **RED (`a9e89027`):**
`test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R` (5 blocks): (1) NULL/zero-row =
byte-identical to no-override (D10); (2) a non-trivial override changes
the convergence curve deterministically (overrides reach the ranking);
(3) out-of-set id warn-dropped without aborting (D5); (4)
above-PSD-bound error propagates; (5) override on a one-unknown animal
runs clean + is honored (overriddenIds/#9 path). **All 5 failed for the
right reason** (4x `unused argument`, 1x wrong-error-message); existing
`gvaConvergence` stayed green (35/35). **Prototyped the override on the
gu-bearing fixture FIRST** (scratchpad copy of the body) to pick a
perturbation (5 offspring pairs @ 0.49) GUARANTEED to move the curve –
because on a no-gu pedigree (qcPed) the metrics are always 1/1 and the
override is output-invisible. - **GREEN (`87eae950`, minimal):**
`kinshipOverrides = NULL` param + `@param` mirroring `reportGV`; after
the `:126` kmat build, compute `overriddenIds` (in-matrix survivors,
read from the override frame the same way the helper does its
intersect) + `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix(kmat, kinshipOverrides)`;
thread `overriddenIds` into the correction. **No NAMESPACE change**
(already exported; helper is `@noRd`). Corrected the 2 false S219 doc
statements + the 2 `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` assertions that pinned
them. `man/gvaConvergence.Rd` + **NEWS** (`.Rmd` + render; removed the
now-false “ignores overrides” clause from the S219 NEWS bullet + added
the item-3 bullet). - **Verify (all clean):** new tests **14/14**;
existing `gvaConvergence` **35/35**; `test_kinshipOverrideDocs`
**15/15**; `reportGV` **101/101**; `modGeneticValue` **164/164**; full
clean regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (3132 pass / 167 skip,
incl. & excl. baseline `test-app-`/`test-e2e-`, `NOT_CRAN=true`);
`devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` **0/0/0**; `spell_check_package`
**0** (reworded singular “proband” -\> “analysis set”/dropped, matching
`reportGV`’s `@param`; did NOT pad WORDLIST); **lint 0 on both changed R
files** (package loaded). - **Lint feedback (owner, mid-session):**
“correct your coding/comment standards to avoid creating new lints.” I
had introduced **2 line-length(80) violations** in `gvaConvergence.R`
(the `@param` text; a former-last-arg line pushed to 81 by my added
trailing comma) – fixed both by re-wrapping. Established firsthand that
`object_usage_linter` warnings go to **0** when the package is loaded
(artifacts of an absent namespace) and `object_name` camelCase warnings
appear only when linting OUTSIDE the project dir (the project `.lintr`
allows `snake_case`/`CamelCase`/`camelCase`; `tests`/`vignettes`/`inst`
excluded). Saved as durable memory \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\].

an existing feature into a SIBLING consumer that shares a pipeline
stage: read the sibling’s FULL handling firsthand (the shared
`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` \#9 branch made matrix-only
application a silent-divergence trap -\> “Match reportGV” was a
correctness call, not a style one); recognize when implementing X
FALSIFIES prior-session docs + the doc-tests pinning them, and fold the
correction in; for a diagnostic whose output is INSENSITIVE to the
change on the easy fixture (no-gu -\> metrics 1/1), prototype the
perturbation on the hard fixture BEFORE freezing RED and scope the
un-observable assertion (the \#9 numeric) as construction-parity; and
**lint changed R files BEFORE the owner has to ask** – with the package
loaded so `object_usage`/`object_name` artifacts don’t mask the real
line-length lints. New memory \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]. Carried
as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],

S219 documented as limits).

**Gotchas:** (1) **`gvaConvergence` is NOT wired into the Shiny app** –
it is a script-level exported diagnostic (only call-sites:
docstring/tests/vignettes). Adding override support does NOT change app
behavior; the app’s GV tab never runs it. (2) **The override is
output-INVISIBLE on a no-gu pedigree** (qcPed): with no rankable
genome-uniqueness, both half-splits produce the same order -\> metrics
always 1/1, regardless of mean kinship. So any “override changed the
result” test MUST use the gu-bearing convergence fixture; qcPed only
proves the override path runs clean. (3) **The \#9 (`overriddenIds`)
supersession numeric is not directly observable through
`gvaConvergence`’s public return** (no per-animal mean kinship) – it is
covered by construction-parity with `reportGV` + `test_reportGV.R`’s
D10/D5/D11 tests. (4) **Lint: keep R lines \<=80 (incl. roxygen); lint
changed files with the package LOADED** – `object_usage`/`object_name`
warnings are artifacts (0 when loaded / when run in-project); a
former-last-arg line + a new trailing comma is a classic 80-\>81 trap
(\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\]). (5) **`runModularApp` sets its OWN
port (observed 6013) and ignores `options(shiny.port=)`** – read the
actual port from the “Listening on 127.0.0.1:PORT” log line for any
Phase-3E curl (carried S219). (6) Carried standing keeps (unchanged):
package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;

FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `gh issue view <n>` errors on a

`NEWS.Rmd`; module/E2E tests need `NOT_CRAN=true`; `git pull` is

**Audited the in-app documentation coverage FIRST (false-negative
discipline):** enumerated every in-app doc surface — inline
`helpText()`, the per-tab `includeHTML()` guidance assets
(`inst/extdata/ui_guidance/*.html`), the About/Documentation tab

(gvaConvergence ignores overrides; relationship label-vs-value
divergence; unknown-parent \#9 edge cases) were documented only
DEVELOPER-facing (roxygen `@param` + code comments in
`modSummaryStats.R`) and PACKAGE-level (NEWS, planning doc) — **ZERO
in-app coverage**; the override-upload helpText covered file FORMAT
only. Verified firsthand by reading the two HTML files + the UI
function. - **RED (`ed05a0cd`):** `test_kinshipOverrideDocs.R` (14
assertions across the three surfaces). **12 failed (documentation
absent) / 0 errors** — confirmed it fails for the right reason. Chose
helpText-EXCLUSIVE discriminators because
`as.character(modGeneticValueUI("gv"))` EMBEDS `genetic_value.html`
(which already names `gvaConvergence`, so asserting it would FALSE-PASS
at RED); verified the asserted phrases absent from the current 9.3KB
rendered UI before freezing. - **GREEN (`3a506772`, minimal):** a second
`helpText` at the override upload (each caveat built with a single
[`paste()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/paste.html) so it renders as ONE
contiguous text node — `helpText()` renders each argument as a separate,
newline-separated node) + an override `<p>` in each of the two guidance
HTML files. **No `man/`/`NAMESPACE` change** (function body + static
assets only; `strong`/`helpText` already used in the function). **NEWS**
(`.Rmd` + render) notes the in-app documentation. - **Verify (all
clean):** doc tests 14/14; full clean regression read **0 failed / 0
error** (3289 assertions, incl. & excl. baseline
`test-app-`/`test-e2e-`; `NOT_CRAN=true`);
`devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` **0/0/0** (Status: OK);
`spell_check_package` **0**.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): DONE (REQUIRED — UI-text change, FM \#24).**
Launched the real
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)

new caveat strings** (the supply-point helpText phrases AND the
guidance-panel “Kinship overrides” headers). **Gotcha learned:**
`runModularApp` set its OWN port (6013) and IGNORED
`options(shiny.port=6099L)`, so the first curl hit a refused port — read
the actual port from the “Listening on” log line. Residual gap (stated,
not skipped): a true browser render/visual confirmation needs a
GUI/Chrome driver unavailable headlessly; the served-HTML grep + the
rendered-UI unit tests cover the text presence.

**Learnings:** **Learning 205** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` —
documenting a shipped feature’s behavior + LIMITS in the in-app UI is a
test-backable deliverable: audit every in-app surface to avoid a false
“undocumented” negative; put the docs where the input is SUPPLIED (the
helpText, not just guidance HTML); assert the doc TEXT in the rendered
UI + static assets (each asserted phrase in ONE text node / one line
because `helpText()` newline-separates args — build with a single
[`paste()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/paste.html)); pick RED discriminators
absent from the WHOLE rendered output (the UI embeds the included HTML);
Phase-3E the real app and read its ACTUAL port from the log; and the
session-boundary meta-lesson (a “session” is a logical unit, but don’t
claim/gate an implementation while still in Phase-0/1 discussion).

**Gotchas:** (1) **Branch `issue13-item3-inapp-docs` is MERGED to

closing keyword, so \#13 stayed CLOSED/unaffected). The merged branch
(local + remote) can be deleted (owner hygiene). (2)
**`helpText(a, b, c)` renders each argument as a SEPARATE,
newline-separated text node** — an asserted/contiguous phrase must live
in ONE string argument or a single `paste(...)` (joins with spaces
BEFORE becoming one node), else `grepl(fixed=TRUE)` misses it across the
newline. The HTML assets have the same constraint (keep a phrase on one
line). (3) **`runModularApp` sets its OWN port (observed 6013) and
ignores `options(shiny.port=)`** — read the actual port from the
“Listening on 127.0.0.1:PORT” log line for any Phase-3E curl. (4)
**`as.character(modGeneticValueUI("gv"))` EMBEDS `genetic_value.html`**
via `includeHTML`, so the GV-tab rendered output already contains that
file’s text (e.g. `gvaConvergence`) — pick UI-test discriminators that
are helpText-EXCLUSIVE. (5) **The three item-3 follow-ups are
DOCUMENTED, not implemented** — the NEWS/UI caveats describe existing
behavior + known limits, not a behavior change. (6) Carried standing
keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `gh issue view <n>` errors on a
Projects-classic deprecation → use `--json`; build-equivalent is
`devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)`=0/0/0; a 0/0/0 check does NOT imply
spelling-clean → `spell_check_package`; `NEWS.md` is GENERATED from
`NEWS.Rmd`; module/E2E tests need `NOT_CRAN=true`; `git pull` is
rebase + chokes on `.DS_Store` → use `fetch`+`reset`.

### What Session 218 Did

**Deliverable:** **Completed the issue \#13 merge arc** (outward-facing
admin) — merged **PR \#90** (Slice 2) and **PR \#91** (Slice 3) into
`master`, so all 3 slices of issue \#13 are now on `master` and **\#13
is CLOSED** (via PR \#91, with Slice 3 actually merged). **DONE.** Owner
picked “merge PR \#90” at Phase 1, then “Reopen, then merge PR \#91”
when the \#90 merge accidentally closed \#13 early. **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-27 / 2026-06-27 **Status:** **DONE.** **Admin /
merge session — TDD code-phases N/A** (no `R/`, tests, `NAMESPACE`,
`man/`, `data/`, `DESCRIPTION` change — two PR merges + issue-state
admin + close-out docs only; phase declared N/A / PRE-RED each response,
like the S214 docs-only session). **0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner
overrides.** - **Merged PR \#90 (Slice 2) carefully, not blindly**
(Learning 202 §0 practice): confirmed PR \#90 `MERGEABLE` / `UNSTABLE`
with the **full R-CMD-check matrix green** (macOS, Windows, Ubuntu
devel/oldrel-1/release; + `pkgdown`/`test-coverage`/`codecov/project`)
and surfaced that the only reds are the known non-blocking `lint` +
`codecov/patch` (UNSTABLE ⇒ non-required) BEFORE merging. Used a **merge
commit** (`9bf615e9`, matching the PR \#89 precedent `0438c2d5`) and
**kept the base branch** to protect the stacked PR \#91. - **Caught +
handled an accidental issue close:** merging PR \#90 **auto-closed
\#13**, because \#90’s body contains the literal substring `closes #13`
(inside the descriptive sentence “Slice 3 closes \#13”) — GitHub reads
any `closes/fixes/resolves #N` in a PR body as a closing keyword and
fires it on merge to the default branch. Slice 3 was not yet merged, so
\#13 read “done” prematurely. **Surfaced it as an owner
`AskUserQuestion`** (reopen-and-pause / reopen-then-merge-#91 /
leave-closed) rather than silently reopening — issue lifecycle is the
owner’s call (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], FM \#23). Owner chose
“Reopen, then merge PR \#91.” - **Reopened \#13, then merged PR \#91
(Slice 3):** reopened \#13 **first** (so \#91’s `closes #13` fires on an
OPEN issue → proper PR-linked close). Merging \#90 had moved \#91’s base
and **re-triggered its CI**; two matrix jobs
(`windows-latest (release)`, `ubuntu-latest (devel)`) went `pending`.
**Held the same green-matrix standard** — waited (~5 min, background
poll) for the full matrix to go **green** rather than merge over a
pending correctness signal (UNSTABLE/non-required would have allowed an
immediate merge). Merged via merge commit (`7cabd8e1`). **\#13 is now
CLOSED** with Slice 3 on master. - **Synced local `master`** via
`fetch` + `reset --hard origin/master` (not `pull`, per standing note);
verified the merge commits (`9bf615e9`, `7cabd8e1`) and Slice 3’s
`R/applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix.R` are present on master; working tree
clean except the standing untracked `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A for the session’s own work (stated, not
skipped).** This session authored no `R/`/runtime code — it merged
already-tested, CI-green code. The merged code’s runtime was verified by

[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md) +

instantiating the modules with the override arg + served `fileInput`)
and by **PR \#91’s green post-merge R-CMD-check matrix** (which ran on
`master` + Slice 3 — a fuller verification than a local check).
**Build-equivalent (`devtools::check`) not run:** nothing in
`R/`/tests/`NAMESPACE`/`data` changed this session (only markdown —

says ‘Slice 3 closes \#13’ — GitHub will treat that as a closing keyword
on merge; reword or expect to reopen” would have prevented the
close→reopen→close churn. (Subtle GitHub-parsing gotcha, easy to miss;
the offending text was S216’s PR-body wording, not S217’s.) ROI: very
high.

**Self-assessment (Session 218): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +

**Learnings:** **Learning 204** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` —
completing a stacked-PR merge arc as careful outward-facing admin:
verify each PR’s FULL R-CMD-check matrix green (not just `mergeable`)
before merging; for a STACK, merge the base PR with a MERGE COMMIT (not
squash) + keep the base branch so GitHub narrows the dependent PR’s diff
and recognizes the base commits as merged — and note that merging the
base MOVES the dependent PR’s base and RE-TRIGGERS its CI (re-verify the
dependent’s matrix went green on the new base before merging it); a
closing keyword (`closes/fixes/resolves #N`) ANYWHERE in a PR body —
even inside a descriptive sentence — fires on merge to the default
branch, so AUDIT PR bodies for stray closing keywords before merging,
and to restore an accidentally-closed tracking issue whose final slice
isn’t merged yet, reopen it BEFORE merging the final PR (whose
`closes #N` re-closes it with the work actually on master). Carried as
applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 202 §0 (the
careful-admin merge practice). **This was an admin/merge session — TDD
code-phases N/A.**

\#37 disposition** (carried S212-S217): close, or refresh body to

**Gotchas:** (1) **A closing keyword (`closes/fixes/resolves #N`)
ANYWHERE in a PR body auto-closes \#N on merge to the default branch** —
even inside a descriptive sentence like “Slice 3 closes \#13.” This
closed \#13 when PR \#90 merged (Slice 3 not yet on master). Audit PR
bodies before merging; reopen + merge-the-final-PR to restore. (2) **For
a stacked PR: merge the base with a MERGE COMMIT (not squash) and keep
the base branch** — squash would orphan the dependent PR’s base commits
and force a rebase; merging the base also RE-TRIGGERS the dependent PR’s
CI (re-verify its matrix green before merging it). (3) **Both issue-13

FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `gh issue view <n>` errors on a

**Learnings:** **Learning 203** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` — wiring
a feature into a DOWNSTREAM consumer’s FALLBACK path (upstream delivery
is a Shiny upload) is a plumbing-mapping problem first: read the actual
reactive return lists + each consumer’s prefer-vs-fallback branch
firsthand (the upstream module usually does NOT expose the input;
transitive vs always-recompute consumers need different reasoning),
apply overrides only where the value is missing, assert the
DETERMINISTIC input of a stochastic consumer, demonstrate a
label-vs-value “narrow” with a pedigree-RELATED pair, DRY the soft
wrapper into one helper but don’t refactor the working upstream that
needs extra return data, and reword the spell-flagged NOUN to the
accepted VERB. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199/200/201/202.

threading. (6) **testServer exposes a module server’s internal
functions/reactives by name** in the test expr
(e.g. `getKinshipMatrix(...)`, `relationshipData()`) — the basis for the
deterministic assertions. (7) Carried standing keeps (unchanged):

FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `gh issue view <n>` errors on a

`fetch`+`reset`.

**Self-assessment (Session 216): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +

carefully** — confirmed the R-CMD-check matrix green and surfaced that
lint-red is non-blocking informational BEFORE merging, rather than
blindly merging-over-red or blindly refusing; (2) **caught the
`req()`-aborts-the-parent pitfall** and used an explicit
[`is.null()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/NULL.html) guard so the no-upload
path can’t abort `gvResults()` (D10); (3) **caught + fixed the
test-double regression** by running the FULL clean regression read, not
just the new files (the breakage was invisible in isolation); (4)
**chose a deterministic fixture** (both-unknown founder override → mean
kinship moves off the matrix, no gene-drop noise, no \#9 interaction) so
the assertion is stable; (5) **did a genuine headless Phase-3E** (real
app launch + served-fileInput grep) and **stated the browser-click gap
explicitly**; (6) **verified the recon’s load-bearing claims firsthand**
before designing RED (FM \#11/#20); (7) **stayed strictly in Slice 2** —
did NOT touch the fallback recompute paths (Slice 3; FM \#18/#25),
surfaced R9 in NEWS instead. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) the module’s
soft `kinshipOverrideData` can’t be a pure helper (`showNotification`
needs session context) so it stays inline — a slight density, offered as
REFACTOR, owner skipped; (b) the browser file-picker click-through
genuinely can’t run headlessly — mitigated by the testServer integration
test + startup smoke, but a residual gap; (c) the recon workflow used
~195k subagent tokens — ultracode-appropriate and it paid off (precise
wiring map), but a leaner read could have reached the same RED design.
Capped at 9 by (a)/(b).

**Learnings:** **Learning 202** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` — wiring
a script-level feature into the Shiny app under strict TDD: budget the
firsthand work for the three wiring-specific failure modes — (1) a
soft/optional input with `req()` ABORTS its mandatory consumer (use
[`is.null()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/NULL.html) → NULL); (2) extending a
real function’s signature SILENTLY breaks the test doubles that mock it
(only the FULL regression read catches it; update every mock to mirror
the real signature); (3) honest headless Phase-3E (launch the real app +
grep the served HTML for the namespaced input id; lean on a `testServer`
integration test; state the browser-click gap) — plus pick a
deterministic fixture for a gene-drop-noisy module, and do
owner-directed outward-facing admin (merge) carefully not blindly.
Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; extends Learning 199/200/201.

reactive ABORTS the caller** — `kinshipOverrideData` deliberately uses
`if (is.null(input$kinshipOverrideFile)) return(NULL)`, not `req()`, so
the no-upload GV run is not halted. Preserve this if you refactor. (4)
**A function-signature change breaks hand-copied test mocks** — any
future param added to `reportGV` must also be added to the
`local_mocked_bindings(reportGV = function(...))` doubles in
`test_modGeneticValue.R` (currently 2); the FULL regression read is what
catches it. (5) **`testServer` simulates a `fileInput`** by
`session$setInputs(kinshipOverrideFile = data.frame(name=, size=, type=, datapath=<real temp csv>))`.

2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;

the \#13-closed state as a decision, didn’t silently reopen.

validation (D6):** `checkKinshipOverrides` (no matrix) WARNS on off-diag
`> 0.5`; `applyKinshipOverrides` (matrix in hand) REJECTS
`> sqrt(diag_ii·diag_jj)` (exact bound — legit inbred pairs whose bound
exceeds 0.5 are not falsely blocked). The plan §4 RED bullet’s “rejects
\> 0.5” was loose; D6/§7 (warn) is authoritative and is what shipped.

All accepted as recommended. - **D11 (the \#1 dragon, a genetics call)
settled via `/grill-me`, grounded in a 4-agent firsthand workflow FIRST
(`wf_a3c184ee-92b`: mechanics + issue-9 S177 rationale + an EXECUTED
numeric model on real `qcPed` + synthesis).** The analysis
**quantified** the stacking (the `+sexMean/2` term ≈ **1 SD**, ~8.4× the
override’s own effect, flips a worked animal **GV rank \#6 → \#179**)
and **corrected** a load-bearing S213-draft mechanics claim: the
spurious `+sexMean/2` is written to `corrected`, never the
`original`/`sexMean` peers read, so it **cannot cascade** (the draft
said it “propagates into other animals’ corrections” – FALSE). **Grilled
the decision tree one question at a time** (fix-vs-accept →
supersede-vs-independent crux, offering the premise-REVERSING “keep the
prior” option → cohort sub-decision → v1 scope edges). **Ratified:
blanket supersession (A)** – skip `+sexMean/2` for any overridden
one-unknown animal; **keep** them as cohort peers (no cascade);
**document** the both-unknown-promotion + shared-sib-pair edges as v1
limits; **track targeted option C** as a follow-up; **pin with a 4-part
regression test.** - **Doc edits (DRAFT → RATIFIED):** status banner +
ratification-record paragraph; §1 stale forward-ref fixed; §3 header +
per-`Dn` `→ RATIFIED (S214)` tags; **D11 fully rewritten** (corrected
mechanics + magnitude + grilled decision + the 4-part test spec);
**Slice-1 scope/RED sharpened** from “the ratified alternative” to the
SPECIFIC blanket-A behavior (no executor interpretation gap); §6 R11 +
the §7 checklist (all 12 boxes `[x]`). Verified firsthand: 0 stale
`*Ratify`/`DRAFT` markers remain.

a planning doc – no runtime/wiring/`R/` change. Build-equivalent
(`devtools::check`) not run: nothing in `R/`/tests/`NAMESPACE`/`data`

**Self-assessment (Session 214): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read IN FULL; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues;

**Learnings:** **Learning 200** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` –

and offer the premise-reversing option; record every answer back into
the doc precisely enough that Slice-1 RED needs no interpretation.

standalone validator `checkKinshipOverrides` (no matrix) WARNS on
off-diagonal `> 0.5`; `applyKinshipOverrides` (matrix in hand) REJECTS
`> sqrt(diag_ii·diag_jj)` (exact bound – legitimate inbred pairs whose
bound exceeds 0.5 are not falsely blocked). (4)
**[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
is the blast-radius boundary** – 6 callers; apply overrides via the

**Gotchas:** (1) **The \#13 plan is a DRAFT, not ratified.** Only D3
(pair-level REPLACE) is decided. D1-D2, D4-D11 carry recommendations the
owner must ratify before Slice-1 RED. **D11 (the \#9-correction stacking
interaction) is the \#1 dragon** – a genetics call; the override applied
before `meanKinship` (`R/reportGV.R:124`) feeds the issue-#9
`+sexMean/2` correction, so an override on a one-unknown-parent animal
STACKS rather than replaces unless the correction is made
override-aware. (2)
**[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
is the blast-radius boundary** – 6 callers (`reportGV:118`,
`modBreedingGroups:173`, `modSummaryStats:357`, `gvaConvergence:126`,
`createSimKinships:58`, `cumulateSimKinships:61`); apply overrides via a
separate leaf at the consumer level, NEVER inside
[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md).
The two simulations must NOT take current-kinship overrides. (3)
**`reportGV`’s `kmat` is `filterKinMatrix(probands, ...)`** – only the
population subset; a strict
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)-on-unknown-id leaf would
abort the run for the canonical sire-dam override (ancestors aren’t
probands), so `reportGV` must warn-drop non-member ids before the leaf
(D5). (4) **`kinship` is the coefficient `f`, NOT relatedness `r`
(=2f)** – off-diagonal `<= 0.5` for non-inbred pairs; the validator must
reject/warn `> 0.5` and the docs/CSV template must say `f`-not-`r` (D6).
(5) Carried standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)

### What Session 211 Did

**Deliverable:** **Disposed of issue \#88** (“the GVA article’s Quarto
`_freeze` is stale -\> the published article omits `fgSE`”). **The issue
premise was FALSE for the published site** – the live article already
shows `fgSE`. Owner pick at the disposition gate: **“Verify, document,
close.”** **DONE** – verified the live site + CI build model, cleared
the stale LOCAL gitignored freeze (commits nothing), audited siblings
(none stale), **closed issue \#88** with the full explanation,
documented. **No production code, no tests, no tracked source/data files
changed** – close-out docs only. **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-26 /
2026-06-27 **Status:** **DONE.** **Verification + documentation session
– TDD code-phases N/A** (no production code; phase declared N/A each
response, like the S209 publish session). **0 stakeholder corrections.**
Owner directed “work on issue \#88” at Phase 1; I surfaced the
premise-is-wrong finding and posed ONE disposition `AskUserQuestion` -\>
owner chose “Verify, document, close.” - **The finding (load-bearing) –
published site was NEVER stale; three independent confirmations:** (1)
the LIVE page renders all three values
`fe 109.67 / fg 47.62 / fgSE 0.29`
(`https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/articles/genetic-value-analysis.html`);
(2) `pkgdown.yaml` does a clean `checkout@v4` with **NO freeze
restore/cache step** and installs the current source via `local::.`, so
CI **re-executes every article fresh** (the freeze lives under
gitignored `.quarto/`, `.gitignore:44`, and never reaches CI); (3) the
pkgdown CI run on the S210 merge **succeeded AFTER `fgSE` shipped** (run
28264621260, 2026-06-26 20:52). - **Root cause of the stale LOCAL freeze
(zero repo/published impact):** the article loads
[`library(nprcgenekeepr)`](https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/)
(the INSTALLED package); the local renv library held a stale 2.0.0
predating `fgSE`
([`renv::status`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/status.html)
out-of-sync – `calcFGSE` not exported, `reportGV` body has no `fgSE`),
so `gv$fgSE` was NULL at local freeze time. CI’s `local::.` install is
why publication stayed correct. - **Action:** cleared the stale local
GVA freeze
(`rm -rf vignettes/articles/.quarto/_freeze/genetic-value-analysis`;
gitignored, `git status` shows no change – commits nothing).
Regenerating in place would REPRODUCE the stale output (installed pkg
still lacks `fgSE`), so clearing + letting CI / a post-`install()` build
regenerate is the correct refresh. **Sibling audit:** all 6 articles
publish (HTTP 200); GVA was the only one with a live `fgSE`-dependent
value (correct); `fg-se-validation` uses static recorded numbers
(`data-raw/fgSEValidation-results.rds`), unaffected. No sibling stale. -
**Closed issue \#88** (`gh issue close 88 --comment ...`) as
verified-correct.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** No
runtime/wiring change – closed an issue + cleared a gitignored cache +
docs. The “runtime” equivalent (the published article render) was
verified DIRECTLY against the live site (the `fe/fg/fgSE` curl above).

**Session 210 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 211): Score 8/10.** S210’s
handoff for its OWN deliverable (the bundled GV report regen) was
excellent and every anchor about it held; **what helped THIS session**
was gotcha \#4 – “the GVA article computes `fg`/`fgSE` LIVE on
`examplePedigree`, not the bundled reports” – which correctly pointed me
at the live-render mechanism fast, and the explicit “issue \#88 filed
for the stale GVA `_freeze`” pointer in SUGGESTED NEXT. **The -2:** the
issue \#88 S210 filed asserted “**the published article is therefore
inconsistent with its own source**” – an **unverified inference from the
local freeze JSON** that turned out FALSE: S210 never checked the live
page, and didn’t reason that the freeze is gitignored + CI re-executes
fresh via `local::.`, so the published site was correct all along. A
spot-check (`curl` the live article) would have caught it, or the issue
could have hedged (“confirm whether the published site is actually
affected – CI may re-execute fresh”). ROI still net-positive: the
pointer got me to the right area, and the wrong-severity framing was
cheap to correct. (This is the same class as S210’s own -1 against S209
– an inference outrunning the evidence; see Learning 197.)

**Self-assessment (Session 211): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read IN FULL; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check – noted the S210 publish/merge happened
after S210’s handoff was written, so the ACTIVE TASK was
stale-but-not-a-ghost), reported, STOPPED for the owner; wrote the **1B
stub BEFORE technical work**; declared the phase each response; posed
the disposition as an `AskUserQuestion` rather than silently closing or
blindly re-rendering. **Strengths:** (1) **verified empirically instead
of executing the issue’s literal ask** – the live page + CI model + CI
run history together proved the premise false, turning a “re-render”
task into a correct close; (2) **diagnosed the full causal chain**
(installed-pkg-stale locally vs `local::.`-fresh in CI) so the close
explains WHY, not just THAT; (3) **proportionate action** – cleared the
gitignored cache (commits nothing) rather than a heavy install+render
or, worse, a regenerate-in-place that would re-bake the stale value; (4)
**completed the sibling audit** the issue asked for (all 6 live, only
GVA affected, `fg-se-validation` static); (5) surfaced the wrong premise
as a DECISION (FM \#23 / \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]). **Weaknesses
(honest):** (a) three self-inflicted shell slips – a zsh `for f ~/...`
parse error, a 2-min timeout from an unbounded `find docs/`, and a BSD
`stat -t` syntax error; all fixed immediately, no impact on the
deliverable, but a tighter first pass would have avoided them; (b)
Phase-3E is N/A (no runtime change) – stated. Capped at 9 by (a).

**Learnings:** **Learning 197** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` – a
“stale cache / re-render the docs” issue may have ZERO published impact;
verify the LIVE artifact + the CI build model (is the cache committed?
does CI consume it or rebuild fresh from source?) BEFORE treating a
local cache as a publication defect; if CI rebuilds fresh, the published
artifact is the source of truth (curl it); the correct “refresh” of a
stale gitignored cache built against a stale installed package is to
CLEAR it (regenerating in place re-bakes the stale value), and surface a
wrong premise to the owner as a disposition decision. Carried as
applied: \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]; extends Learning 196. **This was
NOT a TDD code-phase session.**

`devtools::install()` (or

`.quarto/_freeze` cache (it is intentionally gitignored so CI always

**Gotchas:** (1) **The published GVA article is CORRECT** – it shows

(`.quarto/`, `.gitignore:44`) and CI re-executes every article fresh

### What Session 210 Did

**Deliverable:** **Regenerated the two bundled GV example reports**
(`data/qcPedGvReport.RData`, `data/pedWithGenotypeReport.RData`) so they
**carry `fgSE`** (added to
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)’s
return in S208) **and made the necessary corrections.** The regeneration
also **corrected a latent defect S206 introduced**: the bundled `fg` was
a NON-reproducible `52.7641277`; the documented recipe deterministically
yields **`52.7546854`** (display `52.75`). **DONE** – committed on
branch `regen-bundled-gvreports-fgse` (commit `e8c6745f` + the close-out
commit); **NOT on master, NOT pushed** – the publish method is the
owner’s pick (see SUGGESTED NEXT). Owner-directed for the 2.0.0 release
(owner confirmed “2.0.0 is the bump for the next release”). **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-26 / 2026-06-26 **Status:** **DONE.** Strict-TDD
DEVELOPMENT session (RED -\> GREEN; phase declared each response; **no
REFACTOR** – display formatters settled in S208). **0 stakeholder
corrections.** Owner directed “regenerate and make necessary
corrections” at Phase 1; answered the PRE-RED-\>RED and RED-\>GREEN
gates + one **mid-GREEN `fg`-correction decision** (the additive premise
I had given turned out false – owner chose “Regenerate to
52.7546854”). - **The discovery (load-bearing):** I told the owner the
regen would be “purely additive” (`fg` reproduces `52.7641277`, only
`fgSE` added). **Wrong.** The in-memory verify-before-save guard caught
`fg = 52.7546854 != 52.7641277` (0.0094 gap, not FP noise; `fe` matched
to 8e-9). A `git worktree` at S206’s saving commit (`83d8640d`) ran the
clean recipe and ALSO gave `52.7546854` -\> the committed value was a
**contaminated RNG-state save**, never reproducible from its own recipe
(and was S206’s sub-agent’s original value before S206 “corrected” it to
the wrong number). Adopted the reproducible value -\> bundled data
matches its documented recipe for the first time. - **Purely additive to
user-visible tables:** old-vs-new diff -\> `$report`
(`gu`/`rank`/`value`), `$gu`, `$kinship` byte-IDENTICAL; only `fg`
changed (+ `fgSE = 0.0130413278`, display `0.01`). `gu` is
integer-percent (too coarse to register the gene-drop difference);
kinship is deterministic. - **RED (corrected to the reproducible value,
all failing for the right reason – data still old):** `test_reportGV.R`
bundled-report `fgSE` present/finite/\>0 + \#86 pin
`52.7641277 -> 52.7546854`; `test_summary.nprcgenekeeprGV.R` bundled
[`summary()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/summary.html) shows
`52.75 +/- <SE>`, absent-`fgSE` fixture rewritten to STRIP `fgSE` from a
copy (`gv$fgSE <- NULL` – backward-compat path now independent of the
bundled object), `52.76 -> 52.75` patterns. `test_modSummaryStats.R`
UNCHANGED (synthetic `52.76` fixture, independent of bundled data). -
**Build-equivalent (all green):** `devtools::check(vignettes=FALSE)` =
**0/0/0** (ran all `@examples` + spelling); full suite **0 failed / 0
error** (true offenders excl. baseline = 0); `spell_check_package(".")`
= **0**; only the two `.RData` modified.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): covered by the suite, no separate launch
(stated, not skipped).** The change is golden DATA + tests, not
wiring/startup/dispatch. The `test_summary.nprcgenekeeprGV.R` assertion
drives the REAL [`summary()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/summary.html)
render on the regenerated bundled object and shows
`Founder Genome Equivalents: 52.75 +/- 0.01` – the actual user-facing
render path on real data. The four display surfaces were already
smoke-tested end-to-end in S208; this changes only the bundled value
they read.

**Session 209 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 210): Score 9/10.** S209’s
handoff recorded the post-merge state precisely, and **every
load-bearing anchor held FIRSTHAND**: \#82 and \#86 are CLOSED (verified
via `gh issue view`); the merged `fgSE` work is genuinely on `master`
(`R/calcFGSE.R`, `NAMESPACE` export, `reportGV.R:204`, all four display
sites); `master == origin/master` (HEAD `5e81ca27`, ghost-check clean);
“No \#82/#86 follow-up remains” was TRUE as stated (both issues’
defect/feature lines are complete – I verified: 0 `TODO`/`FIXME`, 0
unchecked plan boxes). It also correctly flagged the version-bump as the
owner-gated live thread. ROI: high – orientation was fast and nothing it
claimed was wrong. **The -1:** S209’s NEWS bullet propagated S206’s
**non-reproducible `52.76`** into NEWS (“39.92 -\> 52.76”) – a value
this session had to correct to `52.75`. S209 couldn’t reasonably have
known (regenerating wasn’t on its radar; the defect was S206’s), but a
spot-check of the bundled `fg` against the documented recipe would have
caught it. A 10 would have either verified the bundled value reproduces,
or hedged the NEWS number.

**Self-assessment (Session 210): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read IN FULL; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner;
wrote the **1B stub BEFORE technical work**; declared the TDD phase
every response; gated both phase transitions + the scope/correction
decisions via `AskUserQuestion`. **Strengths:** (1) **the
verify-before-save guard** (compute in-memory, compare to the committed
value, save only on match) caught a non-reproducible golden value that a
naive [`save()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/save.html)-then-check would have
silently shipped; (2) **diagnosed the cause decisively** with a worktree
at the saving commit rather than guessing – proved it was an S206
contamination, not S208 drift; (3) **re-surfaced the moved value as a
decision mid-GREEN** instead of quietly editing a \#86-established
golden number – the additive premise was MINE, so correcting it was owed
to the owner (FM \#23 / \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]); (4) **answered
the owner’s vignette/`@example` question with evidence** (old-vs-new
byte diff) not assertion – showed only `fg` moved and nothing downstream
reads it; (5) **scoped `resaveRdaFiles` to the two files** after
catching it churned all 19; (6) **filed issue \#88** for the incidental
stale GVA freeze instead of silently expanding scope (FM \#18/#25).
**Weaknesses (honest):** (a) I gave the owner a confidently WRONG
“purely additive” premise in the pre-work analysis – the right move
would have been to dry-run the recipe during the analysis phase (before
claiming additivity), which is exactly Learning 192’s lesson
under-applied; caught it at the save guard, but the owner approved on a
false premise and I had to walk it back; (b) Phase-3E is suite-covered,
no separate app launch – stated, not skipped. Capped at 9 by (a).

**Learnings:** **Learning 196** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` – before
overwriting bundled/golden data, verify the documented recipe reproduces
the COMMITTED values in-memory first (a “purely additive” regen can mask
a pre-existing non-reproducible save); attribute any mismatch with a
`git worktree` at the saving commit; diff old-vs-new to bound the blast
radius (and answer “what downstream is affected”);
`resaveRdaFiles("data/")` recompresses the whole dir (scope it to
files); and re-surface a moved value to the owner as a decision. Carried
as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]; Learnings 192 (extended – also
verify the SAVED object reproduces), 194 (the `fgSE` wiring this
completes for the bundled objects). **This WAS a TDD code-phase
session** (data + tests).

**Gotchas:** (1) **YOU ARE ON LOCAL BRANCH
`regen-bundled-gvreports-fgse`** – this session’s work is committed
HERE, NOT on master (`5e81ca27`, S209), NOT pushed.

the recipe `set_seed(10); reportGV(<ped>, guIter=10000)`
deterministically gives `52.7546854`. Pin/expect the reproducible value.
(3) **`tools::resaveRdaFiles(\"data/\")` recompresses ALL `.RData` in
the dir** – pass explicit file paths
(`c("data/qcPedGvReport.RData","data/pedWithGenotypeReport.RData")`);
`git checkout -- data/` to undo a directory-wide churn. (4)

commit, not squash” so the slice commits + their issue refs survive;

### What Session 208 Did

**Deliverable:** **Implemented Slice 3 of issue \#82** – surface the
founder-genome-equivalent sampling SE (`fgSE`) as **inline `FG +/- SE`**
(ASCII, 2-decimal SE) wherever FG is shown: threaded `fgSE` into
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)‘s
return + displayed inline in the GV-tab summary, Summary-Statistics
founder table, text [`summary()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/summary.html),
and `makeFounderStatsTable` HTML, with the broad D6 guidance
reconciliation. **(DONE – committed on LOCAL branch `issue-82-fgse`; NOT
on master, NOT pushed; publishes with the whole \#82 branch, FM \#18.
Issue \#82 stays OPEN until merge.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-26 / 2026-06-26 **Status:** **DONE.** Strict-TDD DEVELOPMENT
session (RED -\> GREEN; phase declared each response; **no REFACTOR** –
the four display sites’ bare-FG formats differ, so a shared formatter
would force a behavior change + break a backward-compat pin). **0
stakeholder corrections.** Owner directed “#82 Slice 3” at Phase 1;
answered 1 scope `AskUserQuestion` (SE format = **`52.79 +/- 0.05`**,
ASCII 2-dec; bundled reports = **leave additive, guard on presence**) +
the PRE-RED-\>RED and RED-\>GREEN phase gates. - **Compute/return
(`R/reportGV.R`):** after `feFg <- calcFEFG(ped, alleles)`,
`fgSE <- calcFGSE(ped, alleles)` from the SAME gene drop; returned next
to `fg = feFg$FG` (a colony SCALAR – NOT a per-animal `$report`/`$gu`
column, plan F2); `@return` documents it. `NA` (with warning) on
zero-retention degeneracy, same as FG. - **Display (inline `FG +/- SE`,
each site GUARDED `!is.null(fgSE) && is.finite(fgSE)` -\> else bare
FG):** `modGeneticValue` `output$gvSummary` FG row + `founderStats()`
re-export (`fgSE = fr$fgSE`); `modSummaryStats` founder table cell;
`summary.nprcgenekeeprGV` text line; `makeFounderStatsTable` HTML cell
(+`@param`). All `sprintf("%.2f +/- %.2f", fg, fgSE)`. - **Docs (D6
broadest):** FG-SE note + finite-K (Jensen) caveat in
`genetic_value.html`; reconciled `population_genetics_terms.html`,
`summary_stats.html`, the GVA article
(`vignettes/articles/genetic-value-analysis.qmd` – now also prints
`fgSE`), `_summary_statistics.Rmd`. **`gvAndBgDesc.html` and
`_genetic_value_analysis.Rmd` describe NO FG (grep-confirmed) -\>

FG). - **RED (5 files, all confirmed failing for the right reason before

(founder cell `+/-`). - **Build-equivalent (all green):** full suite **0

**Self-assessment (Session 208): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read IN FULL; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check clean – HEAD `2781f5ab`=S207), reported,
STOPPED for the owner; wrote the **1B stub BEFORE technical work**;
declared the TDD phase every response; gated the scope decision + both
phase transitions via `AskUserQuestion`; produced ONLY Slice 3 (no NEWS
bullet – folds into the publish session, Learning 157a; no bundled-data
regen – owner pick; no drive-by cleanups – FM \#18/#25). **Strengths:**
(1) **mirrored the `guSE` precedent exactly** – additive, golden-master
FG/FE byte-unchanged, low-risk wiring; (2) **guarded every display on
present-AND-finite** so degenerate (`NA`) results and old/bundled
objects degrade to bare FG (tested – the `&&` short-circuits NULL before
`is.finite`, and `is.finite` catches the hard-fail `NA`); (3) **resisted
the shared-formatter REFACTOR** after recognizing the four sites’ BARE
formats genuinely diverge (`sprintf` vs `round`/`as.character` vs
HTML-with-N/A) – a helper would have changed
[`summary()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/summary.html)/`makeFounderStatsTable`
bare output `22.3`-\>`22.30` and broken the `grepl(">22.3<")` pin; (4)
**Phase-3E done faithfully without a browser** by driving the real
render code paths (`testServer` outputs + the pure HTML/text fns) on a
real `reportGV(qcPed)` result, showing the literal `52.79 +/- 0.04`; (5)
**verified D6 scope by READING** – grepped all 7 named docs, found 2
have no FG content, edited the 5 that do (did not force empty edits);
(6) **handled the two `expect_named` structure-pin breaks as
additive-contract updates** (inserted `"fgSE"` after `"fg"`), not as
discriminators to weaken; (7) the integration test asserts PROPERTIES
(`>0`, scalar, absent from per-animal surfaces) since reportGV’s
internal gene drop is not externally reconstructible – exactness is
already proven by Slice 1’s oracle. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **lint
two-pass** – my first `# nolint: nonportable_path_linter` attempt pushed
3 deeply-indented lines \>80 chars (`line_length`), needing a second
pass (inline where it fits + `# nolint start/end` block where
indentation is deep); a tighter first pass would have anticipated the
comment length; (b) the reportGV integration test uses `qcPed` (~3.4s,
`skip_on_cran`) and asserts properties not an exact value – correct
(internal RNG not reconstructible) but worth naming; (c) my Phase-3E
smoke SCRIPT had a `\\+` regex-escape bug on first run – a
self-inflicted scripting slip, fixed immediately, no impact on the
deliverable. Capped at 9 by (a).

**Learnings:** **Learning 194** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` –
surfacing an additive companion value (`fgSE` beside `fg`) across N
display surfaces is mostly `guSE`-style wiring, but the two judgment
calls are (a) guard EVERY site on present-AND-finite so
absent/`NA`/old-object cases degrade to the bare value, and (b) DO NOT
extract a shared formatter when the bare-value formats already differ
(the dedup forces a behavior change + breaks a backward-compat pin);
plus an additive return element breaks exact-name-pin structure tests
(update them – they are the contract), reportGV’s internal gene drop is
not externally reconstructible (assert properties, not an exact value),
a headless UI runtime smoke = drive the real render paths on real data,
and D6 “broadest” scope is verified by READING (2 of 7 named docs had no
FG content). Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]
/ \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\]; Learnings 191 (#86 fixed, so
a correct FG pairs with this SE), 193 (Slice 2 calibrated the SE this
surfaces). **This WAS a TDD code-phase session.**

FG (never `+/- NA`). (4) **DO NOT DRY the four display formatters into
one helper** – their BARE-FG formats differ (`sprintf("%.2f")` vs
`round`/`as.character` vs HTML+`N/A`); a shared sprintf-bare helper
changes `22.3`-\>`22.30` and breaks the `grepl(">22.3<")`
backward-compat pin. (5) **The `+/-` format string trips
`nonportable_path_linter`** (the `/`) – suppress with
`# nolint: nonportable_path_linter.` inline where the line stays \<=80,
or `# nolint start/end:` on their own lines where deep indentation would
push the inline comment past 80 (the block comments do NOT change the
if-branch’s return value). (6) **reportGV’s INTERNAL gene drop is not
externally reconstructible** (`set.seed(s); geneDrop(...)` externally
gives DIFFERENT alleles – reportGV consumes RNG before its gene drop);
the integration test asserts PROPERTIES (`>0`, scalar, finite) not an
exact value (exactness is Slice 1’s oracle’s job). (7) **`document()`
re-wraps `man/calcGUSE.Rd`** (pure whitespace, the known local-roxygen
