read-only 4-agent grounding workflow informed the scope decision (ran
concurrently with CI); everything that MUTATES files (the serial
publish + branch delete + the one cohesive TDD change against one
package) stayed SOLO. - **REAL WORK \#1 – item 1, published with full CI
gate (Learning 133/135):** `git fetch`; confirmed `wire-file-pedsource`
was **1 ahead / 0 behind** `origin/master` (1 ahead = S151’s
`1145d3ef`), `origin/master` (`b8a6a5ec`) a strict ancestor (clean
fast-forward), `merge-tree --write-tree` dry-run clean (0 conflict
markers). `git push -u origin`; opened **PR \#62** -\> `master`; watched
all checks via background `gh pr checks 62 --watch` -\> **10/10 PASS**
(`lint` 3m30s, all 5 `R CMD check` platforms incl. ubuntu-devel 16m21s,
`pkgdown`, `test-coverage`, `codecov` patch+project). Confirmed
`mergeStateStatus: CLEAN` + `mergeable: MERGEABLE` + all 10 SUCCESS
BEFORE `gh pr merge 62 --merge` -\> merge commit **`cb46616e`**;
verified it landed (PR `state: MERGED`; `1145d3ef` now an ancestor of
`origin/master`). Reconciled local `master` via `git fetch` +
`reset --hard origin/master` (NOT `git pull` – Learning 135). S151’s
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
is now on `master`. - **REAL WORK \#2 – item 2,
verified-merged-before-delete (S143/S146/S149/S150/S151 pattern):**
confirmed `pedsource-file-provider` (local +
`origin/pedsource-file-provider`, both at `4af5e020`, merged via PR
\#61) is a strict ancestor of `origin/master`, deleted local
(`git branch -d` – safe merged-only) + remote
(`git push origin --delete` – explicitly owner-authorized);
`git fetch --prune` + verified **no ref remains** either side. Created
branch **`wire-focal-file-source`** off reconciled `master`
(`cb46616e`); the 1B stub carried over via
`git stash push SESSION_NOTES.md` -\> `stash pop`. - **REAL WORK \#3 –
item 3 grounding (the wiring-SHAPE analysis -\> Learning 145):** a
read-only 4-agent workflow (the focal-animal pipeline
`getFocalAnimalPed.R`; the `modInput.R` app wiring; the relatives
family + seam; the test surface + planning doc) + firsthand reads of
`getFocalAnimalPed.R`, `modInput.R` (UI 55-144 + server 238-368),
`getEmptyErrorLst.R`, the planning doc Rec \#4/#5, and the existing
modInput focal tests. Surfaced **two load-bearing constraints** that
decided the design beyond the abstract sibling-vs-parameterize call:
**(a)** the positional 7-column rename at `getFocalAnimalPed.R:76` is
**LabKey-shaped** – a file pedigree (its own named columns, file order)
cannot share it, so a separate function is correct (not just stylistic);
**(b)**
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
errors **loudly** but the focal-ped layer is fail-soft, so a
loud-\>fail-soft boundary is needed. Confirmed `getFocalAnimalPed`’s
`fileName` is the focal-**ID list** (not a pedigree), and
`modInput.R:329` keys on `inherits(built, "nprcgenekeeprErr")`. Owner
(pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`) chose **Sibling + full app wiring**
(over a function-only tracer or parameterizing the LabKey-named
function). - **REAL WORK \#4 – strict TDD (RED -\> GREEN -\>
REFACTOR):** **RED** – new
`tests/testthat/test_getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.R` (7 tests:
connected-component read from a focal-id file + pedigree-file fixture
incl. collateral; equivalence to `getFileDirectRelatives`; a `mockery`
delegation check pinning
`getFileDirectRelatives(ids, fileName=pedigreeFileName, sep)`; fail-soft
`NULL` on NULL/missing pedigree file, nonexistent file, missing
id/sire/dam; a `sep=";"` round-trip) + 2 new `test_modInput.R`
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
tests (offline focal-file path builds the QC-cleaned pedigree with
`getLkDirectRelatives` **mocked to
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)** to prove no EHR call; a
bad pedigree file -\> “File Read Error”). Ran -\> all 9 FAIL for the
right reason (function: “could not find function”; modInput: the EHR
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) fired because dispatch did
not exist), **163 existing modInput tests stayed green** as the
regression guard. **GREEN** – new exported
`R/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile(fileName, pedigreeFileName = NULL, sep = ",")`
= read focal Ids -\>
`tryCatch(getFileDirectRelatives(ids, pedigreeFileName, sep), error = NULL)`;
`modInput` UI gained an optional `focalPedigreeFile` input on the focal
panel + its focal server branch dispatches to the offline fn when a
pedigree file is supplied else the unchanged LabKey path (a `NULL` flows
to the existing `is.null(rawData)` “File Read Error” handler – zero new
error code). `roxygenise` -\> `NAMESPACE` (+`export`) +
`man/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.Rd`. 9/9 new green. **REFACTOR** –
extracted the duplicated focal-id read into a shared internal
`R/readFocalAnimalIds.R` (`@noRd`) used by BOTH `getFocalAnimalPed` and
`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile` (behavior-neutral); roxygen + a runnable
tempfile `@examples`; NEWS.Rmd “New features” entry + re-rendered
NEWS.md; CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - **REAL WORK \#5 – caught
a REFACTOR-induced test breakage (the honest miss + recovery):** my “3
files green” REFACTOR re-verify printed only PASS/FAIL/SKIP and
**omitted the ERROR column** (violating the CLAUDE.md “check BOTH
`sum(failed)` AND `sum(error)`” rule), so I missed that extracting
`readFocalAnimalIds` broke the implementation-coupled test
`getFocalAnimalPed detects Excel file format` – it
[`mockery::stub`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mockery/man/stub.html)s
`excel_format`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter` **on `getFocalAnimalPed`**,
but those calls moved into `readFocalAnimalIds`, so the stubs missed and
the real `read_excel` hit a placeholder file (unzip error).
**`devtools::check()` caught it (1 ERROR).** Recovered cleanly:
re-pointed that test to `readFocalAnimalIds` (where the Excel logic now
lives). Full suite re-verified checking BOTH columns -\> **0 failed / 0
error**.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** Exercised the **real,
un-mocked** `getFocalAnimalPedFromFile` outside the harness: focal-id
file + `ExamplePedigree.csv` -\> the full connected component (2922
rows, the file’s own named columns id/sire/dam/sex/gen/birth/…, both
focal IDs + collaterals present); all three fail-soft paths return
`NULL` (missing pedigree file, NULL `pedigreeFileName`, missing
id/sire/dam). Confirmed the optional `focalPedigreeFile` input renders
in `modInputUI("inp")`. Build equivalent: **`devtools::check()` Status
OK (0/0/0)** \[re-confirmed after the test fix\]. Full suite `test_dir`:
**0 failed / 0 error / 2555 passed / 166 skipped**; `lint_package()`
**0** (after reinstalling to clear the Learning 137 object_usage
stale-install artifact); `roxygenise` drift confined to the `NAMESPACE`
export line + the new `man/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.Rd`.

**Session 151 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 152): Score 9/10.** S151’s
handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s
map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed **exactly my three items as the first
three options** – “(Publish S151) push `wire-file-pedsource` -\> PR -\>
CI -\> merge”, “(Delete the merged `pedsource-file-provider` branch)”,
and “(Wire a file pedigree source through the focal-animal APP pipeline
– Option C)”. Every operational gotcha held firsthand: gotcha 1
(file-twin UNPUSHED on `wire-file-pedsource`, `origin/master` at
`b8a6a5ec`, branch based on `b8a6a5ec` so “PRs cleanly / 0 behind”) held
EXACTLY (1 ahead / 0 behind, clean FF, merge-tree clean, PR CLEAN);
gotcha 2 (`pedsource-file-provider` merged-but-exists -\> deletion
candidate; confirmed strict ancestor); gotcha 3
(`getFileDirectRelatives` exported-but-unconsumed – I consumed it);
gotcha 5 was the standout: it pre-named the Option C landmines I’d hit –
the SEPARATE pedigree-file param (its `fileName` is the focal-ID LIST),
the fail-soft-vs-loud handling (`modInput.R:329` expects
`nprcgenekeeprErr`), AND the positional 7-column rename at
`getFocalAnimalPed.R:76` – all three were load-bearing in my
grounding/design; gotcha 7/8 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`; standing
keeps) held. ROI strongly positive – I executed straight off it. **The
-1 (to make it a 10):** gotcha 5 NAMED the landmines but did not name
the WIRING-SHAPE decision (sibling `getFocalAnimalPedFromFile` vs
parameterize vs new-UI-vs-reuse) or the error-contract DIRECTION choice
(NULL-for-file-modality vs `nprcgenekeeprErr`), nor that the positional
rename is INCOMPATIBLE with a file pedigree’s shape (the decisive
technical reason for a sibling) – all of which I surfaced in grounding.
Discoverable, arguably grounding’s job. (Symmetric to S151’s own -1
against S150.)

**Self-assessment (Session 152): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -\> HEAD `1145d3ef` = S151, no
undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the
session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline strengths:** (1)
**published item 1 + deleted item 2 with full safety discipline** –
10/10 CI + CLEAN + MERGEABLE before merge (did not merge blind),
verified landed firsthand, reconciled `master` via provably-safe
`fetch`+`reset`, verified-merged-before-delete with a no-ref-remains
check; (2) **grounded the wiring-SHAPE decision** (read-only 4-agent
sweep concurrent with CI + firsthand reads), surfacing the TWO decisive
constraints (positional-rename incompatibility + loud-vs-fail-soft) so
the owner picked in one shot -\> Learning 145; (3) **a genuine VERTICAL
slice (FM \#25)** – function + modInput server dispatch + UI input,
end-to-end (the app’s focal path now runs offline), not a horizontal
“function-only” slice; (4) **strict TDD held** – phase declared every
response, scope gate + all 3 phase gates, RED proven failing with
constrained matchers + an
EHR-[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) mock to prove no-DB (no
false-pass), GREEN minimal, REFACTOR behavior-neutral; (5) **chose the
contract by CALLER modality** (NULL for the file-input family -\> reused
the app’s existing “File Read Error” path, zero new error code); (6)
**right ultracode hybrid** (read-only workflow for grounding, SOLO for
all file mutation); (7) full verification incl. Phase-3E smoke of the
real fn + the rendered UI input; ASCII NEWS, plain language, 0
corrections, scope confined. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **I missed a
REFACTOR-induced test breakage in my own re-verify** – my “3 files
green” check omitted the ERROR column, so the
extraction-broke-the-Excel-mock-test slipped past me and was caught only
by `devtools::check()` (1 ERROR); I recovered cleanly but this is a real
process slip against the project’s own “check failed AND error” rule
(cost: one extra check cycle); (b) the **REFACTOR touched the working
`getFocalAnimalPed`** (helper extraction) which is what coupled it to
that test – owner-approved and behavior-neutral, but it traded a little
blast radius for DRY; (c) higher layer-count than S150/S151 (fn +
server + UI + helper), appropriate for Option C but more surface. Higher
difficulty than S150/S151’s capability-only adds (a real end-to-end
feature), and strict TDD held with full verification – but the first
pass was NOT clean (check caught a breakage), so 8/10, not 9. (I reserve
9-10 for a higher-difficulty deliverable delivered clean on the first
verification pass.)

**Learnings:** **Learning 145** (when wiring a capability THROUGH an app
pipeline, let GROUNDING pick the shape via concrete
data-shape/error-contract mismatches – a source-shaped transform a new
source would break is the decisive argument for a sibling, not just a
naming smell; match the new fn’s fail contract to the CALLER’s modality
to reuse the most existing handling; make fn+server+UI a real vertical
slice and PROVE “no longer needs X” by mocking X to
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html); a behavior-neutral helper
extraction may touch a working sibling when both are test-covered, but
it RELOCATES implementation-coupled `mockery::stub(where=...)` tests –
re-point them and re-verify the **ERROR** column, not just FAIL;
`skip_on_cran()` files need `NOT_CRAN=true`) added to
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 132 (ASCII NEWS), 133 (don’t
merge blind), 135 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`), 137 (object_usage
stale-install artifact on new functions – reinstall to clear), 139
(delete NEWS.html), 140/143/144 (ground firsthand; ground a scope fork;
prefer a symmetric sibling).

**Gotchas:** (1) **S152’s
[`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.md) +
modInput wiring is on branch `wire-focal-file-source` (UNPUSHED after
this commit); `origin/master` is at `cb46616e`** (carries S151’s
`getFileDirectRelatives`). **Local `master` reconciled to `cb46616e`.**
The branch was based on `cb46616e`, so it PRs cleanly (0 behind).
Publishing S152 is the owner’s call; reconcile any stale branch via
`git fetch` + (strict-ancestor confirmed) `git reset --hard`, NOT
`git pull` (Learning 135). (2) **`wire-file-pedsource` is now MERGED
(via PR \#62) but still exists local + remote** – a deletion candidate
(owner’s call; history preserved in merge `cb46616e`). I deleted ONLY
`pedsource-file-provider` this session (the explicitly-authorized one).
(3) **The app’s focal-animal path now runs OFFLINE when a pedigree file
is supplied.**
`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile(focalIdFile, pedigreeFile, sep)` reads focal
Ids from the first column of `focalIdFile`, builds the connected
component from `pedigreeFile` via `getFileDirectRelatives`, and is
**fail-soft `NULL`** on any pedigree-file problem (matches the app’s
FILE-input family -\> the existing `is.null(rawData)` “File Read Error”
handler at `modInput.R`); this is DISTINCT from `getFocalAnimalPed`
(LabKey), which returns an `nprcgenekeeprErr`. `modInput`’s focal branch
dispatches: pedigree file supplied -\> offline; else -\> LabKey
(unchanged). (4) **The focal family is now `getFocalAnimalPed` (LabKey,
returns the LabKey 7-column positional-renamed ped, fail-soft
`nprcgenekeeprErr`) / `getFocalAnimalPedFromFile` (file, returns the
file’s own named columns, fail-soft `NULL`).** They do NOT share the
`getFocalAnimalPed.R:76` positional rename (it is LabKey-shaped and
would corrupt a file pedigree) – only the focal-id READ is shared
(`readFocalAnimalIds`). (5) **REFACTOR-relocates-mocks gotcha (Learning
145):** extracting `readFocalAnimalIds` moved the
`excel_format`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter` calls out of
`getFocalAnimalPed`, so a test that
[`mockery::stub`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mockery/man/stub.html)-ed them ON
`getFocalAnimalPed` broke (an ERROR, not a FAIL) – I re-pointed it to
`readFocalAnimalIds`. When you extract a helper, RE-POINT any
implementation-coupled mock tests and re-verify the **ERROR** column,
not just FAIL. (6) **`lint_package()` shows `object_usage_linter` “no
visible global function” warnings for NEW functions until you
reinstall** (Learning 137 – the stale-install artifact); reinstall
(`R CMD INSTALL .`) then re-lint -\> the real lints surface (here: 2
over-length roxygen lines, fixed). (7) **NEWS render traps (BOTH):**
re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` (a) drops a `NEWS.html` byproduct that
`R CMD check` flags – delete it (Learning 139); (b) pandoc
smart-converts straight quotes/apostrophes/`--` to curly/en-dash in
NEWS.md – avoid apostrophes + double quotes + `--` in NEW source bullets
(Learning 132); pre-existing curly quotes in OLD entries are not mine
and ship fine. (8) **`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and
chokes on the `.DS_Store` keep – use `git fetch` +
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` on a stale strict-ancestor branch
(Learning 135). (9) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` – never commit. (10)
**`skip_on_cran()`-gated test files (e.g. `test_modInput.R`) need
`NOT_CRAN=true`** to actually run via `Rscript`/`test_file` – a “0
failed, all skipped” result is the gate firing, not a pass. (11)

the 1B stub carried over via stash/pop. - **REAL WORK \#3 – item 3
grounding (the wiring-SHAPE analysis -\> Learning 144):** a read-only
4-agent workflow (the `getPedigreeSource` seam + all callers; the LabKey
caller chain; the existing file-ingestion subsystem; the research doc
Rec \#4/#5) + firsthand reads of `getPedigreeSource.R`,
`getLkDirectRelatives.R`, `getFocalAnimalPed.R`,
`getPedDirectRelatives.R`, `getPedigree.R`,
`test_getLkDirectRelatives.R`, `test_getPedigreeSource.R`. Surfaced 4
wiring shapes with very different blast radius/value: (A) new sibling
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md);
(B) parameterize `getLkDirectRelatives(sourceType=)`; (C) wire the
focal-animal app pipeline (`getFocalAnimalPed`/`modInput.R`); (D)
reconsider (the doc framed the file source for offline TESTS; an offline
focal-subset is already composable via
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids, getPedigree(file))`; the app already reads
pedigree files directly). Plus the contract landmines: the
fail-soft-NULL contract is LabKey-ONLY (file errors loudly);
`getFocalAnimalPed`’s `fileName` is the focal-ID LIST not a pedigree;
the positional 7-column rename. Owner (pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`)
chose **A** – the clean symmetric family (`getPedDirectRelatives` engine
/ `getLkDirectRelatives` LabKey / `getFileDirectRelatives` file), no
naming smell, zero change to existing signatures. - **REAL WORK \#4 –
strict TDD (RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR):** **RED** – new
`tests/testthat/test_getFileDirectRelatives.R` (7 tests: CSV
full-component read incl. collateral O2; equivalence to
`getPedDirectRelatives` over the same file; a `mockery` delegation check
pinning `getPedigreeSource(sourceType="file", fileName, sep)` then
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids, ped, unrelatedParents)` with
`unrelatedParents` threaded; constrained-message errors for NULL/missing
`fileName` (`"fileName"`), missing file (`"not found"`), missing
id/sire/dam (`"column"`); a `sep=";"` round-trip). Ran -\> all 7 FAIL;
the 3 error-contract regexps were NOT matched by “could not find
function” (genuine RED, no false-pass – the S148 RED-discipline lesson).
**GREEN** – new exported `R/getFileDirectRelatives.R`:
`getFileDirectRelatives(ids, fileName = NULL, sep = ",", unrelatedParents = FALSE)`
= `getPedigreeSource(sourceType = "file", fileName, sep)` then
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids, ped, unrelatedParents)`; NO NULL guard (the
file source errors loudly by design – a NULL guard would be untested
dead code). `roxygenise` -\> `NAMESPACE`
(+`export(getFileDirectRelatives)`) + `man/getFileDirectRelatives.Rd`.
7/7 (17 expectations). **REFACTOR** – no structural code change needed
(the 5-line wrapper is already minimal, mirroring `getLkDirectRelatives`
minus the fail-soft guard); roxygen `@export`/@param/@return + a
runnable tempfile `@examples`; NEWS.Rmd “New features” entry +
re-rendered NEWS.md; CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - **REAL WORK
\#5 – proactive spelling check (no check iteration):**
[`spelling::spell_check_package()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/spelling//reference/spell_check_package.html)
after the NEWS re-render found **no errors** (`collaterals` already
whitelisted by S150; my new words `sibling`/`deterministic`/`agnostic`
are in the base dictionary), so `devtools::check()` came back **0/0/0 in
one pass**. Caught + removed the `NEWS.html` byproduct (Learning 139);
NEWS.md verified pure ASCII (Learning 132 – avoided `--` in the new
bullet from the start).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** The deliverable adds an
exported runtime function, so I exercised the **real, un-mocked**
`getFileDirectRelatives` outside the harness: confirmed it is exported
in the namespace; wrote a 6-row CSV pedigree,
`getFileDirectRelatives(ids="O1", fileName=tmp)` -\> the full connected
component `{D1,GC1,O1,O2,S1,X1}` (**includes collateral O2**); all three
loud-error paths fire with the correct messages (NULL `fileName` -\>
“‘fileName’ must be supplied”; missing file -\> “file not found”; no
id/sire/dam -\> “must contain columns id, sire, and dam”); `sep=";"`
round-trip returns the full component. Build equivalent:
**`devtools::check()` Status OK (0/0/0)** (full testthat suite +
spelling + examples incl. `--run-donttest` + vignette rebuild ran inside
it). Full suite `test_dir`: **0 failed / 0 error / 1988 passed / 167
skipped** (5 warnings = pre-existing baseline noise, my new file 0);
`lint_package()` **0**; `roxygenise` drift confined to the `NAMESPACE`
export line + the new `man/getFileDirectRelatives.Rd`.

**Session 150 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 151): Score 9/10.** S150’s
handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s
map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed **exactly my three items as the first
three options** – “(Publish S150) push `pedsource-file-provider` -\> PR
-\> CI -\> merge”, “(Delete the merged `walk-unification` branch)”, and
“(Wire the `"file"` provider to a caller) …
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
still hardcodes `"labkey"`, so the `"file"` source is capability-only.”
Every operational gotcha held firsthand: gotcha 1 (file-provider
UNPUSHED, `origin/master` at `1c883d16`, branch based on `17a3ee24` now
an ancestor of master so “PRs cleanly”) held EXACTLY
(`1 ahead / 1 behind`, merge-base `17a3ee24`, merge-tree clean, PR
CLEAN); gotcha 2 (`walk-unification` merged-but-exists -\> deletion
candidate; confirmed strict ancestor); gotcha 3 (the file source
unwired, un-curated, loud-erroring – all confirmed in grounding); gotcha
4 (`getPedigreeSource` signature/`"file"` requires `fileName`); gotcha 6
(NEWS.html + en-dash traps – handled); gotcha 7 (`pull.rebase` -\>
`fetch`+`reset`); gotcha 8 (standing keeps). Key files named
`getPedigreeSource.R`/`getLkDirectRelatives.R`. ROI strongly positive –
I executed straight off it. **The -1 (to make it a 10):** the “wire the
file provider” option gave EXAMPLES (“an offline/file-based focal-animal
pedigree path, or exposing file-sourced pedigrees through the seam”) but
did NOT name the actual wiring-SHAPE decision (new sibling vs
parameterize vs full-pipeline vs reconsider) or pre-flag the contract
landmines I had to surface in grounding (the
fail-soft-NULL-vs-loud-error mismatch; the overloaded `fileName`; the
composition-already-possible critique). Discoverable and minor –
arguably grounding’s job – but naming the wiring-shape decision would
have made it a 10. (Symmetric to S150’s own -1 against S149.)

**Self-assessment (Session 151): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -\> HEAD `4af5e020` = S150, no
undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the
session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline strengths:** (1)
**published item 1 + deleted item 2 with full safety discipline** –
10/10 CI + CLEAN + MERGEABLE before merge (did not merge blind),
verified landed firsthand, reconciled `master` via provably-safe
`fetch`+`reset`, verified-merged-before-delete with a no-ref-remains
check both sides; (2) **grounded the wiring-SHAPE decision** (read-only
4-agent sweep + firsthand reads), surfacing 4 options with different
blast radius/value AND the contract landmines, so the owner picked in
one shot -\> Learning 144; (3) **recognized the clean symmetric FAMILY
pattern** (Ped/Lk/File) and the PARITY argument that answers the
duplication critique (`getLkDirectRelatives` is itself a thin wrapper),
and chose **no NULL guard** (inherits the loud-error contract – no
untested dead code); (4) **strict TDD held end-to-end with a clean first
pass** – phase declared every response, scope gate + all 3 phase gates,
RED proven failing with **constrained** error regexps (no false-pass),
GREEN minimal (5-line wrapper), REFACTOR behavior-neutral (no code
change); (5) **proactive spell-check -\> `devtools::check()` 0/0/0 in
ONE pass**; (6) **right ultracode hybrid** – read-only workflow for
grounding, SOLO for all file mutation; (7) full verification – new file
7/7, suite 0/0, lint 0, check 0/0/0, Phase-3E smoke of the real
un-mocked function, roxygenise drift confined to the export line + new
`.Rd`; ASCII-only prompts, plain language, 0 corrections, scope
confined. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) the **GREEN change is small** – a
5-line exported wrapper; the value is the grounding/scope discipline +
clean TDD + first-class capability parity, not algorithmic depth; (b)
this is **capability/plumbing parity, NOT an end-user feature** – it
gives the file source a first-class entry point, but the HIGHER-value
wiring (the focal-animal app pipeline, Option C) stays deferred, so like
S150 this is an honest tracer-bullet (a clean new export not yet
consumed by the app/pipeline); (c) the **composition-already-possible
critique is real** – `getPedDirectRelatives(ids, getPedigree(file))`
works today; the new function’s marginal value is the validated seam +
named parity (I made the parity case explicitly, but a skeptic could
call it thin); (d) **three items** (two admin + one substantive) –
owner-authorized pairing, legitimate, but the substantive deliverable is
a bounded additive wrapper. The first pass was clean (genuine RED,
minimal GREEN, check 0/0/0 first try, no NEWS re-render iteration, no
spelling iteration). Consistent with S149/S150’s 8/10; I reserve 9-10
for a higher-difficulty single deliverable (e.g., the app-pipeline
wiring or a genuinely algorithmic change).

**Learnings:** **Learning 144** (to WIRE a new provider/capability to a
caller, prefer a clean SYMMETRIC SIBLING over parameterizing a
domain-named function – one export, zero blast radius on existing
signatures, no naming smell; answer the “composition already works”
critique with PARITY not novelty; a wrapper inherits its source’s
contract so don’t copy a guard that can’t fire – no NULL guard on a
loud-erroring source; enumerate wiring shapes in a pre-RED scope
`AskUserQuestion`; an exported function changes NAMESPACE + man) added
to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 133 (don’t merge blind),
135 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`), 137 (claim never precedes evidence;
expect the object_usage stale-install artifact on a new function), 139
(delete NEWS.html), 140/143 (ground a recommendation firsthand; ground a
scope fork), 141/142 (the walk semantics this family relies on).

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT = owner’s pick.** All three items DONE. Item 1

deleted). Item 3

took no outward action on item 3’s publish. - **(Delete the merged
`pedsource-file-provider` branch)** fully merged via PR \#61; local +

ONLY the explicitly-authorized `walk-unification` this session. -
**(Wire a file pedigree source through the focal-animal APP pipeline –
Option C, deferred)** the higher-value follow-on now that
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)

/ `modInput.R` build a focal-animal pedigree OFFLINE from a pedigree
file so the Shiny app no longer requires LabKey for that path. Needs a

`fileName`), fail-soft-NULL vs errors-loudly handling (the file source
errors; the app’s NULL-\>`nprcgenekeeprErr` path at `modInput.R:329`
expects fail-soft), and the positional 7-column rename. Larger; likely
its own session. - **(Remaining LabKey Rec \#5 / server-side –
deferred)** server-side filtering / `executeSql` / consuming the
centers’ `study.Pedigree`/`ehr.kinship`; and/or a non-LabKey other-EHR
provider on the same seam. Gated on measured pull size + per-center
availability + a live server (and the focal-id-filter vs
connected-component-walk landmine). - **(Permanent NEWS render fix)**
`html_preview: false` in `NEWS.Rmd` + pandoc smart-off (or
`.Rbuildignore`/`.gitignore` for `NEWS.html`) – ends BOTH the NEWS.html
NOTE (Learning 139) and the `--`-\>en-dash trap (Learning 132).
Candidate, not done. - **(Answer Open Q 8.1 against the live server)**
the actual ONPRC/SNPRC LabKey server version (owner / live-server
only). - **(CRAN Phase 5, owner-run)** win-builder x3 + R-hub v2 +
`submit_cran()` – owner PAT + email; HARD STOP
(`docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md`). - **A GitHub issue** –
\#46, \#45/#28/#9, \#2, \#37, \#36, \#29, or older
\#13/#12/#11/#10/#5/#1. **Do NOT** bundle options (FM \#18/#25); **do
NOT** start any without the owner picking.

**Gotchas:** (1) **S151’s
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
is on branch `wire-file-pedsource` (UNPUSHED after this commit);
`origin/master` is at `b8a6a5ec`** (carries S150’s `"file"` provider).
**Local `master` reconciled to `b8a6a5ec`.** The branch was based on
`b8a6a5ec` (current master), so it PRs cleanly (0 behind). Publishing
S151 is the owner’s call; reconcile any stale branch via `git fetch` +
(strict-ancestor confirmed) `git reset --hard`, NOT `git pull` (Learning
135). (2) **`pedsource-file-provider` is now MERGED (via PR \#61) but
still exists local + remote** – a deletion candidate (owner’s call;
history preserved in merge `b8a6a5ec`). I deleted ONLY
`walk-unification` this session (the explicitly-authorized one). (3)
**[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
is a NEW EXPORTED capability, NOT consumed by any caller yet** – it is
the file twin of
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
(reads via the `"file"` provider, then the source-agnostic
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)
walk). It returns an **UN-CURATED** ped (column-shaped via
`getPedigree`, validates only id/sire/dam; downstream
[`runQcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runQcStudbook.md)
curates) and **errors loudly** (like the `dataframe`/`file` seam
branches), NOT fail-soft (unlike `labkey`/`getLkDirectRelatives`). NO
NULL guard by design. (4) **The clean symmetric family is now
`getPedDirectRelatives` (in-memory engine) / `getLkDirectRelatives`
(LabKey) / `getFileDirectRelatives` (file).** An offline focal-subset
was already composable via
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids, getPedigree(file))`; the new function adds
the validated seam + a named, first-class, tested entry point with
parity to the LabKey wrapper. (5) **The HIGHER-value wiring (the
focal-animal app pipeline) is the natural deferred follow-on** –
[`getFocalAnimalPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPed.md)/`modInput.R`
still require LabKey for the focal-animal path. Wiring a file pedigree
source there needs a SEPARATE pedigree-file param (its `fileName` is the
focal-ID LIST, not a pedigree), fail-soft-vs-loud handling (the app’s
`nprcgenekeeprErr`/`failedDatabaseConnection` path at `modInput.R:329`
expects NULL, but the file source errors), and the positional 7-column
rename (`getFocalAnimalPed.R:76`). (6) **Server-side filtering /
`executeSql` remains DEFERRED** – gated on measured pull size +
per-center query availability/permissions + a live server; AND a naive
focal-id server filter is incompatible with the client-side
connected-component walk. Not attemptable/observable here. (7) **NEWS
render traps (BOTH):** re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` (a) drops a `NEWS.html`
byproduct that `R CMD check` flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it
(Learning 139); (b) smart-converts `--` to a non-ASCII en-dash in
NEWS.md – avoid `--` in the source (Learning 132). Permanent fix still a
candidate. (8) **`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes
on the `.DS_Store` keep – use `git fetch` +
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` on a stale strict-ancestor branch
(Learning 135). (9) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` – never commit. (10) Carried:
feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config.

`fileName` (`"fileName"`), missing file (`"not found"`), missing

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** The deliverable adds a
runtime branch to `getPedigreeSource()`, so I exercised the **real,
un-mocked** `"file"` branch outside the harness: wrote a 3-row CSV,

with id/sire/dam (ids `S1,D1,O1`); NULL `fileName` and a missing path
both error; the existing `dataframe` branch is identity-intact and

**Learnings:** **Learning 143** (when an owner offers an “A or B” scope
fork, ground BOTH directions before recommending – a read-only parallel
sweep keeps file-mutating work solo – and weight OFFLINE TESTABILITY
heavily; when extending a pluggable seam, derive the new provider’s
contract by mirroring its sibling branches, not by inventing one; plus
the server-side-focal-filter / connected-component-walk incompatibility)
added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 133 (don’t merge
blind), 135 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`), 137 (claim never precedes
evidence), 139 (delete NEWS.html), 140 (ground a recommendation
firsthand), 141 (walk divergence), 142 (the walk-unification S149
executed).

**Gotchas:** (1) **S150’s file-provider is on branch
`pedsource-file-provider` (UNPUSHED after this commit); `origin/master`
is at `1c883d16`** (carries S149’s walk-unification). **Local `master`
reconciled to `1c883d16`.** The branch was based on `17a3ee24`, which is
now an ancestor of `master` (via merge `1c883d16`), so it PRs cleanly.
Publishing S150 is the owner’s call; reconcile any stale branch via
`git fetch` + (strict-ancestor confirmed) `git reset --hard`, NOT
`git pull` (Learning 135). (2) **`walk-unification` is now MERGED (via
PR \#60) but still exists local + remote** – a deletion candidate
(owner’s call; history preserved in merge `1c883d16`). I deleted ONLY
`labkey-pedsource-adapter` this session (the explicitly-authorized one).
(3) **The `"file"` provider is a NEW internal capability but NOT wired
to any production caller** –
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
still hardcodes `getPedigreeSource("labkey")`. The `"file"` source
returns an **UN-CURATED** ped (column-shaped via `getPedigree`, NOT
`qcStudbook`’d) – consistent with the `labkey`/`dataframe` branches;
downstream
[`runQcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runQcStudbook.md)
curates. It **errors loudly** (like `dataframe`), not fail-soft (unlike
`labkey`). (4) **`getPedigreeSource()` signature is now
`(sourceType, siteInfo, ped, fileName, sep)`** – `"file"` requires
`fileName` (errors if NULL or the file is missing); `sep` defaults
`","`; Excel/CSV dispatch is `getPedigree`’s, so `.xlsx` works through
the same branch. (5) **Server-side filtering / `executeSql` remains
DEFERRED** – the research doc gates it on measured pull size +
per-center query availability/permissions + a live server; AND a naive
focal-id server filter is incompatible with the client-side
connected-component walk (filtering to focal rows leaves the walk
nothing to traverse – it needs recursive/Pedigree-query work). Not
attemptable or observable here. (6) **NEWS render traps (BOTH):**
re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` (a) drops a `NEWS.html` byproduct that
`R CMD check` flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it (Learning 139); (b)
smart-converts `--` to a non-ASCII en-dash in NEWS.md – avoid `--` in
the source (Learning 132). Permanent fix still a candidate. (7)
**`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes on the
`.DS_Store` keep – use `git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<branch>`
on a stale strict-ancestor branch (Learning 135). (8) **Standing
keeps:** `.DS_Store` + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` – never commit.
(9) Carried: feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config.

Created branch **`walk-unification`** off the reconciled `master`
(`6424509b`) and popped the stub back cleanly. - **REAL WORK \#3 – item
3 grounding (the load-bearing analysis -\> Learning 142):** read both
walks + traced the worked fixture firsthand.
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
walked a strict ancestor/descendant lineage (two separate frontiers,
each re-seeding from the previous generation only) -\>
`{O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}`;
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)
walks the full connected component (re-seeds from the whole accumulated
id set) -\> `{S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1}` (pulls in the collateral sibling O2).
**Proved delegate == in-place:** after a full-component walk the
trailing `addIdRecords(unrelated, ...)` is a GUARANTEED no-op – the
walk’s fixpoint is exactly `getParents(ids) subset-of ids`, so no
referenced parent is ever missing -\> “delegate to
`getPedDirectRelatives`” and “widen the walk in-place” produce IDENTICAL
results in all cases. **Blast radius (grep):** the sole PRODUCTION
consumer is
[`getFocalAnimalPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPed.md)
(renames 7 positional columns, formats dates -\> feeds QC/kinship/GVA; a
larger pedigree is more complete, not breaking; the column contract is
preserved by `getPedDirectRelatives`’s `ped[ped$id %in% ids,]` return);
every other reference MOCKS `getLkDirectRelatives` wholesale
(walk-agnostic), so no other test moved. Owner (pre-RED approach
`AskUserQuestion`) chose **delegate** (the cleaner, research-doc-Rec-#4
path). - **REAL WORK \#4 – strict TDD (RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR):**
**RED** – FLIPPED S148’s deferred guard in `test_getLkDirectRelatives.R`
to assert the full component `{S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1}` (incl. the
previously-EXCLUDED sibling O2) + a new equivalence assertion
(`result$id` setequal to
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids="O1", ped=fixture)$id`); ran -\> 3 assertions
FAIL (Absent: O2), no-session warning test + `expect_called` still pass.
The deferred-and-guarded behavior change carried its own RED spec (-\>
Learning 142). **GREEN** –
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
now fetches `pedSourceDf` via `getPedigreeSource("labkey")`,
NULL-guards, then returns
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids=ids, ped=pedSourceDf, unrelatedParents=unrelatedParents)`;
the in-line strict-lineage while-loop + `addIdRecords` tail removed.
Target 5/5; full suite 0/0. **REFACTOR** – roxygen @return/title now
describe the full connected component + delegation; removed the now-dead
`getSiteInfo`/`getDemographics` test stubs (getLkDirectRelatives no
longer calls them); NEWS.Rmd “Changes” entry + re-rendered NEWS.md;
CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - **REAL WORK \#5 – caught BOTH
NEWS render traps:** the re-render dropped a `NEWS.html` byproduct
(deleted – Learning 139) AND smart-converted my `--` to a non-ASCII
en-dash in NEWS.md (Learning 132) – reworded the source to drop `--`
(the rest of NEWS avoids it) and re-verified NEWS.md is **pure ASCII**
(`LC_ALL=C grep -P '[\x80-\xFF]'` -\> none).

`git reset --hard`, NOT `git pull` (Learning 135). (2)
**`labkey-pedsource-adapter` is now MERGED (via PR \#59) but still
exists local + remote** – a deletion candidate (owner’s call; history
preserved in merge `6424509b`). I deleted ONLY `labkey-config-defaults`
this session (the explicitly-authorized one). (3) **THE BEHAVIOR CHANGE
IS LIVE:**
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
now returns the **full connected pedigree component** (collaterals
included), so the LabKey/EHR focal-animal pedigree (via
[`getFocalAnimalPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPed.md))
GROWS vs the old strict lineage. The deterministic test in
`test_getLkDirectRelatives.R` asserts `{S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1}`
incl. sibling O2 – it FAILS if anyone reverts to the strict-lineage
walk. Verified via in-memory fixture + Phase-3E; **NOT observed against
a live LabKey server** (none here) – the actual EHR result-set growth is
inferred, not observed. (4)
**[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
now DELEGATES to
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)**
(fetch via `getPedigreeSource("labkey")`, NULL-guard, delegate).
`getParents`/`getOffspring`/`addIdRecords` remain in the package (used
by `getPedDirectRelatives`). (5) **NEWS render traps (BOTH):**
re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` (a) drops a `NEWS.html` byproduct that
`R CMD check` flags as a top-level NOTE – delete it (Learning 139); (b)
smart-converts `--` to a non-ASCII en-dash in NEWS.md – avoid `--` in
the source (Learning 132). Permanent fix (`html_preview: false` + pandoc
smart-off / `.Rbuildignore`) still a candidate, not applied. (6)
**`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes on the
`.DS_Store` keep – use `git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<branch>`
on a stale strict-ancestor branch (Learning 135). (7) **Standing
keeps:** `.DS_Store` + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` – never commit.

### What Session 148 Did

**Deliverable:** Owner-directed 2-item pairing (the S146/S147 pattern –
trivial admin + one substantive deliverable): **(1)** publish S147’s

`getPedDirectRelatives` **fetch boundary** + a deterministic mocked
integration test \[the substantive deliverable, strict-TDD\]. **(BOTH
DONE.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-20 **Status:** **DONE.** Owner
first said “Publish S147”; I claimed the session (1B stub) and started
the publish, but the owner immediately flagged that **a publish is
admin, not a true deliverable** – a correct call. I agreed, gave the
honest analysis (the publish is the deferred tail of S147’s work), and
**re-scoped via `AskUserQuestion`** to the 2-item pairing (publish rides
along as the trivial admin item; the owner picked **Rec \#4** as the
real work). Item 1 = **VERIFICATION/admin** (push -\> PR -\> CI -\>
confirm CLEAN -\> merge; no production code -\> no TDD gates). Item 2 =
**strict-TDD** (RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR, all three phase gates via
`AskUserQuestion`, preceded by a pre-RED **scope** `AskUserQuestion`
(fetch-boundary slice vs walk-unification vs plan-only) and a pre-RED
**approach** `AskUserQuestion` (internal `@noRd` vs exported)). **0
stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized **SOLO** (a serial publish + one
small cohesive TDD refactor against one package; fanning out
file-mutating agents would risk conflicts and add no confidence over
firsthand verification – the S144/S145/S147 rationale). - **REAL WORK
\#1 – item 1, published with full CI gate (Learning 133/135):**
`git fetch`; confirmed `labkey-config-defaults` was **2 ahead / 0 behind
`origin/master`** (exactly S147’s two commits `cefc14a4` + `3da6723f`),
origin/master a strict ancestor (clean publish); the uncommitted 1B stub
was NOT pushed (commits only). `git push -u origin`; opened **PR \#58**
-\> `master`; watched all checks via a background
`gh pr checks 58 --watch` -\> **10/10 PASS** (`lint` 3m26s, all 5
`R CMD check` platforms incl. ubuntu-devel 16m17s, `pkgdown`,
`test-coverage`, `codecov` patch+project). Confirmed
`mergeStateStatus: CLEAN` BEFORE `gh pr merge 58 --merge` -\> merge
commit **`1dd0c7e6`**; verified it landed (PR `state: MERGED`; both
`cefc14a4` and `3da6723f` are ancestors of `origin/master`). The Rec \#2
centralization is now on `master`. - **REAL WORK \#2 – item 2 grounding
caught a walk-divergence landmine (-\> Learning 141):** read
`getPedDirectRelatives.R`, `getLkDirectRelatives.R`,
`getDemographics.R`, `getParents.R`, `getOffspring.R`, `addIdRecords.R`,
`test_getDemographics.R`, `test_getLkDirectRelatives.R`, `.lintr`, and
the research doc (§5 Option B, §6 risk 6, §7 Rec 4/5). The doc’s Rec \#4
says “make
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
delegate its walk to
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)”
– but reading both walks, they are **NOT equivalent**:
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
re-seeds `parents`/`offspring` from the **previous generation only** (a
strict ancestor-up + descendant-down lineage), while
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)
re-seeds from the **full accumulated id set** (the full
connected-component closure, pulling in collaterals – siblings/cousins).
So “delegate the walk” is a **behavior change**, not a refactor.
Surfaced this via the pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`; owner chose
**“Adapter at fetch boundary”** (true refactor, walk byte-identical) +
**“Internal @noRd adapter.”** - **REAL WORK \#3 – strict TDD (RED -\>
GREEN -\> REFACTOR):** **RED:** new
`tests/testthat/test_getPedigreeSource.R` (6 tests: dataframe
passthrough; dataframe validation errors; labkey rename via
`mapPedColumns`; labkey NULL-on-warning + NULL-on-error; bad-source
`match.arg`) + a deterministic walk test in
`test_getLkDirectRelatives.R` (focal `O1` -\> strict-lineage set
`{O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}`, EXCLUDING the collateral sibling `O2` – the guard
against swapping in the full-component walk). Ran -\> failing.
**Caught + fixed a RED-discipline flaw:** the `expect_error` tests
initially PASSED falsely (a bare `expect_error` is satisfied by “could
not find function”) – constrained them to the intended error regexps so
absence-of-function no longer satisfies them -\> genuinely RED.
**GREEN:** new internal `R/getPedigreeSource.R` (`@noRd`,
`sourceType = c("labkey","dataframe")`); rewired
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
to obtain `pedSourceDf` from the adapter, walk + `addIdRecords` tail
**byte-identical** (the ignored `unrelatedParents` arg preserved).
**REFACTOR:** removed the now-unused
`@import futile.logger`/`@importFrom stringi stri_c` tags from
`getLkDirectRelatives` (verified `git diff NAMESPACE` empty); NEWS.Rmd
“Internal changes” entry + re-rendered NEWS.md;
CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - **REAL WORK \#4 – lint
false-positive caught + resolved by rename, not nolint:**
`undesirable_function_linter` flagged the selector parameter named
`source` (the bare symbol resolves to base
[`source()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/source.html)). `.lintr` excludes
`tests/` and there is no existing `source` identifier in `R/`. Rather
than scatter `# nolint`, I **renamed the parameter `source` -\>
`sourceType`** (lint-clean by construction; function name + behavior
unchanged), updating the two `R/` files + the (lint-excluded but
call-bound) test calls.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** The deliverable changes
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)’s
runtime fetch path, so I exercised the **real, un-stubbed**
`getPedigreeSource`: `"dataframe"` passthrough returns the ped
identical; `ped=NULL` -\> error matching `data.frame`; missing
`id/sire/dam` -\> error matching `column`; `"bogus"` -\> `match.arg`
“should be one of”; and confirmed `getLkDirectRelatives`’s body now
**calls `getPedigreeSource`** and **no longer calls `getDemographics`
directly**. Build equivalent: **`devtools::check()` Status OK (0/0/0)**
(full testthat suite + spelling + examples + vignette rebuild ran inside
it). Full suite `test_dir`: **0 failed / 0 error / 1959 passed / 167
skipped**; `lint_package()` **0**; `roxygenise` made **no**
`NAMESPACE`/`man` change. Caught + removed the `NEWS.html` render
artifact before check (Learning 139); no smart-quote artifacts in the
NEWS.md diff (Learning 132).

**Session 147 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 148): Score 9/10.** S147’s
handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s
map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed **both of my items as the first and third
options** – “(Publish S147) push `labkey-config-defaults` -\> PR -\> CI
-\> merge” (both publish paths pre-stated) and “(LabKey Rec \#4 – the
larger remaining work) formalize a data-source adapter on the
`getPedDirectRelatives` seam + a mocked integration test. The last
substantial LabKey item before CRAN re-submission.” Gotcha 1 (Rec \#2
UNPUSHED on `labkey-config-defaults`; `master` at `c12cfafd`) held
**exactly** firsthand (`2 ahead / 0 behind`). Every operational gotcha
held: `pull.rebase=true` -\> `fetch` (used it), the NEWS.html render
artifact (deleted it again), the load-bearing no-config fallback /
`defaultSiteParams()` single-source-of-truth (the adapter consumes
`siteInfo$lkPedColumns`/`mapPedColumns`, didn’t touch it),
feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge convention. ROI strongly positive – I
executed straight off it. **The -1 (to make it a 10):** it pointed at
Rec \#4 as “formalize a data-source adapter … + a mocked integration
test” but carried the research doc’s “delegate the walk” framing
**without flagging that `getLkDirectRelatives`’s walk and
`getPedDirectRelatives`’s walk produce different result sets** – the
landmine I had to find firsthand in the grounding pass (Learning 141).
Minor and discoverable (arguably the research doc’s gap, not the
handoff’s), but naming it would have made it a 10.

**Self-assessment (Session 148): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -\> HEAD `3da6723f` = S147, no
undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the
session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline strengths:** (1)
**took the owner’s pushback seriously** – when the owner flagged
“Publish S147” is not a true deliverable, I agreed, gave the honest
analysis, and re-scoped to a genuine substantive deliverable via

walk-divergence landmine** (`getLkDirectRelatives` strict-lineage vs
`getPedDirectRelatives` full-component) and I surfaced it via the
pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` rather than blindly following the doc
-\> Learning 141; (3) **published item 1 with the full CI gate**
(10/10 + CLEAN before merge, verified landed firsthand); (4) **strict
TDD held end-to-end** – phase declared every response, scope +
approach + all 3 gates, RED proven failing before GREEN, no impl in RED,
REFACTOR behavior-neutral; (5) **caught my own RED-discipline flaw**
(bare `expect_error` false-passes) and tightened the regexps so the
tests genuinely fail RED; (6) **no-behavior-change verified, not
assumed** – byte-identical walk + a characterization test that pins the
strict lineage AND the deliberately-excluded collateral; (7) **lint
false-positive resolved by rename, not `# nolint`** + cleaned the stale
import tags with `git diff NAMESPACE` verification; (8) full
verification – suite 0/0, lint 0, check 0/0/0, 3E, NEWS.html deleted;
ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined. **Weaknesses
(honest):** (a) the **RED tests had false-passes on the first run**
(caught + fixed, but a cleaner first pass would have constrained the
`expect_error` regexps from the start); (b) the **lint false-positive on
`source` cost a rename iteration** – anticipatable (a param named
`source` is a known `undesirable_function_linter` trip); (c) **moderate
difficulty** – a bounded TDD refactor + a publish; the value is the
grounding discipline + the walk-divergence catch, not algorithmic depth;
(d) the deliverable is the **fetch-boundary SLICE** of Rec \#4
(walk-unification + a `file`/other-EHR provider deferred) – correct
scoping (the full thing is a behavior change + ~1-2 days), but a slice.
I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty single deliverable with a clean
first-pass (no RED false-pass fix, no lint-rename iteration).

**Learnings:** **Learning 141** (two similarly-named functions can
produce different result sets, so a “make A delegate its walk to B”
recommendation is a BEHAVIOR change, not a refactor – read both
algorithms + diff their results before unifying; the true-refactor slice
is the FETCH boundary with the consuming algorithm byte-identical,
locked with a characterization test) added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`.
Carried as applied: 133 (don’t merge blind), 135 (`fetch` not `pull`),
137 (re-lint after `load_all`), 139 (delete NEWS.html), 140 (ground a
recommendation firsthand).

background `gh pr checks 57 --watch` -\> **11/11 PASS** (`lint` 4m36s,
all 5 `R CMD check` platforms incl. ubuntu-devel 17m7s, `pkgdown`,
`test-coverage`, `codecov` patch+project). Confirmed
`mergeStateStatus: CLEAN` BEFORE `gh pr merge 57 --merge` -\> merge
commit **`c12cfafd`**; verified it landed (PR `state: MERGED`; both
`4fd9bd40` and `799e98f3` are ancestors of `origin/master`). Reconciled
local `master`: stashed the stub, `git switch master`, confirmed
strict-ancestor, `git reset --hard origin/master` (not `git pull` –
Learning 135), created branch `labkey-config-defaults` off master,
popped the stub. The floor `Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0)` confirmed on `master`
(`DESCRIPTION:52`). - **REAL WORK \#2 – item 2 grounding caught two
research-doc landmines (-\> Learning 140):** read `getSiteInfo.R`, the
example config, `test_getSiteInfo.R`, `test_loadSiteConfig.R`,
`loadSiteConfig.R`, `getParamDef.R`, the research doc (§4.3 / §7 Rec 2 /
§6 risk 5), and grepped ALL `getSiteInfo` callers + every test asserting
its shape. Found the doc’s literal Rec \#2 sub-steps were unsafe for
THIS repo: (a) “reduce the no-config fallback to a clear error” is a
**BREAKING** change – the ONPRC fallback backs the Shiny app’s default
launch (`appUI`/`appServer`/`modORIPReporting`) and is pinned by **5
test files** + examples + the silent-`expectConfigFile=FALSE` contract;
(b) “align the example flat `dam`/`sire` to the lookup form” would make
the SNPRC example WRONG – the doc’s own §4.3 says flat `dam`/`sire` is
CORRECT for SNPRC (direct columns). Surfaced both via the pre-RED scope
`AskUserQuestion`; owner chose **“Centralize, no behavior change”** +
**“Document the center-specific form.”** - **REAL WORK \#3 – strict TDD
(RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR):** **RED:**
`tests/testthat/test_defaultSiteParams.R` (2 tests / 17 assertions:
canonical ONPRC values + names; `getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)`
LabKey fields == `defaultSiteParams()`, deterministic via
`withr::local_envvar(HOME=tempdir)`) -\> both ERROR (“could not find
function defaultSiteParams”), proven failing. **GREEN:** new internal
`R/defaultSiteParams.R` (`@noRd`) = single source of truth for the 7
ONPRC fallback params; rewired
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)’s
no-config branch to source from it, return **byte-identical**.
**REFACTOR:** center-specific `lkPedColumns` comments in the example
config (SNPRC flat `dam`/`sire` = direct columns; ONPRC `Id/parents/dam`
= curated lookup) + a characterization guard in `test_loadSiteConfig.R`
pinning the SNPRC flat form; NEWS.Rmd Documentation entry + re-rendered
NEWS.md; CHANGELOG/BACKLOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS. - **REAL WORK \#4 –
caught + removed a `NEWS.html` render artifact (-\> Learning 139):** the
first `devtools::check()` returned 0/0/**1 NOTE** (“Non-standard
file/directory at top level: ‘NEWS.html’”) –
[`rmarkdown::render`](https://pkgs.rstudio.com/rmarkdown/reference/render.html)
of a `github_document` writes an `html_preview` byproduct.
`rm -f NEWS.html` (untracked, not in the change set) and **re-ran**
`devtools::check()` -\> **0/0/0** (claim never precedes evidence –
Learning 137).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** The deliverable changes
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)’s
runtime path (now sources the fallback from `defaultSiteParams()`), so I
exercised the real un-stubbed function: with `HOME` forced to a
config-less temp dir, `getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)`’s 7 LabKey
fields each **MATCH the exact pre-change hardcoded literals**, the
17-field structure is unchanged, and `defaultSiteParams()` == the
literals -\> “no behavior change” proven, not assumed. Build equivalent:
**`devtools::check()` Status OK (0/0/0)** (full testthat suite +
spelling + examples + vignette rebuild ran inside it); `lint_package()`
**0** (after `load_all` – the one `object_usage_linter` flag on
`defaultSiteParams` was the Learning-137 stale-install artifact);
`roxygenise` made **no** `NAMESPACE`/`man` change (`@noRd`).

**Session 146 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 147): Score 9/10.** S146’s
handoff (my own immediately-prior session) was a near-perfect executor’s
map. Its SUGGESTED-NEXT listed **exactly my two items as the first two

positive – I executed straight off it. **The -1 (to make it a 10):** it

design landmines I had to find firsthand – that the no-config fallback
is load-bearing (so “reduce to error” is breaking) and that flat

Discoverable in the grounding pass (I found them in minutes), minor, and
arguably the research doc’s gap rather than the handoff’s – but naming
them would have made it a 10.

**grounding caught two research-doc landmines** (load-bearing fallback;

would have anticipated the `github_document` `html_preview` byproduct
(now Learning 139), and I left the PERMANENT fix (`html_preview: false`
/ `.Rbuildignore`) as a candidate rather than applying it (avoided scope
creep, but the trap can recur); (c) the “config-ize” landed as a
centralization-into-an-internal-constant (the owner’s no-behavior-change
pick), the lightest of the scoped options – correct, but not the heavier
“ship a config file”; (d) left the merged `rlabkey-version-floor` branch
undeleted (owner’s call) – recreates a small cleanup candidate. I
reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty single deliverable with a clean
first-pass verification (no artifact re-run).

**Learnings:** **Learning 139** (re-rendering a `github_document` Rmd
drops a `<name>.html` preview byproduct that `R CMD check` flags as a
top-level NOTE – delete it before check/commit) and **Learning 140** (a
research/recommendation doc can contradict its OWN ground-truth
sections + the live code – ground a recommendation firsthand before
implementing; both of Rec \#2’s literal sub-steps were unsafe here)
added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 133 (don’t merge
blind), 135 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`), 137 (object_usage
stale-install artifact + claim-never-precedes-evidence).

**Gotchas:** (1) **S147’s Rec \#2 work is on branch
`labkey-config-defaults` (UNPUSHED after this commit); `master` (local +
`origin`) is at `c12cfafd`** – it carries the S146 `Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0)`
floor but NOT the Rec \#2 centralization. Publishing S147 is the owner’s
call. (2) **`rlabkey-version-floor` is MERGED (via PR \#57) but still
exists local + remote** – a deletion candidate (owner’s call; history
preserved in merge `c12cfafd`). (3) **The no-config ONPRC fallback is
LOAD-BEARING** – it backs the Shiny app’s default launch + is pinned by
5 test files + examples + the silent-`expectConfigFile=FALSE` contract;
any future change to it MUST keep
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)’s
return byte-identical or it is a breaking change. `defaultSiteParams()`
(`R/defaultSiteParams.R`) is now the single source of truth for those 7
params. (4) **`lkPedColumns` is CENTER-SPECIFIC:** SNPRC flat
`dam`/`sire` (direct columns); ONPRC `Id/parents/dam` (curated lookup).
The example config (center=SNPRC) keeps flat; the no-config default
(ONPRC) uses the lookup form – do NOT “align” them, they are correct
as-is (now documented in the example + the guard test). (5) **NEWS
render artifact:** re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` creates `NEWS.html`
(`github_document` `html_preview`) which `R CMD check` flags as a
top-level NOTE – delete it before check/commit (Learning 139; permanent
fix not yet applied). (6) **`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`)
and chokes on the `.DS_Store` keep – on a stale strict-ancestor branch
use `git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<branch>`, NOT `git pull`
(Learning 135). (7) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` – never commit. (8) Carried:
feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge convention; `coalesce_linter` disabled
in `.lintr`; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated;
[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAILS FAST without a credential (S144).

### What Session 146 Did

**Deliverable:** Owner-directed 2-item pairing (“1 then 2”): **(1)**
delete the merged-dormant `labkey-apikey-auth` branch (local + remote
hygiene); **(2)** LabKey research **Rec \#1** — pin an `Rlabkey` version
floor in `DESCRIPTION` (the substantive deliverable). **(BOTH DONE.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-20 **Status:** **DONE.** Item 1 =
**VERIFICATION/admin** (a ref op, no production-code change). Item 2 = a
**CONFIG/packaging change** (owner pick “Config change, R CMD check” — a
`DESCRIPTION` version floor has no behavioral logic to unit-test, so
**no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR gates**; the build equivalent IS the
verification). **One `AskUserQuestion` (2 questions: floor value +
verify approach)**, posed AFTER grounding the floor + the version
research — preceded by an owner “clarify” on that question, which the
owner used to direct me to mine the EHR-module repos for the live LabKey
version. **0 stakeholder corrections.** Owner-authorized 2-item pairing
(S142/S143 pattern): item 1 trivial admin, item 2 substantive.
Right-sized: SOLO for the git admin + the one-line edit + verification;
**one focused workflow** (4 per-repo extractors → adversarial verify →
synthesis) to resolve the genuinely knowledge-gated “what LabKey version
do the centers run.” New session within the conversation; claimed it (1B
stub BEFORE technical work, now this handoff). - **REAL WORK \#1 — item
1, verified-merged-before-delete (S143 pattern):** `git fetch`;
confirmed `labkey-apikey-auth` (local + remote both at `6b0e892b`) is a
**strict ancestor of `origin/master`** (fully merged via PR \#56).
Deleted local (`git branch -d` — safe merged-only flag) + remote
(`git push origin --delete` — outward-facing, explicitly
owner-authorized “1”); verified **no ref remains** either side and
`master` still carries the auth feature
(`merge-base --is-ancestor 6b0e892b master` = YES). - **REAL WORK \#2 —
item 2, grounded the floor FIRSTHAND (caught a doc off-by-one):** the
package calls exactly two `Rlabkey` fns — `labkey.selectRows()` (old)
and S144’s `labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=, baseUrl=)`. The S143 research
doc said `apiKey` landed in 2.1.130, but the call also passes
`baseUrl=`, and `Rlabkey`’s own installed NEWS shows `baseUrl=` support
landed at **2.1.131** (apiKey 2.1.130, baseUrl 2.1.131) — so the true
minimal client-correctness floor is **2.1.131**, not 2.1.130. (→
Learning 138.) - **REAL WORK \#3 — resolved the “what server version”
gate via the EHR-module repos (owner-requested):** a workflow read the
four vendor repos (`ehrModules` + `onprcEHRModules` +
`snprcEHRModules` + `nircEHRModules`) via `gh api`. None pins a server
version in-file (build-injected via `ManageVersion`/centralized Gradle),
so the signal is the **highest non-SNAPSHOT `release` branch** under
LabKey’s `YY.M` scheme: **all four target 26.6** (June 2026;
corroborated by newest `*-26.000-26.001.sql` dbscripts), maintained
range ~19.x..26.6, all `confirmed:true` after adversarial per-repo
verify. **Honesty caveat carried:** a maintained release branch = the
version the module code is BUILT FOR, **not** proof the live ONPRC/SNPRC
servers RUN 26.6 (a center can deploy older) — bounded by the maintained
range, not pinned; Open Q §8.1 stays strictly unobserved. - **REAL WORK
\#4 — owner decision, edit, verify:** one `AskUserQuestion` (2
questions) → owner picked floor **`>= 3.2.0`** (conservative policy
bump: `Rlabkey` 3.2.0 assumes server ≥ 24.1, consistent with the 26.6
evidence) + **“Config change, R CMD check.”** Before writing the number,
confirmed firsthand it is a real release (`Changes in 3.2.0` in NEWS —
“only supported for LabKey Server v24.1 or later”) and installed 3.4.6 ≥
3.2.0. Edited `DESCRIPTION:52` `Rlabkey,` → `Rlabkey (>= 3.2.0),`;
committed **`4fd9bd40`** on new feature branch
**`rlabkey-version-floor`** (off `master`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — a `DESCRIPTION` metadata change,
no runtime behavior change** (a dependency floor changes install-time
resolution, not the runtime code path, which is byte-identical). The
build equivalent / verification is **`devtools::check()` → Status OK
(0/0/0)** — the full testthat suite (58s), spelling, examples +
`--run-donttest`, and the vignette rebuild all ran inside it and passed;
installed `Rlabkey` 3.4.6 satisfies the new floor. Matches S144’s
baseline. (Per the agreed config-change verification path; not silently
skipped — FM \#24.)

**Self-assessment (Session 146): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks
since the file is 1.5 MB; dashboard 98/100; ghost-check → HEAD

(strict-ancestor + post-delete master-retains check) — safe,

firsthand and CAUGHT the 2.1.130 → 2.1.131 off-by-one** by reading the
dependency’s own NEWS against the EXACT call (all args) — exactly the
error a version pin must not make; (3) **resolved a genuinely
knowledge-gated question** (live server version) by mining the vendor
module repos rather than declaring it unanswerable — workflow +
adversarial per-repo verify; (4) **honest about the residual gap** —
module-target ≠ deployed-version; Open Q §8.1 still unobserved; (5)

it** (claim never precedes evidence — Learning 137); (6) **posed the two
author’s-call decisions via `AskUserQuestion`** (and honored the owner’s
clarify → mine-the-repos request) before touching `DESCRIPTION`; (7)
**verified as the agreed config change** — `devtools::check()` OK 0/0/0;
(8) ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined.
**Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **low-to-moderate difficulty** — a one-line
`DESCRIPTION` change + a bounded research lookup; the value is the
off-by-one catch + verification discipline, not depth; (b) the **live
ONPRC/SNPRC server version is STILL strictly unobserved** — the floor
rests on the module-target inference + the fact that a `>=` floor
doesn’t actually need the exact server version (its job is
client-correctness + CRAN hygiene, which a floor CAN enforce;
server-compat it cannot); (c) item 2 was a **2-item pairing**
(owner-authorized, so legitimate). I reserve 9–10 for a
higher-difficulty single-deliverable session with no inherited
unobservable dependency.

**Learnings:** **Learning 138** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` —
pinning a dependency version floor: derive the client-correctness
minimum from the dependency’s own changelog against the EXACT call
(every arg); resolve a “what version does the site run” gate by mining
the vendor module repos (highest non-SNAPSHOT release branch = target,
distinct from deployed); a `>=` floor enforces only client-correctness +
CRAN hygiene, not server-compat (CRAN discourages upper bounds); verify
a DESCRIPTION floor as a config change via `R CMD check`. Carried as
applied: Learning 137 (claim never precedes evidence; confirmed 3.2.0
real + satisfied before writing), Learning 135 (`fetch`+`reset` not
`pull`, not needed this session but held).

baseUrl 2.1.131 / `Changes in 3.2.0` real / 3.4.6 ≥ 3.2.0),
`docs/research/labkey-integration-options-2026-06-19.md` (§3.4, §7 Rec
1, §8.1), the 4 EHR-module repos via `gh api`. **NOT committed (standing
keeps):** `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked); `.DS_Store`.
**Transient (NOT repo):** the version-detection workflow output (`/tmp`
tasks dir); `/tmp/s146_check.R` + `/tmp/s146_check.log`.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The floor change is on local branch
`rlabkey-version-floor` (UNPUSHED)** at the docs commit; **`master`
(local + `origin`) is still at `2554c846` (2.0.0) with NO floor.**
**Publishing item 2 is the owner’s call** (see Suggested Next) — I took
no outward action on it. Do NOT assume `master` carries the floor yet.
(2) **`labkey-apikey-auth` is DELETED** (local + remote) — done this
session (item 1); do not push/checkout it (its history is preserved in
PR \#56 merge `a39e73dc` on `master`). (3) **The floor is `>= 3.2.0`** —
a conservative POLICY pick (server ≥ 24.1), NOT the minimal correctness
floor (which is **2.1.131**: apiKey 2.1.130 + baseUrl 2.1.131, both used
by `setLabKeyDefaults`). A `>=` floor canNOT stop a too-NEW client
outrunning an old server (deployment matter; CRAN discourages the upper
bound that would) — its job is client-correctness + CRAN hygiene. (4)
**Module-target ≠ deployed version:** all four centers’ EHR modules
TARGET LabKey **26.6** (highest release branch), but that is the version
the code is BUILT FOR, not proof the live ONPRC/SNPRC servers RUN 26.6 —
research doc **Open Q §8.1 stays strictly unobserved** (bounded by the
maintained range ~19.x..26.6). Pinning precisely needs the live server.
(5)
**[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAILS FAST** without a credential (carried S144) —
`stop("No LabKey credential found.")`; the real LabKey path needs
`NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY` / a config `apiKey` token / `.netrc`. (6)
**`git pull` is configured to rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes
on the `.DS_Store` keep; on a stale branch use `git fetch` + (after
`merge-base --is-ancestor` confirms strict-ancestor)
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` — NOT `git pull` (Learning 135). (7)
**Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` — never
commit. (8) Carried: feature-branch → PR → merge convention;
`coalesce_linter` disabled in `.lintr`; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated.

now auto-configures auth before querying. **(DONE.)** **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-19 **Status:** **DONE. Strict-TDD (RED → GREEN →
REFACTOR; all three phase gates via `AskUserQuestion`).** **0
stakeholder corrections.** Did **NOT** bundle the three quick-wins (FM

\#3 of {version floor, config defaults, API-key auth}), then a pre-RED

source = env var `NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY` precedence then config

[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
auto-configures = fail-fast), then the three TDD phase gates.

feature against one package; fanning out file-mutating agents would risk
conflicts and add no confidence over firsthand verification. New session
within the conversation; claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). -
**REAL WORK \#1 — grounded the design firsthand BEFORE RED (found two
load-bearing constraints):** read `getSiteInfo.R`, `getDemographics.R`,
`getConfigFileName.R`, `getTokenList.R`, `getParamDef.R`, the example
config, `test_getSiteInfo.R`, `test_getDemographics.R`,
`test_getFocalAnimalPed.R` (the `mockery` idiom). Constraints: (a)
`getParamDef()` **stops** on an absent token → an optional `apiKey`
needs a soft reader; (b) `test_getSiteInfo.R` asserts
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)’s
**exact** field-name vector → do NOT add `apiKey` to `getSiteInfo`
(would break the test + ripple through ~10 Shiny-module callers). So the
helper reads the token itself; `getSiteInfo` untouched (lowest blast
radius). - **REAL WORK \#2 — RED (7 + 3 failing tests, proven):**
`tests/testthat/test_setLabKeyDefaults.R` (7 tests / 25 assertions:
env-var key, config-token key, env-over-config precedence, netrc via
`$NETRC`, netrc via home dir, no-credential `stop`,
empty-string-env-as-absent) using `mockery` (stubs ONLY
`labkey.setDefaults`) + `withr` (env control), all routed through an
injected `siteInfo` so every branch is deterministic off host state.
Added
[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
wiring tests (auto-auth + error propagation) to
`test_getDemographics.R` + made its skip-heavy integration test tolerate
the new fail-fast. Ran both → all fail (function undefined / wiring
absent). - **REAL WORK \#3 — GREEN (minimum impl):**
`R/setLabKeyDefaults.R` (exported): apiKey from
`Sys.getenv("NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY")` else
`getConfigApiKey(siteInfo$configFile)`; if present →
`Rlabkey::labkey.setDefaults(apiKey=, baseUrl=)` (returns
`method="apiKey"`); else if `hasNetrc()` → `method="netrc"`; else
`stop("No LabKey credential found." ...)`. Internal
`R/getConfigApiKey.R` (soft token read) + `R/hasNetrc.R` (`$NETRC` then
home `.netrc`/`_netrc`). Wired `R/getDemographics.R` to call
`setLabKeyDefaults(siteInfo)` before `labkey.selectRows()`.
`roxygenise()` → NAMESPACE export + `man/setLabKeyDefaults.Rd`. Full
suite **0 failed / 0 error / 1926 passed / 167 skipped**. - **REAL WORK
\#4 — REFACTOR/finalize + verify:** diagnosed 4 `object_usage_linter`
warnings as a **stale-install artifact** (only the NEW cross-refs
flagged; 0 after `load_all`; CI installs `local::.` before linting —
verified `lint.yaml`) → no `# nolint` on correct code (→ Learning 137).

optional `apiKey` token as a COMMENT in

`tests/testthat/test_setLabKeyDefaults.R`,
`docs/setup/labkey-authentication.md`. **CHANGED — code:**
`R/getDemographics.R` (calls `setLabKeyDefaults(siteInfo)` before the
query), `NAMESPACE` (+export +importFrom, generated),
`tests/testthat/test_getDemographics.R` (wiring tests +
fail-fast-tolerant integration test),
`inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config` (commented optional `apiKey`
token), `NEWS.Rmd` + `NEWS.md` (“(development version)” entry),
`.gitignore` (whitelisted `docs/setup/` so the setup guide is versioned
— `docs/*` is otherwise ignored). **CHANGED — close-out:**
`CHANGELOG.md` (S144 `[Unreleased]`), `BACKLOG.md` (Rec \#3 marked DONE;
remaining quick-wins + adapter left open), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`
(Learning 137), `SESSION_NOTES.md` (this handoff). **NO change to:**
`getSiteInfo.R`, `DESCRIPTION`, `data/`. **Read firsthand:** the files
in REAL WORK \#1. **NOT committed (standing keeps):**
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked); `.DS_Store`. **Transient
(NOT repo):** `/tmp/nprc_check/` build+check dir.

**Gotchas:** (1) **Two commits are now UNPUSHED:** S143’s close-out
`1a61dd4a` was already on local `master` unpushed at session start;
S144’s work is on a NEW local branch **`labkey-apikey-auth`** (off that
`master` tip), also unpushed. `origin/master` is still at `659aa864`
(S142). Publishing is the owner’s call — push the feature branch + PR
(the S142 convention; lets CI run), or fast-forward `master`. (2)
**[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
now FAILS FAST** without a credential — calls
[`setLabKeyDefaults()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/setLabKeyDefaults.md)
which [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)s with “No LabKey
credential found.” if there is no `NPRCGENEKEEPR_LABKEY_APIKEY` env var,
no `apiKey` config token, and no netrc. This is intended; it replaces a
later opaque `Rlabkey` error. Anything that drives
`getDemographics`/`getLkDirectRelatives` for real now needs a credential
(the test suite stubs them, so tests are unaffected). (3) **The auth
path is NOT validated against a live LabKey server** (none available
here) — the branch logic is fully mock-tested + runtime-smoked, but the
actual server handshake is unexercised. (4)
**`lintr::object_usage_linter` falsely flags freshly-added functions**
as “no visible global function” until the package is re-installed/loaded
— re-lint after `pkgload::load_all(".")` (→ Learning 137); CI is clean
because `lint.yaml` installs `local::.`. (5)
**[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
was deliberately NOT given an `apiKey` field** (its exact field set is
asserted by `test_getSiteInfo.R`); the credential reader is
self-contained — keep it that way unless you also update that test + the
Shiny callers. (6) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` — never commit. (7) Carried: working line
`master`, feature branch → PR → merge; `pull.rebase=true` (use
`fetch`+`reset` on a stale branch, Learning 135); `coalesce_linter`
disabled; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated.

### What Session 143 Did

**Deliverable:** Owner-directed 2-item pairing in one message — **(1)**

~42 KB): current `Rlabkey` API surface + auth model, alternative
integration approaches, EHR schema/query ground truth,
version/maintenance risk, with a recommendation. **(BOTH DONE.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-19 **Status:** **DONE.
VERIFICATION/RESEARCH phase** (declared; no production-code change → no
RED→GREEN→REFACTOR gates, no phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`). **0

under ultracode** for item 2 — a 43-agent workflow (investigate →

firsthand grounding + verification (read the 8 LabKey R files + the
example config to build the ground-truth anchor BEFORE the fan-out, fed
it to every agent as fact, and re-verified the recommendation-bearing
codebase claims after the draft). New session within the conversation;
claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - **REAL WORK \#1 — branch
