`add-methodology` is a **strict ancestor of `origin/master`**
(`git merge-base --is-ancestor add-methodology origin/master` = YES —
fully merged via PR \#55 / merge `f44a5322`; local + remote tips both
`7016c376`). Deleted local (`git branch -d add-methodology` — the safe
merged-only flag) + remote (`git push origin --delete add-methodology` —
outward-facing, explicitly owner-authorized). Verified **no ref
remains** and `master` still carries the work
(`git merge-base --is-ancestor 507de407 master` = YES, the S141 lint
fix). - **REAL WORK \#2 — built the ground-truth anchor firsthand:**
read `R/getDemographics.R`, `getSiteInfo.R`, `getLkDirectRelatives.R`,
`getLkDirectAncestors.R`, `getTokenList.R`, `getConfigFileName.R`,
`DESCRIPTION`, and `inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config`. Key
facts pinned: the ENTIRE integration is one `labkey.selectRows()` of
`study.demographics`; auth is the implicit `.netrc`/API-key default (the
example config L32-34 *explicitly* requires `.netrc` + links the LabKey
netrc docs); `Rlabkey` unversioned in `Imports` (`DESCRIPTION:52`);
cross-center drift visible already (example config uses flat
`dam`/`sire` + `/snprcEHR` vs the ONPRC fallback’s `Id/parents/dam`
lookup). - **REAL WORK \#3 — the research workflow:** 5 parallel
investigators (Rlabkey/CRAN; base `LabKey/ehrModules`; ONPRC;
SNPRC/NIRC; architecture/alternatives), each returning structured
`{claim, evidence, source, confidence, loadBearing}`; a `pipeline()`
adversarial-verify stage (refute each load-bearing claim against its
primary source via WebFetch/`gh api`); a synthesis stage (drop/correct
refuted, flag uncertain, build a claim-source appendix). **37
load-bearing claims → 33 confirmed, 3 refuted, 1 uncertain;** the
synthesis carried the CORRECTED claims with “Correction from review”
notes. - **REAL WORK \#4 — finalized + firsthand-verified the draft:**
re-checked the codebase claims the recommendations rest on
(`getPedDirectRelatives.R:41-43` source-agnostic seam;
`test_getDemographics.R` skips on CRAN + network → never
deterministically tested; `mockery` in `test_getFocalAnimalPed.R`;
`getLkDirectRelatives` does NOT yet delegate to the seam). Added a
“Method/provenance” note, placed the doc in a NEW `docs/research/` dir
(no prior LabKey doc existed — checked first), removed the `/tmp`-style
scratch draft.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — no runtime/behavior change**
(branch deletion is a ref op; the research deliverable is a doc; no
`R/`/`data/`/startup/config path touched). The proportionate “build
equivalent” for a RESEARCH_DOCUMENTATION deliverable is the
**claim-source verification**: 33/37 load-bearing claims confirmed
firsthand (adversarial stage), the rest corrected/flagged; all codebase
claims re-verified firsthand against the working tree; live-server-only

established the true baseline + the one real risk before editing:**
local `lint_package()` = 57 (= CI exactly), `lintr`/`cyclocomp`
installed locally so verification is firsthand. The “dragon”:
`coalesce_linter` (11) wants `%||%`, but it needs R \>= 4.4 (pkg
`Depends: R (>= 4.1.0)`) and shiny 1.13 **no longer exports** `%||%`
(verified `"%||%" %in% getNamespaceExports("shiny")` = FALSE) — so
adopting it would silently break R 4.1-4.3. Surfaced this; owner chose
to **disable `coalesce_linter`** in `.lintr` (consistent with the 7
linters it already NULLs). - **REAL WORK \#2 — fixed 46 code lints
across 28 files via a fix-\>adversarial-review workflow:** 31
`return_linter` (drop terminal
[`return()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/function.html); restructure terminal
`if/else` chains —
chooseDate/removeDuplicates/convertDate/dataframe2string/qcStudbook), 6
`seq_along`, 5 `file.path` (1 R/ + 4 data-raw), 2 line wraps, 2 `!any`.
27 files clean by both fixer + independent reviewer. - **REAL WORK \#3 —
central re-lint caught a CASCADE the fixers couldn’t see (-\> Learning
134):** after the workflow, re-lint read **5, not 0** — all in
`removeDuplicates.R`: the `boolean_arithmetic` autofix
`sum(duplicated(x))==0L` -\> `!any(duplicated(x))` cleared its target
but TRIGGERED `if_not_else` + `any_duplicated` + `unnecessary_nesting`.
Rewrote to `if (anyDuplicated(x) > 0L) <dups> else NULL` + a guard
clause for the [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) branch -\>
all four cleared, behavior preserved (incl. empty-vector edge). Re-lint
-\> **0**. - **REAL WORK \#4 — context-blind-linter judgments:** (a)
`nonportable_path_linter` flagged a **MIME-type** string in
`modPedigree`’s `fileInput(accept=...)`; the author had already
`# nolint`-ed the adjacent `"text/csv"` line (intent = false positive)
but missed the second string — the fixer wrapped the MIME type in
[`file.path()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/file.path.html) (output
identical, semantically wrong), so I restored the readable string +
extended the `# nolint`. (b) `cyclocomp` wart: added `any::cyclocomp` to
`lint.yaml` (`.lintr` already NULLs the linter; `linters_with_tags()`
warns at config-load when absent — non-fatal). (c) the one roxygen line
wrap (`R/data.R:358`) made `man/` stale, so regenerated (roxygen2 ==
pinned 8.0.0; diff confined to `man/rhesusPedigree.Rd`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED by `R CMD check`.** The
change is behavior-neutral R code + lint/CI config; `R CMD check`
(`--no-manual --ignore-vignettes`) loaded the namespace, ran all
examples (+ donttest) and the full testthat + spelling suites -\>
**Status: OK (0/0/0)**, which exercises the real runtime paths. No
separate Shiny-app launch needed: no startup/dispatch/config-resolution
behavior changed (the only `mod*.R` edits were a `# nolint` comment + a
restored-identical MIME string + implicit-return/`seq_along`/`%||%`-free
rewrites). The `.lintr`/`lint.yaml` edits are CI-only, not runtime.

**DONE. VERIFICATION/docs phase** (declared every response — no
production logic changed; no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR transition, so no
phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Owner

(the 3 content fixes) + the close-out commit
(CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES); both pushed. - **REAL WORK
\#1 — diagnosed the `names'` flag as a RENDER artifact, fixed at source
(→ Learning 132):** S138 said “fix the quote (don’t whitelist)” at
`NEWS.md:51`. But `NEWS.Rmd:53` *already* used straight ASCII quotes —
pandoc’s `smart` extension curls `'row.names'` → `‘row.names’` at
render, and the closing `'` against `names` makes the bogus token
`names’`. So there was no source quote to “straighten.” Wrapped the
literal message in backticks in `NEWS.Rmd`
(`` `duplicate 'row.names' are not allowed` `` — code spans are
smart-quote-exempt and read as the verbatim message), re-rendered
`NEWS.md` (github_document, `html_preview=FALSE`) → **exactly a one-line
diff**;
[`spelling::spell_check_package()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/spelling//reference/spell_check_package.html)
→ **0 flags** (was 1). Did NOT hand-edit `NEWS.md` (generated) and did
NOT disable `smart` globally (would curl-strip every other quote in the
500-line file). - **REAL WORK \#2 — mapped the README duplication before
cutting:** the citation rendered twice — first from the shared child
`vignettes/manual_components/_introduction.Rmd` (also consumed by
`a3manual.Rmd` + `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd`), second from README.Rmd’s
own hardcoded block (lines 46-51). Removed **only the README.Rmd block**
→ README-only blast radius (manual/tutorial vignettes untouched),
citation survives once in the Introduction. Re-rendered via
`devtools::build_readme()`; `git diff README.md` = removed block + an
incidental, correct
[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)
date refresh (`2026-06-17` → `2026-06-19`). Confirmed
`grep "For more information"` README.md = 1 hit. - **REAL WORK \#3 —
plan §9 reconcile, evidence-based:** verified firsthand DESCRIPTION

`names'` fix should be “fix the quote, don’t whitelist.” Its firsthand
facts held (versions at 2.0.0, branch sync, the README de-dup carried as
FM \#8). ROI clearly positive — I executed straight off the menu. **The
−2:** (a) the `names'` item pointed at `NEWS.md:51` and called it a
“curly apostrophe in a quoted base-R error” — accurate *symptom*, but it
did not flag that this is a **render artifact** (`NEWS.Rmd` already has
straight quotes), so “fix the quote” could mislead a literal reader
toward a source quote-swap that doesn’t exist, or toward editing the
generated `NEWS.md` directly; I had to diagnose the smart-quote root
cause myself (now Learning 132). (b) “Close \#53” rode the menu as an
open item, but the owner had already closed it before this session — a
world-changed staleness (not S138’s fault, but the kind of thing a
“verify state before acting” note guards against — cf. Learning 129).
(c) No note that `README.md`/`NEWS.md` are generated and the README dup
spans a *shared* child — I mapped that firsthand. A one-line “both are
generated; README dup = shared `_introduction.Rmd` + README.Rmd’s own
block” would have made it a 10.

**Self-assessment (Session 139): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top via offset reads, dashboard
98/100, git + `gh`, ghost-check → HEAD `db100064`=S138, clean modulo
standing keeps), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s direction; claimed
the session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline strengths:** (1)
**diagnosed the `names'` flag as a pandoc smart-quote render artifact**
rather than chasing a non-existent source typo or hand-editing the
generated `NEWS.md` — and fixed it the durable way (code span at
source), verifying the re-render diff was one line + spell check 0; (2)
**mapped the README duplication before cutting** and removed the
LOW-blast-radius copy (README.Rmd’s own block, not the shared child) —
manual/tutorial vignettes untouched; (3) **verified every claim
firsthand** (versions, `0/0` branch sync, \#53 already closed, the real
PR diff scope) instead of trusting the handoff; (4) **caught that \#53
was already closed** → did not redundantly act; (5) **opened the PR but
did NOT merge** — respected the outward-facing/owner decision (merge
timing + CRAN gating), with an accurate, comprehensive body that scopes
the real diff and doesn’t overclaim; (6) confined every commit (excluded
the standing keeps), captured Learning 132; ASCII-only, plain language,
0 corrections. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **batched a 6-item menu in
one session** — a deliberate, owner-authorized override of “1 and done”,
but worth naming as an exception, not a precedent; (b) **low-to-moderate
difficulty** — docs/admin + two re-renders; the value is the
render-artifact diagnosis + verification discipline, not depth; (c) **no
full `R CMD check`** this session (proportionate — tree unchanged since
S138’s OK gate, doc build-equivalents passed — but named, not assumed);
(d) the README diff carries an **incidental version-date refresh**
beyond the pure de-dup (correct, since the README was genuinely
regenerated today, but it’s a second change in the same file). I reserve
9-10 for a higher-difficulty, single-deliverable session.

### What Session 138 Did

**Deliverable:** owner pick **(option 2 — the real fix for issue \#53)**
— rename the malformed sire column from `si.re` (period) / `si re`
(space) to **`sire.id`** across the 6 example pedigree datasets + sweep
dependent source/docs/tests, and remove the now-unneeded `si`
`inst/WORDLIST` whitelist (S137’s tentative resolution, **superseded**).
**(DONE.)** Full **strict TDD** RED→GREEN→REFACTOR→VERIFY with a
phase-gate `AskUserQuestion` at every transition. **Target name
`sire.id` (owner-chosen):** a period in a column name is a realistic
“inexperienced data provider” messy header, so the fix KEEPS a
period-bearing header under test (rather than removing periods) — but in
a sensible place that tokenizes to real words (`sire`+`id`) so it no
longer spell-flags. Verified: `fixColumnNames("sire.id")` → `sire`,
exercising MORE normalization than `si.re` (periodRemoved + sireIdToSire
vs periodRemoved only). **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-19 **Status:**
**DONE. Strict TDD** (declared every response). **Gated
`AskUserQuestion`s (4):** PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR (all
“full scope”), plus the pre-RED owner confirmation of the target name.
**0 stakeholder corrections.** Builds directly on S137’s audit (issue
\#53) — this is the deferred real fix the owner chose to do now instead
of S137’s whitelist. New session within the conversation (claimed it, 1B
stub BEFORE technical work). - **RED:** new
`tests/testthat/test_exampleData_columnNames.R` — (1) data contract
pinning all 6 datasets’ exact names to include `sire.id`; (2)
malformed-name-absent check; (3) a normalization-invariant guard
(`fixColumnNames` → canonical). Confirmed RED: tests 1+2 = 12 assertions
FAIL (names held `si.re`/`si re`), guard = 6 PASS, 0 errors. - **GREEN
(minimum to pass):** regenerated the 6 `.RData` —
`load → rename the si.re/si re column to sire.id → save(compress="gzip")`,
with a `stopifnot(identical(new, old))` proving **values
byte-identical** (only the name moved); nrow preserved. Re-ran → **18/18
green, 0 error.** (createPedOne.R deliberately left for REFACTOR — the
test reads the shipped `.RData`, not the generator.) - **REFACTOR
(behavior-neutral):** `R/createPedOne.R:17` `` `si re` `` → `sire.id`;
`si.re` → `sire.id` in the 5 `R/data.R` roxygen blocks (replace_all,
exactly 5 hits) →
[`roxygen2::roxygenise()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/roxygenize.html)
regenerated **exactly** the 5 `man/*.Rd` (NAMESPACE untouched, `si.re`
gone from `man/`); **removed the `si` line from `inst/WORDLIST`**;
updated the website-only vignette prose
`vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:91`. - **VERIFY:** full
clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (866 blocks incl. app/e2e).
**One necessitated test fix** surfaced by the suite (NOT by the
pre-flight grep): `test_summary.nprcgenekeeprErr.R:23` asserted the
summary change-log has **9** newlines; `sire.id` adds the `sireIdToSire`
line → **10** (`9L`→`10L`, with a comment). Verified the new output is
correct (the extra line is a real normalization step), then re-ran →
0/0. **`R CMD check` (`--no-manual --ignore-vignettes`) → `Status: OK`**
(0/0/0). `spell_check_package()` → only the unrelated `names’`
(`NEWS.md:51`). Installed the dev package + ran the vignette’s
`qcStudbook(pedGood, reportChanges=TRUE)` chunk → correct richer
change-log (`periodRemoved: "sire.id to sireid"` +
`sireIdToSire: "sireid to sire"`). → **Learning 131.**

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** This changes
runtime-consumed data, so I exercised the real path: `R CMD INSTALL` the
dev package with the regenerated data (loads OK), then ran
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
on the new `pedGood` (the vignette’s actual chunk) → correct output;
plus the full testthat suite (which runs qcStudbook on every renamed

regenerates at the next site build, so there is no committed artifact to

**Self-assessment (Session 138): 9/10.** **Headline strengths:** (1)
**clean strict-TDD execution** — RED proven failing for the right
reason, GREEN minimal (data only) with a byte-identical-values proof,
REFACTOR behavior-neutral, all four phase gates posed as

check in the regen, not just trusted); (4) **VERIFY caught the
output-shape test the pre-flight grep missed** — and I verified the new
value was *correct* before bumping it, not just made it pass; (5) **real
build-equivalent** (`R CMD check` Status: OK, not just the test read) +
runtime smoke (install + run the vignette chunk); (6) confined the diff,
regenerated `man/` cleanly, removed the now-dead whitelist, left the
unrelated `names’` flag alone; (7) captured Learning 131. **Weaknesses
(honest):** (a) my **pre-flight breakage scan under-predicted** — it
said “no test breaks” but one did (output-shape); caught in VERIFY (no
shipped defect), but a cleaner first pass would have run the suite
before claiming no breakage (the lesson of 131); (b) **did not re-render
the quarto vignette** — correct call (the freeze cache is git-ignored
and auto-regenerates; re-rendering produces no committed change), but it
means the *local* freeze cache is momentarily stale until the next site
build; (c) **moderate, not high, difficulty** — a careful rename +
sweep. I reserve 10 for a first pass whose own breakage prediction is
complete.

—

use `si.re` (period) and **`pedOne` uses `si re` (space)** — the carried

**cross-checked the adversary’s own corrections firsthand** (it cited

**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-19 **Status:** **DONE.
AUDIT/VERIFICATION phase** (declared every response; no
RED→GREEN→REFACTOR — the only shipped-content change is the
gate-invariant `inst/WORDLIST`, consumed only by the
`error=FALSE`+`skip_on_cran` `tests/spelling.R`; `R CMD check` never
reads it, so it is the same class S134 made without a TDD cycle). **No
gated `AskUserQuestion`** (no RED→GREEN→REFACTOR transition occurred).
**0 stakeholder corrections** — the owner’s WORDLIST direction was a
scope *decision*, not a correction of an error; I surfaced the honest
trade-off before executing. Right-sized **fan-out** under ultracode: the
“is it a bug / what’s the right name” question genuinely benefited from
independent perspectives + an adversarial pass (and the adversary caught
a real synthesis overreach), but the highest-value step was
**cross-checking the adversary firsthand** — neither agent’s single
“correct name” survived the source read. Reclaimed the **S137** number
from an abandoned same-numbered owner-run-Phase5 stub (CRAN is
owner-blocked; that stub had no committed work). - **🚨 REAL WORK \#1 —
VERIFIED THE DEFECT FIRSTHAND + CORRECTED THE INHERITED COUNT:** a
[`names()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/names.html) scan of every
`data/*.RData` found the malformed sire column in **6** datasets, not 5
— the five `si.re` (period) datasets **plus** `pedOne`’s `si re` (space,
generated by `R/createPedOne.R:17`). Grep confirmed the malformed token
lives ONLY in `R/data.R` roxygen + generated `man/*.Rd` + one
website-only vignette — nowhere in `R/` logic or `tests/`. - **🚨 REAL
WORK \#2 — PROVED IT IS NOT FUNCTIONALLY BROKEN (empirically):**
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html) +
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)
→ `si.re`, `si re`, `sire_id`, `Sire_ID`, `Sire Id`, `sire` **all
normalize to `sire`**; `ego_id,si.re,dam_id,sex,birth_date` →
`id,sire,dam,sex,birth`. So
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
(`R/qcStudbook.R:174`) accepts every form; canonical schema is
`c("id","sire","dam","sex","birth")` (`R/getRequiredCols.R:24`). The
defect is **internal inconsistency**, NOT a break — not a CRAN blocker.
`.Rbuildignore:60` (`^vignettes/articles$`) confirms the
`studbook-quality-control.qmd` reference is website-only, not
check-gated. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#3 — CROSS-CHECKED THE ADVERSARIAL PASS
(→ Learning 130):** the synthesis recommended target `sire_id`; the
adversary correctly struck the synthesis’s false “period-header
contradicts the value-level period rule” argument (headers/values are
separate code paths) BUT argued the target should be the space form
`si re`, citing the `fixColumnNames` `@example` — which I read firsthand
and found uses **`Sire_ID`** (underscore), undercutting the adversary’s
own evidence. The repo ships **three** messy-sire conventions (`si re`
space in `createPedOne`+~8 local fixtures; `Sire_ID` underscore in the
`@example`; `si.re` period in the 5 `.RData`) and the period form
matches none. So the issue **frames the choice** (`si re` / `sire_id` /
`sire`, with trade-offs) for the owner rather than asserting one. - **🚨
REAL WORK \#4 — OWNER’S LEAST-DISRUPTIVE RESOLUTION, EXECUTED WITH
BEFORE/AFTER VERIFICATION:**
[`spelling::spell_check_package()`](https://docs.ropensci.org/spelling//reference/spell_check_package.html)
before = 2 flags (`si` in 5 `man/*.Rd`; `names’` in `NEWS.md:51`). Added
`si` to `inst/WORDLIST` in correct C-locale position (between
`sexRatioWithAddions` and `simParent`). After = `si` cleared; only the
**unrelated** `names’` remains — left untouched (separate item; its real
fix is the NEWS curly-quote, not a whitelist). This **silences** the
symptom; the malformed names stay in the shipped data, now tracked by
**\#53** (low priority). Deliberately reverses S134’s “leave `si`

behind — push before R-hub”). `git status` “up to date” only compares
the LOCAL tracking ref (updated only on fetch), so it is ambiguous
between “already pushed” and “stale view.” A `git fetch` (succeeded —
network works here) then
`git rev-list --left-right --count origin/add-methodology...HEAD` →
**`0 / 0`**, `origin/add-methodology` = local HEAD `24175785`. So the
remote already carries S133 `withr` (`b93a5b4c`) + S134 (`56b66ae0`) +
S135 (`24175785`) — **the push prerequisite is done; R-hub will check
the correct 2.0.0 tree with no `git push`.** S135 verified the “behind”
state *correctly at its time*; the branch was pushed afterward, so the
(accurate-then) instruction had gone stale — captured as Learning 129 (a
verified remote-state claim still expires; re-`fetch` before acting). -

false-flagged `devtools` (only in `#'` comments) and `shinytest2.R` (a
filename in a comment). Rewrote it to use `getParseData(parse(f))`
`SYMBOL_PACKAGE` tokens — comment/string/filename-immune, exactly what
the parser resolves `pkg::` to — yielding precisely the one real
offender (`withr`), matching the check. - **RED:** `_s133_red.R`

set `CITATION.cff` version (**stale at 1.0.7**) → `2.0.0` + new
`date-released: '2026-06-18'`; ran the two version-dependent tests.

Phase 3 — the package is now **version-consistent at 2.0.0** (resolving
the intentional Phase-3a NEWS-says-2.0.0 / DESCRIPTION-says-1.1.0.9000
mismatch). **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-18 **Status:** **DONE. Full
strict TDD (declared every response).** **Gated `AskUserQuestion`s:**
(1) pre-RED **scope** — version-consistency only vs +permanent guard
test (owner chose **version-consistency only**); (1b, same call) the
carried maintainer-judgment **NEWS Minor→Major** promotion (owner chose

**GREEN→REFACTOR** declared **N/A** (mechanical metadata bump, no
structure). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode:
**SOLO** — three mechanical edits; the value is the disciplined TDD
framing of a no-new-behavior change, verifying the §3.1 inventory
against the live tree, the litter-free render, and the whole-tree
dangling-pointer sweep, not depth. New session within the conversation:
claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - **🚨 REAL WORK \#1 — RED
BOUND TO THE VERSION-CONSISTENCY INVARIANT, not a behavior (→ Learning
124):** a version bump has no new behavior, and both version-dependent
tests are *dynamic* (read
[`packageVersion()`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/packageDescription.html)),
so the bump makes nothing go red-then-green. The honest RED surface is
the *invariant the change must establish*: `_s131_red.R` parsed
`DESCRIPTION` (`read.dcf`), `CITATION.cff` (`version:` line), and
`README.md` (rendered version line) and asserted all three == `2.0.0`,
the stale `1.1.0.9000` / `1.0.7` are gone, and CITATION has a
`date-released` — **7 assertions, 7 failures** on the current tree,
**0** after the edits + re-render. (Metadata-bump cousin of RED-binding
to a rendered `NEWS.md` / `man/.Rd`.) - **🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — VERIFIED
THE PLAN’S §3.1 INVENTORY AGAINST THE LIVE TREE before editing:** a
`git grep` confirmed exactly three bumpable hits (`DESCRIPTION:4`,
`README.md:8`, `CITATION.cff:11`) and nothing hidden in
`R/`/`man/`/`tests/`/`data-raw/`/`inst/` (no `inst/CITATION`).
`README.Rmd:10` + the `appUI` About panel are on the §3.1 “NO edit —
auto-tracks
[`getVersion()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getVersion.md)”
list and were correctly left. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#3 — LITTER-FREE
RENDER + CONFINED DIFF:** re-rendered via
`rmarkdown::render(output_format = github_document(html_preview = FALSE))`
(suppresses the `NEWS.html`-style preview litter at the source,
improving on Learning 123’s clean-up-after). Snapshotted `git status`
before/after: the ONLY new modified file was `README.md`, and its diff
is confined to the single version line (`1.1.0.9000 (2026-06-01)` →
`2.0.0 (2026-06-17)`) — no figures, no `README.html`. - **🚨 REAL WORK
\#4 — WHOLE-TREE SWEEP FOUND DANGLING POINTERS the retitle left:** a
closing `git grep` for the old strings found, *out of the locked scope*,

Control* vignette’s `pedGood` column description at
`vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.qmd:90-91` (carried from
S116). **(DONE — reworded the lead-in from “The shipped `pedGood` data
set has deliberately messy headers (`ego_id`, `si.re`, `dam_id`,
`birth_date`):” to “…has five columns – four with deliberately messy
headers (`ego_id`, `si.re`, `dam_id`, `birth_date`) and an
already-canonical `sex` that QC leaves untouched:”. A single 3-line
prose edit.)** Website-only article (`vignettes/articles/` is
`.Rbuildignore`d) — **no
`R/`/`man/`/`NAMESPACE`/`DESCRIPTION`/`NEWS`/`data/`/test/CRAN-vignette
change.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-18 **Status:** **DONE. Full
strict TDD (declared every response).** **Two gated `AskUserQuestion`s
for the genuine fork + the mandatory transitions:** (1) pre-RED
**approach** — rewrite the lead-in vs append a clause (owner chose
**rewrite the lead-in**); (2) **PRE-RED→RED**; (3) **RED→GREEN**; (4)
**GREEN→REFACTOR** declared **N/A** (prose content, structure
preserved). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode:
**SOLO** — a 3-line prose fix; the value is catching that the carried
“omits sex” framing was imprecise, binding the RED probe to the live
object, and rendering to verify the prose now matches the chunk. New
session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical
work). - **🚨 REAL WORK \#1 — THE CARRIED ONE-LINER WOULD HAVE PRODUCED
A WRONG FIX (→ Learning 122):** the carried nit said “omits the `sex`
column,” which reads as “add `sex` to the parenthetical.” A probe on the
live object refuted that literal fix: `names(pedGood)` = `ego_id`,
`si.re`, `dam_id`, **`sex`**, `birth_date`, and the article’s own
`changes` chunk renames exactly the FOUR non-`sex` columns — `sex` is
**absent from `changedCols`** because it already uses the canonical
name. Adding `sex` to the “deliberately messy headers” list would have
mislabeled it AND contradicted the chunk’s rendered output two lines
below. The accurate fix conveys “five columns: four messy + an
already-clean `sex`.” (S116’s richer framing “the dataset is 5 columns”
was buried ~350 lines down in the notes; the compressed carried
one-liner lost it.) - **🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — BOUND THE RED PROBE TO THE
LIVE OBJECT + SOURCE `.qmd` (Learning 122):** a vignette has no
generated `man/.Rd` to bind to (unlike Learnings 119/120). The probe
parses the lead-in paragraph from the `.qmd` and asserts 3 ground-truth
**preconditions** (`names(pedGood)` has 5 cols incl. `sex`; the 4 listed
headers == pedGood’s non-`sex` columns) + 2 **RED assertions** (lead-in
mentions `sex`; states “five”) → **2 failures / 3 passing** on current
prose; **0 failures (all 5 pass)** after the edit. Binding to
`names(pedGood)` means the test fails if the data ever diverges from the
prose. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#3 — RAN THE REAL BUILD-EQUIVALENT (render) AND
CLEANED ITS LITTER:**
`pkgdown::build_article("articles/studbook-quality-control")` rendered
clean; verified in the HTML that (a) the new “has five columns – four
with deliberately messy headers” sentence is present, and (b)
`changedCols` still shows exactly the 4 renames with **no `sex to X`**
(prose now matches what the chunk demonstrates). The render writes HTML
to the **gitignored** `pkgdown_site/` but LITTERED the tracked tree with
`pkgdown/` (favicons),
`vignettes/articles/studbook-quality-control.rmarkdown` (Quarto
intermediate), and an auto `vignettes/articles/.gitignore` — snapshotted

back to the intended set. - **RED:** `_s129_red.R` (parse the `.qmd`

**bound the RED probe to the live object** (`names(pedGood)`) + the
source paragraph, the strongest binding available for a vignette (no
generated `man/.Rd`); (3) **ran the real build-equivalent** (pkgdown
render) and verified BOTH the new prose AND the consistency of the
executed `changes` chunk (no `sex` rename), not just that it compiled;
(4) **found and cleaned the render’s tracked-tree litter** (`pkgdown/`,
`*.rmarkdown`, auto `.gitignore`) by snapshotting `git status`
before/after; (5) **confined the diff to one `.qmd`**; (6) **correctly
classified Phase-3E as N/A** and said so explicitly (not FM \#24); (7)
file-based probes from command \#1, no shell-backslash misfire; 0
corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all
temp probes. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) the pkgdown render littered
the tree and I had to discover + clean it mid-flight — rendering to an
isolated temp dir up front would have avoided the extra step (now
captured as Learning 122 for the next session); (b) full `R CMD check`
and full testthat NOT run — proportionate (no code/test/data change; the
article is `.Rbuildignore`d), but it means the verification is the
render + probe, not a full check; (c) **very low technical difficulty**
— a 3-line prose edit; the value is the correctness-nuance catch, the
live-object-bound probe, and the render verification, not depth. I
reserve 10 for a harder delivery that is also full-`check`-verified.

### What Session 125 Did

### What Session 125 Did

**Deliverable:** owner’s pick **(A9)** — hardened
[`addGenotype()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addGenotype.md)
so its integer allele encoding is **consistent regardless of whether the
two allele columns arrive as character or factor**. **(DONE — coerced
both allele columns to character at the top of the function; factor
input now produces output identical to character input, verified on the
real bundled `rhesusGenotypes`: 35-allele dictionary codes 10001–10035,
max distinct codes per allele = 1.)** This **realizes the latent code
fragility S124 surfaced and flagged** (Learning 117):
`genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` / `[, 3L]` indexes a name-keyed integer
vector by a factor → R uses the factor’s INTEGER CODES not its labels →
encoding inconsistent between the two columns (and between callers).
First **CODE fix** in the S118–S125 run (S118–S122 were doc/prose;
S123–S124 were DATA). Scoped to **`R/addGenotype.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R` + `man/addGenotype.Rd`**; **no
`NAMESPACE`/`DESCRIPTION`/`NEWS`/`data/`/`checkGenotypeFile.R` change**
(`NAMESPACE` proven byte-identical). **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17
**Status:** **DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response).** **Two
gated `AskUserQuestion`s for the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory
transitions:** (1) pre-RED **scope** — addGenotype-only vs
also-harden-checkGenotypeFile (owner chose **addGenotype-only** /
point-of-use); (2) **PRE-RED→RED**; (3) **RED→GREEN**; (4)
**GREEN→REFACTOR**. The coerce-at-top-vs-coerce-at-index implementation
detail was ground-truth-determined (coerce-at-top is strictly more
robust — it also fixes the version-fragile
[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)-on-factors dict build) →
presented as the gate baseline, NOT re-gated (Learning 113). **0
stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only
**Explore breadth scan** to confirm scope (callers / sibling-pattern /
checkGenotypeFile behavior) with **all load-bearing claims verified
firsthand**, then **SOLO** TDD (the fix is a deterministic 2-line
coercion — a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within
the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - **🚨
REAL WORK \#1 — THE SCOPE DECISION TURNED ON THE CALL GRAPH, NOT THE DOC
(→ Learning 118):** `addGenotype`’s roxygen says genotype “is to be
provided by `checkGenotypeFile` so it is not checked” — which reads as
“harden the gate.” The breadth scan + firsthand reads refuted that as
the *complete* answer: `addGenotype` has **DIRECT callers that bypass
`checkGenotypeFile`** (the roxygen example passing `rhesusGenotypes`,
`test_addGenotype.R:17`, `test_geneDrop.R:28`), so a gate-only fix
leaves them exposed. The only fix that closes the bug for ALL callers is
at the point of use. Also confirmed: `checkGenotypeFile` is a pure
structural validator (no type coercion), and **no sibling genotype
function shares the `dict[factorColumn]` pattern**
(`getGVGenotype`/`getGenotypes`/`getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes`/`hasGenotype`
are clean) → scope stayed narrow. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — THE RED FIXTURE
HAD TO MAKE PER-COLUMN CODES DIVERGE FROM THE GLOBAL SORT:** the naive
fixture (both columns the same allele set in sorted order) does NOT
reproduce the bug — per-column factor codes coincidentally align with
the global `sort(unique())` positions. The minimal trigger is
`first_name = c("a","b")`, `second_name = c("b","c")` as factors:
globally a→10001, b→10002, c→10003, but the per-column codes (a,b→1,2
and b,c→1,2) make allele **“b” encode as 10002 in `first` but 10001 in
`second`**. Designing it required tracing the exact index arithmetic,
not just “make it a factor.” Character input is the oracle. - **🚨 REAL
WORK \#3 — COERCE AT THE TOP, NOT ONLY AT THE INDEX:** the breadth agent
suggested wrapping only the two lookups
(`genoDict[as.character(genotype[, 2L])]`). That fixes the lookup but
leaves the dict BUILD (`sort(unique(c(col1, col2)))`) relying on
[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)-on-factors (pre-R-4.1 dropped
factor attrs → mis-named dict). Coercing both columns to character once
at the top fixes build AND lookup in one place, version-robustly. -
**RED:** two new tests in `test_addGenotype.R` — (1) factor-input output
identical to character-input output (`expect_identical` on `$first` and
`$second`); (2) allele “b” gets the same code as `first` and as `second`
— confirmed **2 failed / 0 error** on the unhardened function; the
existing character-input test stayed green (4/0). **GREEN:** the 2-line
coercion at the top of `addGenotype`; all 3 tests pass; full suite 0/0.
**REFACTOR:** added a one-line `@details` roxygen note documenting the
internal coercion; regenerated `man/addGenotype.Rd` (rd roclet only →
NAMESPACE untouched). - **Used file-based R probes from the first
command** — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings
108/109); no misfires. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** dev-process history
→ CHANGELOG here. This change **SHIPS** and is **user-facing**
(`addGenotype` output changes for any factor-columned input) → fold into
the **CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0** (flagged, NOT edited now).
**NO auto-memory** — the work lives in the repo
(\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
committed ONLY the repo files; excluded `.DS_Store` (modified) +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep); the four
`_s125_*.R` probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no
`Rplots.pdf`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior
change.** “Suite passes” was necessary but insufficient (the fix changes
`addGenotype` output for factor input). Ran
`addGenotype(rhesusPedigree, rhesusGenotypes)` on a **factor-coerced
copy of the real bundled object** (375×12, 31 genotype rows):
`first`/`second` came out **identical** to the character path; the
combined dictionary is the full **35 alleles (codes 10001–10035)**;
**max distinct codes per allele = 1** (consistent encoding). Computed
the consistency property at the **genotype-row level**
(`gr <- !is.na(first_name)`, aligned `al`/`co` vectors) to avoid the
S124 short-logical-recycling trap (gotcha \#11). Full `R CMD check` not
run (`devtools` absent) — proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 +
load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the
factor-vs-character smoke on the real object.

**Session 124 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 125): Score 9/10.** S124
pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as **(A9)** and made it trivial to
execute: it gave the exact bug mechanism (`genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]`
indexes by integer codes not labels), the exact fix location (“coerce
the allele columns to character inside `addGenotype`”), the exact test
design hint (“feed a factor-columned genotype, assert consistent
name-based codes”), and the exact numbers to expect (combined dict 35
alleles, codes 10001–10035 vs the buggy per-column 18/23). Every carried
gotcha held: the shell-backslash→file-probe trap (I used file probes
from command \#1, no misfires), the **consistency-check trap gotcha
\#11** (verify at the genotype-row level, NOT by indexing the 375-row
output with a 31-row logical — S124 hit this itself and documented it; I
followed the warning and avoided the round-trip), the standing
`.DS_Store`/`PED_GV_AUDIT` keep-call, and the branch state (5 ahead of
`origin/add-methodology`). ROI very high; no false credit; the bug
characterization was accurate firsthand. **The −1:** S124’s A9 wording
offered the fix location as “inside `addGenotype` (or in
`checkGenotypeFile`)” without noting that **direct callers bypass
`checkGenotypeFile`**, so the “or checkGenotypeFile” branch — taken
literally — would NOT have fully closed the bug. It listed `addGenotype`
first (correct lead), but I had to run the breadth scan to confirm the
gate-only path was insufficient. A perfect handoff would have flagged
the direct-caller asymmetry.

**Self-assessment (Session 125): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14
issues, ghost-check → HEAD `9b713206` = S124, clean), reported, STOPPED
for the owner’s pick; claimed the session (1B stub BEFORE technical
work). **Headline strengths:** (1) **the scope decision turned on the
call graph, not the doc contract** — proved (via breadth scan +
firsthand reads) that direct callers bypass `checkGenotypeFile`, so the
point-of-use fix is the only complete one, and that the bug is isolated
(no sibling pattern) so scope stayed narrow; (2) **designed a RED
fixture that actually reproduces the bug** — traced the index arithmetic
to make per-column codes diverge from the global sort (the naive fixture
would have passed even unfixed); (3) **coerced at the source, not just
the buggy line** — also neutralized the version-fragile
[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)-on-factors dict build the breadth
agent’s patch would have left; (4) **verified everything firsthand**
(bug mechanism, caller types, checkGenotypeFile behavior) — used the
Explore scan for completeness but re-derived its load-bearing claims;
(5) **real Phase-3E smoke on the actual bundled object**
(factor-coerced) with the consistency property computed correctly at the
genotype-row level — sidestepped the S124 gotcha \#11; (6) **proved zero
regressions** (3/3 tests, full suite 0/0, checkRd 0, NAMESPACE
byte-identical, diff confined to 3 files); (7) correct TDD discipline
(declared every response; gated the genuine scope fork + the 3
transitions; did not re-gate the ground-truth-determined coerce-at-top
detail — Learning 113); (8) file-based probes from command \#1; 0
corrections; plain language; ASCII-only question options; removed all
temp probes. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) full `R CMD check` NOT run
(`devtools` absent) — the checkRd + load_all + suite + NAMESPACE-diff +
smoke is the proportionate build-equivalent, not the full check; (b) low
technical difficulty — the fix is 2 lines; the value is the scope
analysis, the reproducing fixture, and the realistic smoke, not depth;
(c) I did not add an explicit NA-with-factor RED case (the coercion is
NA-safe — `as.character(factor-with-NA)` preserves NA, and the character
NA path is already covered by `test_geneDrop.R` — but a dedicated
assertion would have made it airtight; owner-approved RED scope was the
two tests). I reserve 10 for a delivery that is also
full-`check`-verified.

**DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response).** **Four gated
`AskUserQuestion`s:** (1) pre-RED **scope** — id-only vs
all-three-columns (owner chose **all three** — full type-correctness);
(2) **PRE-RED→RED**; (3) **RED→GREEN**; (4) **GREEN→REFACTOR**. One
genuine fork (scope) + the 3 mandatory transitions; coerce-in-place and
the `data-raw/` convention were
ground-truth/established-pattern-determined → not re-gated (Learnings
113/116). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode: a
read-only **3-prong investigation workflow** (provenance / dependents /
test-patterns) to frame scope, with **all key claims verified
firsthand** before authoring; then **SOLO** coercion + oracle
verification (the work is a deterministic coercion — a fan-out for the
edit would be theater). New session within the conversation: claimed it
(1B stub BEFORE technical work). - **🚨 REAL WORK \#1 — THE FIRSTHAND
INVESTIGATION REFRAMED A7 (→ Learning 117):** S123 flagged A7 as
“de-factor `id`”. Probing showed **three** factor columns, and that `id`
is the column that does NOT matter — `merge(ped, genotype, by="id")`
coerces it either way and every consumer is id-agnostic (independently
confirmed by the dependents scan). The columns that matter are the two
**allele** columns:
[`addGenotype()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addGenotype.md)’s
`genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` indexes a name-keyed vector by a factor → R
uses the factor’s INTEGER CODES not its labels → an **inconsistent**
encoding (same allele → different code in `first` vs `second`).
Character columns index by name → **consistent** (verified: same
allele→same code = TRUE for character / FALSE for factor; combined dict
35 alleles, codes 10001–10035 vs the buggy per-column 10001–10018 /
10001–10023). The package’s own `test_addGenotype.R` already feeds
`stringsAsFactors=FALSE` — the shipped factor object was the anomaly. So
scope was a **correctness** decision, not cosmetic; gated it; owner
chose all three. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — PROVENANCE confirmed
COERCE-not-REGENERATE:** `data/rhesusGenotypes.RData` has a single 2020
commit (`31c4679d`), no scripted generator, shares all 31 obfuscated ids
with `rhesusPedigree` (re-deriving via non-deterministic
[`obfuscatePed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/obfuscatePed.md)
would change ids + desync the sibling). New idempotent
`data-raw/rhesusGenotypes.R` coerces the three columns and re-saves in
place (`compress="xz"`, 734→608 B). The value-identical
`inst/extdata/obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv` is an
independent cross-check (all 3 columns identical). - **🚨 REAL WORK \#3
— BUNDLED doc + man with the data change:** reverted `R/data.R:347` id
doc `factor`→`character` (first_name/second_name make no type claim,
untouched) and regenerated `man/rhesusGenotypes.Rd` (1-line diff) in the
SAME deliverable so the data-doc-mismatch class (S114–S122) stays
closed. - **RED:** `test_rhesusGenotypes.R` (22 assertions pinning types
AND value-preservation — dim 31×3, names, unique 31/18/23, NA 0,

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior
change.** “Suite passes” was necessary but insufficient (the data change
alters `addGenotype` output). Ran

obfuscated from `inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` via
[`obfuscatePed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/obfuscatePed.md),
hand-saved 2020-02-02 (commit 31c4679d), never scripted/seeded.
Obfuscation is non-deterministic, so re-deriving from the CSV would
produce **different** obfuscated ids/dates than the shipped object (and
desync sibling objects sharing those ids). The committed
`data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R` therefore **loads the existing object and
coerces its TYPES while preserving all VALUES** (idempotent), rather
than regenerating. “Re-export” for an opaque artifact = value-preserving
type coercion, not regeneration. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — INVESTIGATED
BEFORE GATING to PRUNE UNSAFE OPTIONS (→ Learning 116):** two
plausible-sounding options were eliminated before the owner ever saw
them — “re-derive from CSV” (non-deterministic) and “drop `exit`”
(`getPotentialParents.R:68-76,114` reads `ba$exit`; dropping breaks the
object’s main consumer). Verified firsthand that **no test asserts the
current factor/logical types** (grep: none) so the fix breaks nothing.
The scope gate offered only safe choices. - **🚨 REAL WORK \#3 — BUNDLED
the doc + man with the data change:** changing the object’s types
without updating its `R/data.R` doc + regenerating `man/` in the SAME
session would have reopened the exact data-doc-mismatch class S114–S122
spent eight sessions closing. So one coherent deliverable: `.rda` +
`data-raw/` + doc reversal (id `factor`→`character`; birth
`factor of birth-date strings`→`Date vector`; exit `logical`→`Date`) +
`man/` regen + tests + `.Rbuildignore`. - **RED:**
`test_rhesusPedigree.R` (31 assertions pinning **types AND
value-preservation** — dim, id-all-unique, sire/dam 124 NA, birth
range + BRI2MW 1998-12-06, exit all-NA), confirmed **failing** on the
degraded object (id/sire/dam integer-factors; birth factor — `min`
errored “not meaningful for factors”; exit not S3). **GREEN:** ran the
coercion (file 7449→4340 B via xz), updated the 3 doc lines, regenerated
`man/rhesusPedigree.Rd` (3-line diff). **REFACTOR:** N/A (own code
clean) — the now-redundant `as.character`/`as.Date` no-ops in
`test_getPotentialParents.R`/`test_modPotentialParents.R` were
**flagged, not removed** (adjacent scope, FM \#8; owner chose skip). -
**Used file-based R probes from the first command** — dodged the
recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109); no misfires. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]:** dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. This
change **SHIPS** and is **user-facing** (column types changed) → fold
into the **CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0** (flagged, NOT edited
now; more NEWS-worthy than the S114–S122 prose fixes). **NO
auto-memory** — the work lives in the repo
(\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
committed ONLY the repo files; excluded `.DS_Store` (modified) +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep); the seven
`_s123_*.R` probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no
`Rplots.pdf`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE — this IS a runtime-behavior
change.** “Suite passes” was necessary but insufficient (the
potential-parents tests do their own `as.character`/`as.Date`, now
no-ops, which could mask a still-wrong type). The conclusive test ran
**[`getPotentialParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPotentialParents.md)
on the SHIPPED corrected object directly, with no conversions** (`birth`
is `Date`, `id` is character) → ran cleanly, 50-element result; the
suite’s positional dam/sire assertions pass. (Note:
[`getPotentialParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPotentialParents.md)
returns an **unnamed positional list** — the tests index by position,
e.g. `dams_1`=element 1, `dams_4`=element 4 — so `names(result)` are NOT
focal ids; an early naive spot-check on that was reconciled, not a
defect.) Full `R CMD check` not run (`devtools` absent) — proportionate
equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all 162 + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE
byte-diff + the consumer smoke test.

**Session 122 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 123): Score 9/10.** S122
pre-named THIS deliverable precisely as option **(A6)**, characterized

`exit` all-NA logical, id/sire/dam/sex factors), and correctly flagged
it as a **DATA change touching `data/` + tests under strict TDD — a
separate, larger deliverable than a doc fix** (which is exactly how it
played out). Every carried gotcha held: the near-identical
`examplePedigree`/`rhesusPedigree` describe blocks (I anchored the id
edit on the following `sire` line so I wouldn’t hit `rhesusGenotypes`’s
identical id line at R/data.R:347), the `roxygenise(roclets="rd")` regen

(“so `birth` is a real `Date` and **`exit` holds real dates** (or is
**dropped**)”) contained one infeasible sub-option (there are NO exit
dates in the source — `exit` is all-NA there too) and one unsafe
sub-option (dropping breaks `getPotentialParents`). Right about the bug;
slightly off on the proposed remedies — both resolved by this session’s
investigation.

**Gotchas:** (1) **`data-raw/` pattern is now established.** Data
objects are stored as **`.RData`** (not `.rda`); re-export by
overwriting in place — `save(obj, file="data/X.RData", compress="xz")` —
and **do NOT use `usethis::use_data`** (it writes a lowercase `.rda`,
creating a DUPLICATE). `data-raw/` is build-ignored (`^data-raw$`). (2)
**`data-raw/rhesusPedigree.R` is idempotent** — re-running it on the
already-corrected object is a no-op. (3) **`rhesusGenotypes` is the next
opaque `.rda`** (id still factor) — option (A7); shares ids with
`rhesusPedigree` and is merged by
[`addGenotype()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addGenotype.md)
(both-character merge is fine, but verify). (4)
**[`getPotentialParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPotentialParents.md)
returns an UNNAMED positional list** — index by position (`dams_1`=elem
1, `dams_4`=elem 4); `names(result)` are NOT focal ids. (5) **Redundant
no-op conversions remain** in `test_getPotentialParents.R:4-7` +
`test_modPotentialParents.R:120-125` — deferred REFACTOR (A8), safe to
remove. (6) **`si.re`/`si re` are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures** (carried) —
do NOT “clean” them. (7) **Shell backslash trap (recurring)** — write R
probes to a FILE and `Rscript` it. (8) **Markdown IS enabled** in
roxygen — keep prose free of stray markdown. (9) Standing uncommitted:
`.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked) —
owner’s keep-call. (10) **Branch state:** after this commit
`add-methodology` is **4 ahead of `origin/add-methodology`** (S120–S123)
and carries **6 commits not on `origin/master`** (S118–S123) — rides a
future PR \#53 (Learning 112). (11) Carried CRAN gotchas: `NEWS.md` from
`NEWS.Rmd`; renv explicit-snapshot omits Suggests; the package is

### What Session 122 Did

**Deliverable:** owner’s pick **(A5), promoted to the full sweep** — a
one-pass factual-claim audit of **all 24 data docs** in `R/data.R`
against their live objects (dims, column names/order, types, factor
levels, counts, prose), fixing every confirmed discrepancy, then
regenerating the 4 affected `man/` pages. **(DONE — 9 fixes across 4
objects, all oracle-verified: checkRd 0 problems ×4, `NAMESPACE`
byte-identical, `load_all` 162 exports, full-suite regression 0 fail / 0
error, rendered `.Rd` ↔︎ source in sync.)** Scoped to **`R/data.R` + 4
data `man/` pages**; `NAMESPACE`/`DESCRIPTION`/`data/` unchanged.
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17 **Status:** **DONE. TDD phase =
N/A** (roxygen prose — only `#'` comment lines changed, no executable R,
no package logic or test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response,
as S108–S121). **Two gated `AskUserQuestion`s** — (1) **scope** (narrow
2-doc fix vs. full 24-doc audit; owner chose **full**, the deliverable
Learning 114 had proposed); (2) **the 2 data-artifact findings**
(document actual types now vs. leave docs + queue a data fix; owner
chose **document actual types now**). The 7 unambiguous doc-bug fixes
were ground-truth-determined → not separately gated (Learning 113 —
over-gating avoided). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under
ultracode as **oracle ground truth computed once by me + a workflow (5
independent lens scanners for completeness over all 24 docs, then an
adversarial refuter per unique discrepancy)** → **Learning 115.** New
session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical
work). - **The audit (12 candidates → 10 confirmed, 2 rejected):** the
adversarial pass earned its keep both ways — it independently re-derived
and **confirmed** all 10 real mismatches AND **rejected** flagging
`rhesusPedigree` `sire`/`dam` (they make NO type claim — “the
male/female parent … NA for unknown”; “correcting” them to “factor”
would invent a fix). Bounding scope is the skeptic’s symmetric job in a
fix-audit. - **The 9 fixes (each verified firsthand against the live
object before authoring — Learning 109/111):** **`examplePedigree`**
`\item{sex}` `"M","F","U"` → `"F","M","H","U"` (live factor: 4 levels
incl. zero-count `H`); `\item{ancestry}` “character vector or NA …
free-form text” → “factor with levels: INDIAN, CHINESE, HYBRID,
JAPANESE, OTHER, UNKNOWN …” (a closed 6-level factor, not free-form
character). **`rhesusPedigree`** `\item{id}` “character” → “factor”;
`\item{sex}` `"M","F","U"` → `"F","M"` (no `U` level); `\item{birth}`
“Date vector” → “factor of birth-date strings (282 levels)”;
`\item{exit}` “Date vector” → “logical vector, all `NA` (no exit dates
recorded …)”. **`rhesusGenotypes`** `\item{id}` “character” → “factor”.
**`qcBreeders`** `@description` + `@source` “A list of …” → “A character
vector of …” (counts 3 males/26 females confirmed correct). Edited
`rhesusPedigree`’s whole `\describe` block as one chunk because it is
near-identical to `examplePedigree`’s — the shared lines
(`id -- character`, `Date vector of birth/exit`) are **correct in
`examplePedigree`** (id IS character; birth/exit ARE Date) and must NOT
be touched; block-level disambiguation kept the edits surgical. - **🚨
2nd-order finding — DATA artifact (gated, doc fixed, DATA flagged for a
future session):** `rhesusPedigree` ships `birth` as a factor-of-strings
(not Date) and `exit` as **all-375-NA logical** (no exit dates at all) —
the obfuscation degraded these columns. Owner chose to **document the
actual types now** (docs are now accurate to the shipped object), but
the underlying DATA oddity may warrant a re-export (birth → real Date;
exit → real dates or drop the column). That is a **DATA change**
(touches `data/`, tests, TDD) — **flagged as option (A6), NOT done this
session** (FM \#8, “1 and done”). - **Used file-based R probes from the
first command** — dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings
108/109); no misfires. Oracle ground truth derived once by me
(deterministic); the fan-out reasoned over it; R execution kept with the
orchestrator to avoid concurrent-temp-file races. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]:** dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These
help pages **SHIP** → fold S122’s 9 fixes into the **CRAN Phase 3 NEWS
rewrite at 2.0.0** (flagged, NOT edited now). **NO auto-memory** — the
work lives in the repo (\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** committed ONLY the repo files; excluded
`.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked,
standing keep); the eight `_s122_*.R`/`.js` probe/workflow files were
TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no `Rplots.pdf`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped.**

**Gotchas:** (1) **NEW — `rhesusPedigree` ships DEGRADED columns (option
A6):** `birth` is a factor-of-date-strings (282 levels), `exit` is
logical with all 375 values NA, and `id/sire/dam/sex` are factors
(stringsAsFactors-era load). The docs now describe this accurately; if
the owner wants real `Date` birth/exit, that is a `data/` re-export +
test change, not a doc edit. (2) **`examplePedigree` and
`rhesusPedigree` have NEAR-IDENTICAL `\describe` blocks** —
id/sire/dam/sex/gen/birth/exit/age items share text, but
`examplePedigree`’s are CORRECT (id character; birth/exit Date) while
`rhesusPedigree`’s were wrong. When editing either, anchor on a UNIQUE
block-level chunk (e.g. the trailing `}` after `age` for rhesusPedigree;
the `ancestry`/`status`/`recordStatus` tail for examplePedigree) — a
single-line `\item` match will hit the wrong block. Same trap for the
`\item{id}{-- character column of animal IDs}` line (appears at
examplePedigree:20 CORRECT, rhesusGenotypes:347 + rhesusPedigree:361
which were wrong). (3) **`si.re`/`si re` are INTENTIONAL raw fixtures,

### What Session 117 Did

**Deliverable:** owner’s pick **(A2)** — the data-doc short-`@title`

from long “X is a `<type>` …” run-on sentences into short noun-phrase
titles, moving the descriptive detail into `@description`, then
regenerated the 24 data `man/` pages. **(DONE — verified by
[`tools::checkRd()`](https://rdrr.io/r/tools/checkRd.html) 0 problems on
all 24 pages + `NAMESPACE` byte-identical (`git diff --quiet`) +
`load_all` (162 exports) + full-suite regression read (0 fail / 0
error) + a 3-lens adversarial critic panel returning 0 block / 0
should-fix / 3 nits.)** Scoped to **`R/data.R` + 24 data `man/` pages**;
`NAMESPACE`, `DESCRIPTION`, `data/` unchanged. **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-17 **Status:** **DONE. TDD phase = N/A** (roxygen prose; no
executable R line changed — only `#'` comments — so no package logic or
test surface; RED vacuous; declared every response, as S108–S116). **One
gated pre-RED `AskUserQuestion`** (two questions: scope = all 24
vs. worst-8; style = short noun phrase vs. name-led — owner chose **all
24 + short noun phrase**, grounded in a `pedGood` before/after preview).
No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). **0 stakeholder
corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode as **SOLO edits + oracle
(checkRd/load_all/tests) for the structural dimension, PLUS a 3-lens
critic workflow for the NON-oracle quality dimension** (title
aptness/accuracy/consistency — which no oracle can judge) → **Learning
111.** New session within the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE
technical work), did the full Phase 0 reads this same turn. - **What
changed (1 source file, 24 regenerated `man/` pages, 0 new committed
files):** **`R/data.R`** — 24 data-doc blocks restructured: short
noun-phrase title, blank line, then the moved descriptive sentence as
`@description`; `\describe`/`@examples`/`@source`/structure-dumps
preserved byte-for-byte. **`man/` (24 regenerated):**
`exampleNprcgenekeeprConfig`, `examplePedigree`, `finalRpt`,
`focalAnimals`, `lacy1989Ped`, `lacy1989PedAlleles`, `ped1Alleles`,
`pedDuplicateIds`, `pedFemaleSireMaleDam`, `pedGood`, `pedInvalidDates`,
`pedMissingBirth`, `pedOne`, `pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam`, `pedSix`,
`pedWithGenotype`, `pedWithGenotypeReport`, `qcBreeders`, `qcPed`,
`qcPedGvReport`, `smallPed`, `smallPedTree`, `rhesusGenotypes`,
`rhesusPedigree`. **Plus** `CHANGELOG.md` (S117 `[Unreleased]`),
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 111), `SESSION_NOTES.md` (this
handoff). **No `NAMESPACE`, `DESCRIPTION`, NEWS, test, CI-workflow,
CRAN-vignette, or `data/` change.** - **🚨 REAL WORK \#1 — VERIFIED
EVERY COUNT BEFORE AUTHORING IT; FOUND `qcPed` SELF-CONTRADICTS (→
Learning 111):** before moving any “N rows and M columns” sentence into
a description, I checked all 10 count-bearing docs against the loaded
objects. Exactly one was wrong: `qcPed` title said “277 rows and 6
columns” but the object is **280×8** — and roxygen’s auto-`\format`
already read “280 rows and 8 columns”, so the page literally
contradicted itself. Wrote the accurate 280×8 in the new description and
flagged the correction (the S115/S116 verify-and-correct pattern). All 9
others (incl. `ped1Alleles` 554×6, the six error-set peds,
`pedWithGenotype` 280×12) verified accurate, preserved verbatim. - **🚨
REAL WORK \#2 — PAIRED SOLO+ORACLE WITH AN ADVERSARIAL PANEL FOR THE
SUBJECTIVE DIMENSION (→ Learning 111):** `checkRd`/`load_all`/tests
prove the rewrite RENDERS and is structurally clean, but cannot judge
whether a title is APT/accurate/non-duplicative. So I ran a 3-lens
critic workflow (accuracy / completeness / style+consistency), each lens
grounding itself in `git show HEAD:R/data.R` + the loaded objects.
Result **0 block / 0 should-fix / 3 nits**: (a) `examplePedigree`’s
`\describe` documents `\item{recordStats}` but the actual column is
`recordStatus` — PRE-EXISTING (identical in HEAD), in a `\describe`
block I only touched at the title → **flagged, not fixed (FM \#8)**,
verified real against the data first; (b) `smallPed` “Small hypothetical
pedigree (17 animals)” was a confusable near-duplicate of `lacy1989Ped`
“Small hypothetical pedigree (Lacy 1989)” → **applied** the
disambiguation to “Hypothetical 17-animal pedigree” (improving my own
un-previewed draft); (c) “studbook” vs “pedigree” for the 6 QC fixtures
→ **kept studbook** (owner approved it in the style preview; it’s
defensible — studbook input fixtures that represent pedigrees, feeding
`qcStudbook`), noted for future. - **`ped1Alleles` made well-formed:**
two `## Copyright` comment lines interrupted its roxygen block (between
title and `@format`); relocated them above the block so it is
contiguous. Regenerated `.Rd` carries title + `@description` + `@format`
cleanly. - **Used file-based R probes from the first command** — dodged
the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings 108/109) that bit
S112–S114; no misfires this session. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:**
dev-process history → CHANGELOG here. These help pages **SHIP** → fold

from 11 data-doc titles; `R/calcFE.R` `@return` dropped the spurious
`\code{r}` clause (`FE = 1/sum(p^2)` uses only `p`); `R/calcFEFG.R` +
`R/calcFG.R` `@return` closed the unbalanced paren (`sum( (p ^ 2) / r}`
→ `sum( (p ^ 2) / r)`). **`man/` (65 regenerated):** the 62 title
pages + the 3 GV `\value` pages. **Plus** `CHANGELOG.md` (S114

the 51 single-block function titles were de-periodized by a
deterministic script run in **dry-run first** — it printed every title
paragraph + its before/after, all 51 audited before any write (each
correctly found the title = first roxygen paragraph, incl. the 12
multi-line titles). Each function file then changed **exactly one line**
(51 insertions / 51 deletions — no encoding rewrite). `R/data.R` (11
titles) + the 3 `@return` blocks were hand-edited from a fresh read.
Verification was an **oracle**: a per-page `checkRd` comparison of all
**65** changed pages vs their HEAD versions (normalize line numbers,
set-diff messages) proved **62 pages had ONLY
`\title should not end in a period` removed and 0 pages gained any new
problem**; the 3 GV pages changed `\value` prose with no `checkRd`
impact. Title-period NOTEs package-wide **62 → 0**.
[`pkgload::load_all()`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
OK (**162 exports** — NAMESPACE consistent). No stray `Rplots.pdf`. -
**🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — RE-VERIFIED EVERY INHERITED FLAG (→ Learning
105/106):** the 62 count matched S113’s claim exactly; `calcFE`’s
spurious `r` and the `calcFEFG`/`calcFG` unbalanced paren both confirmed
real against source before editing. None actioned blindly. - **The
recurring shell backslash trap (bit me TWICE early):**
`Rscript -e '... "\\.Rd$" ...'` and `grep("^\\\\title", ...)` get
mangled through the Bash layer (a backslash level stripped →
`'\.' is an unrecognized escape` / a `\t` tab matching nothing).
Switched to **file-based R probes** (Write tool, run `Rscript file.R`) +
backslash-free constructs (`endsWith`, `[.]`, `fixed=TRUE`,
`[[:space:]]`). S112/S113 hit variants — reach for the file-based probe
FIRST. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** dev-process history → CHANGELOG
here. These help pages **SHIP** → fold the title/return corrections into
the **CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0** (flagged, NOT edited now).

commit ONLY the repo files; exclude `.DS_Store` (modified) +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep); the three
`_s114_*.R` probes are TEMPORARY (removed before commit).

line-by-line; tallied every added line (only the field rename, 24
`data(...)` forms, the appUI rewrap, the `mulatta` line, and the Sharp
author line — no surprises).
[`tools::checkRd()`](https://rdrr.io/r/tools/checkRd.html) on **all 26**
changed pages introduces **zero new problems**: the 11 reported
`\title`-ends-in-period NOTEs are PRE-EXISTING — confirmed identical on
the committed HEAD versions, and **62 of 190** `.Rd` carry it
package-wide (a separate A2 item). Did NOT assume a NOTE on a changed
file was new. - **The rejected alternative (pin 7.3.2):** roxygen2 is
**not in `renv.lock`** (dev-only dep, not snapshotted), so pinning needs
a manual `renv::install(roxygen2@7.3.2)` that does NOT survive
[`renv::restore`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html)
— more work, not durable, and it preserves the typo + missing author.
Adopting needs no lock change. The owner chose adopt. - **Verification
(build-equivalent for a generated-doc change):** complete-diff audit (27

extends 105):** the 4 drifts were code-read claims (3 from S108, 1 from
S110) never confirmed against runtime. An 8-agent workflow (verifier +
adversarial cross-checker per claim) confirmed 3 and **refuted
`getPyramidPlot`**: its `@return` (“the return value of `par('mar')`”)
is correct — the function returns
[`plotrix::pyramid.plot()`](https://plotrix.github.io/plotrix/reference/pyramid.plot.html)’s
value, but `pyramid.plot` ends with `return(oldmar)` where
`oldmar <- par("mar")`, so the returned value *is* a `par("mar")` vector
(verified: returns `c(5.1,4.1,4.1,2.1)`). Trusting the flag would have
replaced a correct `@return` with a wrong one. The cross-check also
surfaced same-defect instances beyond scope (the sibling `p`
descriptions; a 2nd `orderReport` bullet). - **🚨 REAL WORK \#2 — THE
roxygen VERSION-MISMATCH BLAST-RADIUS TRAP (→ Learning 106):**
[`roxygen2::roxygenise()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/roxygenize.html)
rewrote **~30** `.Rd` files + changed `DESCRIPTION` — far beyond my 4
functions — because committed `man/` is roxygen **7.3.2**
(`RoxygenNote: 7.3.2`) but the dev lib is **8.0.0** (reformats every
page; migrates the field to `Config/roxygen2/version:`). I
`git checkout -- man/ DESCRIPTION` to drop the version artifacts, then
hand-edited the 4 affected `\value` blocks to match source — scoping the
diff to 5 `R/` + 4 `man/`. **General rule: read `git diff --stat` after
any regen; a diff beyond your edits = a tool/version mismatch
reformatting files — don’t commit it as part of a content fix.** -

[`tools::checkRd()`](https://rdrr.io/r/tools/checkRd.html) clean on all

### What Session 111 Did

**Deliverable:** the owner’s pick **(A’)** — one prose fix in
`vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd`: corrected the
inverted focal-population description. The article said the focal set
was “the founders still in the colony,” but the filter
`!(is.na(sire) & is.na(dam)) & is.na(exit)` selects **non-founders**
(animals with at least one known parent) still in the colony. Now reads
“the non-founders still in the colony (those with at least one known
parent)” — the parenthetical defines the term inline so the fix also
removes the ambiguity. **(DONE — verified two ways + re-rendered.)**
Resolves the S108-discovered inversion, open and carried forward as
option (A’) through S109/S110. **One-line fix only** — did NOT touch the
roxygen drifts, the CRAN plan, or any other article (FM \#18/#25/#8).
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17 **Status:** **DONE. TDD phase =
N/A** (documentation prose using an existing exported-function
walkthrough; no `R/` logic or test surface — declared every response,
same as S104–S110). **No `AskUserQuestion`** — the owner named (A’)
directly; no scope sub-question remained. **0 stakeholder corrections.**
Right-sized under ultracode as **SOLO** — a one-line prose correction
with a deterministic two-way verification has no breadth to fan out and
no competing claims to adjudicate; a Workflow would be theater (the S106
/ S100 call) → **Learning 105**. New session within the conversation:
claimed it (1B stub before technical work) and confirmed fresh post-S110
state (the full Phase 0 reads were done this same turn). - **What
changed (4 edits, 0 new):** **EDIT**
`vignettes/articles/breeding-group-formation.qmd` (the one prose
sentence, lines ~60–63). **EDITS:** `CHANGELOG.md` (S111

`SESSION_NOTES.md` (this handoff). **No `R/`, test, NAMESPACE, `man/`,
DESCRIPTION, NEWS, ROADMAP, CI-workflow, planning-doc, or CRAN-vignette
change** (the fix changes neither package contents nor roadmap state;
ROADMAP needed no edit). - **🚨 THE REAL WORK WAS RE-VERIFYING AN
INHERITED FLAG, NOT TRUSTING IT (→ Learning 105):** A flagged error from
a prior session is a claim to re-check, not a fact to action — the
discoverer (S108) couldn’t fix it precisely because it wasn’t their
deliverable, so the claim had never itself been re-verified against
data. I confirmed it two ways before editing: (1) **internal
corroboration** — the same sentence trims the pedigree to the focal set
“plus the ancestors needed to compute their kinships,” and founders have
no ancestors, so the focal set must logically be non-founders; (2)
**ground truth** — ran `qcStudbook(examplePedigree)` + the article’s
exact filter: **327 focal animals, NONE founders, ALL with ≥ 1 known
parent** (the studbook has 1,668 founders, zero in the focal set; the
327 matches the GVA article’s population-of-interest count). Both agreed
→ fix safe. - **Verification (build-equivalent):**

*female* skew REVERSED from reality:** the plot shows 35 F / 11 M, but
the living colony is 35 F / **54 M** (male-majority). A plot from
incomplete data can show the OPPOSITE of reality, not just an attenuated
version — this became the article’s central honesty / QC-tie-in lesson.
(3) **Verified through 3 paths:** `quarto render` (Quarto 1.7.33) →
clean HTML + both figures (deterministic *shape* — no RNG/seed; `qcPed`

`pkgdown::build_article("articles/age-sex-pyramid")` (pkgdown 2.2.0)

confirmed both against the data/paper before FIX-FORWARD applying:

should have stated this first-draft), and reframed the Reference so V&R
2015 is the *package’s* origin, not the pyramid method’s source (the
pyramid is general demography via
[`plotrix::pyramid.plot()`](https://plotrix.github.io/plotrix/reference/pyramid.plot.html)).

complete census needs birth dates). (2) **Base graphics (plotrix):** the
plot call must be the chunk’s last expression; `#| results: hide`
suppresses the (non-plot) return value. **`getPyramidPlot.R` `@return`

(3,694) → standardized 3,694-row df; `reportErrors=TRUE` returns a
diagnostic LIST (or `NULL` when clean), and each shipped error-demo set
triggers exactly one finding
(`pedFemaleSireMaleDam`→`femaleSires`/`maleDams`;
`pedInvalidDates`→`invalidDateRows=3,4`;
`pedDuplicateIds`→`duplicateIds`; `pedMissingBirth`→`missingColumns`;
`pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam`→`sireAndDam`); `reportErrors=FALSE`
(production) **auto-corrects** female-sire/male-dam (s1→M, d1→F) and
removes exact duplicates (9→8) but **STOPS** on missing column, invalid
date, sire==dam, young parent, period-in-ID. (2) **Running it REFUTED my
own assumption** — I’d read the code and assumed invalid dates were
coerced to `NA`; they actually stop the function. A behavior you only
read in source is a hypothesis until you run it. (3) **Verified through
3 paths:** `quarto render` (Quarto 1.7.33) executes the chunks → clean
HTML (deterministic — no RNG/seed; `examplePedigree` ships an `age` col
so no [`Sys.Date()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/Sys.time.html) leaks;
confirmed byte-identical across two renders);
`pkgdown::build_article("articles/studbook-quality-control")` (pkgdown
2.2.0) wraps it in the site template; an `R CMD build` tarball confirms
`vignettes/articles/` is **absent** → zero CRAN risk, the 21 shipping
vignette files intact. (4) **Verified the verifier (2 lenses):**
code-correctness (all 9 challenged claims confirmed vs `qcStudbook.R` +
~18 helpers) + pedagogy/render-determinism (byte-identical double
render). No must-fix; confirmed the one real flag against the code
(`qcStudbook` calls `convertSexCodes(ignoreHerm=TRUE)` → `H` folds to
`U`, never output) and FIX-FORWARD corrected the article’s “M/F/U/H” →
“M/F/U”; added a `## Setup {#sec-setup}` section for house-style
parallelism; re-rendered (prose-only). - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:**
website-only documentation = dev-process history → **CHANGELOG only**
(S109 entry), no NEWS (\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\]). **NO

([`pkgdown::build_article`](https://pkgdown.r-lib.org/reference/build_articles.html),

**Gotchas:** (1) **cwd-drift trap (I hit it):** a build command that
does `cd /tmp && R CMD build …` strands the shell cwd in `/tmp`; the
*next* `git status`/`quarto render` then runs in the wrong directory.
Use absolute paths + `git -C <repo>`, or `R CMD build <abspath>` without
`cd`. (2) **`qcStudbook(reportErrors=TRUE)` returns a LIST or `NULL`
(when clean), NOT a data frame** — you can’t get the cleaned df AND the
error list in one call; production mode (`FALSE`) returns the df or
STOPS. (3) **Shipped error-demo data sets are the teaching hooks:**
`pedGood` / `pedFemaleSireMaleDam` / `pedInvalidDates` /
`pedDuplicateIds` / `pedMissingBirth` / `pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam` — each
triggers exactly one finding. (4) **Age–sex pyramid article (the next
ready candidate) determinism caveat:**
[`getPyramidPlot()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPyramidPlot.md)
embeds
[`lubridate::now()`](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/now.html)
in the plot TITLE (date drifts) and
[`getPyramidAgeDist()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPyramidAgeDist.md)
computes ages from `now()` — but shipped `qcPed` carries a frozen `age`
column, so `getPyramidPlot(qcPed)` only drifts the title date
(cosmetic). It is **base graphics** (plotrix) → the plot call must be
the chunk’s last expression. (5) **The 3 S108-discovered roxygen drifts
(orderReport/calcFEFG/reportGV) remain unrepaired** (A″, gated — ships).
(6) **CI gh-pages build is NOT locally verified** — watch the next push
to `main`/`master` (or a PR/release) for the pkgdown workflow result.
(7) This close-out leaves `add-methodology` **9 ahead of origin**
(S101–S109) — push when ready (accumulate-then-push). (8) Standing
uncommitted: `.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`
(untracked) — owner’s keep-call. (9) Discovered, NOT mine (still open):
the `breeding-group-formation.qmd` “founders” → “non-founders” prose
inversion (A′). (10) Carried-forward CRAN gotchas remain valid:
`NEWS.md` generated from `NEWS.Rmd`; renv explicit-snapshot omits ALL
Suggests; pkgdown/quarto are dev-lib-only (re-install after a fresh
[`renv::restore`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html));
`RSelenium` undeclared; the package is ARCHIVED on CRAN (Learning 95).

**Ran the real
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
pipeline before writing prose** and let the printed output be the ground
truth — 327-animal population of interest (`examplePedigree` 3694 rows →
trimmed 704), 199 High Value / 128 Low Value, `fe` 109.67 / `fg` 47.62,
kinship 327×327. This handed me the teaching example for free: rank-1
`1SPLS8` has higher genome uniqueness but NOT the lowest mean kinship —
which is exactly why the ranking uses both metrics. (2) **Source-checked
the scoring scheme in the implementation, not the roxygen** —
`orderReport.R`/`rankSubjects.R` (the High-Value mean-kinship tier gates
on the **z-score** `zScores <= 0.25`, not raw mean kinship) and
`calcFEFG.R` (fe = 1/Σp², fg = 1/Σ(p²/r), r = mean founder alleles
retained → fg ≤ fe; Lacy 1989). **The article is MORE correct than the
package’s own roxygen in 3 places** (the `orderReport` @return “mean
kinship \< 0.25”; `calcFEFG`’s description of `p`; `reportGV` @return “A
dataframe” — it returns a list). (3) **Verified through 3 paths:**
`quarto render` (Quarto 1.7.33) executes the chunks on shipped data →
clean HTML (deterministic via `set_seed(1L)`; the gene-drop behind
gu/fe/fg is stochastic, mean kinship is not);
`pkgdown::build_article("articles/genetic-value-analysis")` (pkgdown
2.2.0) wraps it in the site template; an `R CMD build` tarball confirms
`vignettes/articles/` is **absent** → **zero CRAN risk**, shipped
vignettes (a2interactive/a3manual/manual_components) intact. (4)
**Verified the verifier:** the adversarial reviewer returned **no
must-fix**; I confirmed each of its 3 low-severity flags against the
code (`meanKinship.R` is `colMeans` incl. the diagonal → “all animals
incl. itself”; tier-2 sort ties break on ascending z-score; imports tier
also requires `id %in% founders`), applied the fixes, and re-rendered
(output unchanged — prose-only). - **Discovered, NOT mine — flagged for
a future session:** the sibling S107 article
`breeding-group-formation.qmd` comments its focal set is “the founders
still in the colony,” but the filter `!(is.na(sire) & is.na(dam))`
selects **non-founders** (≥ 1 known parent). One-line prose inversion;
left untouched (FM \#8 — not this deliverable). -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]:** website-only documentation = dev-process
history → **CHANGELOG only** (S108 entry), no NEWS (never ships;
\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\]). **NO auto-memory** — the work

package called ‘rmarkdown’”. (2) **pkgdown article name carries the
`articles/` prefix:**

errors “Can’t find article”). (3) **Building generates artifacts to
clean before commit:** `pkgdown_site/` (the site dest, NOT `docs/`),

`vignettes/articles/.gitignore` (`/.quarto/`, redundant with the root
`.gitignore`), a `*_files/` support dir, and the `.html` — the articles
dir should end with only `_quarto.yml` + the `.qmd` sources. (4)
**`set_seed(1L)` makes the article deterministic;**
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
does NOT seed internally (gene-drop is stochastic; mean kinship is not).
