website-only), `DESCRIPTION` (+`Config/Needs/website: quarto` so the
pkgdown CI job installs the `quarto` R pkg),
`.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml` (+`quarto-dev/quarto-actions/setup@v2`
so CI installs the Quarto CLI), `.gitignore` (+`.quarto/`), `ROADMAP.md`
(slice-2-done), `CHANGELOG.md` (S107 `[Unreleased]`),
`docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md` (§7.1
slice-2 row → ✅ Done), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 101),
`SESSION_NOTES.md` (this handoff). **No `R/`, test, NAMESPACE, `man/`,
NEWS, or CRAN-vignette change.** - **🚨 THE REAL WORK WAS THE
VERIFICATION (→ Learning 101):** (1) **Recon corrected an imprecise plan
note.** §7.1 said “add a `_quarto.yml`” without saying *where*; the
canonical pkgdown docs say it + the `.qmd` belong **inside
`vignettes/articles/`** (pkgdown makes that dir a Quarto project), so
one `.Rbuildignore` line excludes everything — a root `_quarto.yml` (my
Phase-1 assumption) was wrong. (2) **Built through the REAL consumer,
not a proxy.** `quarto render` proves the chunks execute (~1.7 s on
`examplePedigree`, deterministic via `set_seed(1L)`); but the
deliverable’s claim is “*pkgdown* mixed mode works,” so I installed
pkgdown **2.2.0** + the `quarto` R pkg locally and ran
`pkgdown::build_article("articles/breeding-group-formation")` → it
discovers the `.qmd`, runs `quarto render`, wraps it in the pkgdown
template (navbar). That surfaced an integration detail a renderer
wouldn’t — the article’s pkgdown name carries the `articles/` prefix
(bare stem errors “Can’t find article”). (3) **Zero CRAN risk PROVEN**
by a real `R CMD build` tarball: `vignettes/articles/` is absent; the
shipping vignettes are unaffected. (4) **Verified the verifier:** a
fresh adversarial reviewer flagged “1/64 = second cousins” as wrong
(claimed third cousins) — but it confused coefficient of relationship
(r; 2nd cousins = 1/32) with kinship coefficient (φ = r/2 = 1/64, which
is what the package computes); the package’s own manual says “second
cousins” at this threshold. Original was correct, left unchanged. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]:** website-only doc/config = dev-process history
→ **CHANGELOG only** (S107 entry), no NEWS (never ships;
\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\]). **NO auto-memory** — the slice
now lives in the repo (analysis §7.1 + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG)
(\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
committed ONLY the 11 changed/new files; excluded `.DS_Store`
(modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep);
removed the local `pkgdown/` favicon artifact + `pkgdown_site/` + all
render artifacts before committing.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped.** A
build-ignored, website-only pkgdown article changes NO package or app
runtime behavior (no `R/`/test/app-path change; the article doesn’t
ship). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the
build-equivalent render (`quarto render` → clean HTML, chunks execute)
**and** the real-consumer build
([`pkgdown::build_article`](https://pkgdown.r-lib.org/reference/build_articles.html)
on pkgdown 2.2.0) **and** the package-contents proof (`R CMD build`
tarball). **NOT FM \#24** — no build step mistaken for correctness; I
verified the actual executed surface through two independent paths.
**The one thing NOT locally verifiable: the GitHub Actions gh-pages site
build** (the DESCRIPTION + workflow CI changes) — confirm on next push
(see gotcha 3).

### What Session 106 Did

**Deliverable:** **§7.1 Slice 1** of the adopted Hybrid documentation
policy — convert the developer doc `inst/extdata/meeting_notes.Rmd` →
`inst/extdata/meeting_notes.qmd` (Quarto), making the three
`inst/extdata/` dev docs uniformly `.qmd`. **(DONE — `git diff -M`
similarity 99%; Quarto renders to HTML; both filenames build-ignored
`ships=FALSE`.)** Implementation slice (one of the adopted Hybrid
policy’s slices); the deliverable is the conversion. **One slice only**
— did NOT start slice 2/3/4 (FM \#18/#25). **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-17 **Status:** **DONE. TDD phase = N/A** (format conversion of a
build-ignored doc; no RED/GREEN/REFACTOR code or test surface; declared
every response — same as S104/S105). **One `AskUserQuestion` (pre-RED
SCOPE decision, not a TDD gate):** slice 1 vs slice 2 — owner picked
**slice 1**. No TDD phase-transition gates (none apply). **0 stakeholder
corrections.** Right-sized **SOLO** under ultracode — a single-file
mechanical conversion (`git mv` + one YAML line) with a deterministic
render + build-ignore check has no breadth to fan out and no independent
claims to verify; a Workflow would be theater (the S101–S105 call). →
**Learning 100.** - **What changed (1 doc converted + 5 housekeeping):**
**`inst/extdata/meeting_notes.qmd`** — `git mv` from `.Rmd` (preserves
history) + the single YAML line `output: html_document` → `format: html`
(matching siblings `claude_code.qmd` / `software_design_doc.qmd`);
**body byte-for-byte** (`git diff -M HEAD` = `similarity index 99%`,
only the YAML line differs). Plus
**`docs/planning/quarto-documentation-future-proofing-analysis.md`**
(§7.1 slice-1 row → ✅ Done), **`ROADMAP.md`** (slice-1-done note under
the doc-engine policy), **`CHANGELOG.md`** (S106 `[Unreleased]` entry),
**`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`** (Learning 100), **`SESSION_NOTES.md`** (this
handoff). **No `.Rbuildignore` edit needed** (see below). **No `R/`,
test, NAMESPACE, `man/`, DESCRIPTION, NEWS, or vignette change.** - **🚨
THE REAL WORK WAS THE VERIFICATION (→ Learning 100) — a rename-only
“conversion” would have hidden three things:** (1) **An Rmd→qmd SEMANTIC
difference.** The render warned that `nprcgenekeepr:::` (R’s
internal-call operator, quoted from a 2020 CRAN-review reply, in
**prose**) looked like a malformed `:::` fenced div — `:::` is
meaningful in Quarto, inert in R Markdown. Checked the rendered HTML: it
renders correctly (mid-line `:::` is not a div delimiter → heuristic
false-positive, no output defect). **Left byte-faithful** rather than
rewrite the author’s historical prose for a cosmetic warning (FM \#22).
(2) **The doc is NOT reproducibly renderable — and that’s not mine to
“fix.”** Its 5 R chunks hardcode 2020 absolute paths
(`/Users/rmsharp/.../20160816_GeneticManagementTools`) and need packages
absent from the default lib (standing renv-not-materialized);
`include=FALSE` hides output but the chunk still *executes*, so a full
render fails on the dead paths. It’s a **historical meeting-notes log**,
not a live computational doc. Build-equivalent satisfied via
`quarto render --no-execute` (YAML + structure → 81 KB HTML, code shown
not run); execution limitation **stated, not silently skipped**. Making
it executable would be a behavior change (FM \#8). (3) **“It was
build-ignored” ≠ “the new file is.”** Confirmed by applying every
`.Rbuildignore` regex to BOTH filenames in R: `meeting_notes.Rmd` and
`meeting_notes.qmd` both `ships=FALSE` (covered by
`^inst/extdata/meeting_notes\.` — extension-agnostic — and
`^inst/extdata/.*\.qmd$`). Had the pattern been `...\.Rmd$`, the
conversion would have started SHIPPING the `.qmd` into the CRAN tarball
— a silent package-contents change for an archived package
mid-resubmission. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** a build-ignored dev-doc
format standardization = dev-process history → **CHANGELOG only** (S106
entry), no NEWS (never user-facing). **NO auto-memory** — the
decision/slice now lives in the repo (analysis §7.1 + ROADMAP +
CHANGELOG) (\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** committed ONLY the 6 changed files; excluded
`.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked,
standing keep).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not silently skipped.**
Converting a build-ignored developer doc changes NO package or app
runtime behavior (no `R/`/test/app-path change; the file doesn’t even
ship). The verification appropriate to the deliverable = the
**build-equivalent render** (`quarto render --no-execute` → clean
HTML) + the **package-contents check** (both filenames `ships=FALSE`) +
the faithful-diff check (`similarity 99%`) — all done. **NOT FM \#24** —
no build step mistaken for correctness; there is no runtime behavior to
test.

docs → Quarto (pkgdown articles via mixed mode, `revealjs` slides,
`inst/extdata/` dev docs). **§7.1 slices (suggested order):** (1)
standardize the 3rd `inst/extdata` dev doc `meeting_notes.Rmd`→`.qmd` —
**no CRAN risk**; (2) new pkgdown articles in Quarto (mixed
`_quarto.yml`) — **no CRAN risk**; (3) `revealjs` decks as needed — **no
CRAN risk**; (4) **reposition the manual** (`a3manual.Rmd` + 13
children) off CRAN vignettes onto the website — **CRAN-touching, gated
on CRAN-plan coordination.** - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** a
documentation-process decision = dev-process history → **CHANGELOG
only** (S105 entry), no NEWS (S104 analysis-doc precedent;
\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\]). **NO auto-memory** — the
decision now lives in the repo (analysis doc + ROADMAP + CHANGELOG), so
a memory would duplicate the source of truth
(\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
commit ONLY the 5 changed docs; exclude `.DS_Store` (modified) +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep).

reframed mid-session:** opened as “is Rmd→Quarto conversion

The recommendation document is mine to author per the planning rules. →
**Learning 98.** - **🚨 RECOMMENDATION = HYBRID / partial adoption
(owner’s decision; 3 options in the doc §7):** KEEP the 4 CRAN vignettes
on `knitr`/`rmarkdown` (zero CRAN risk, officially supported
indefinitely, endorsed for this case by the Quarto maintainer); ADOPT
Quarto on the non-CRAN surface where it carries no CRAN dependency and
pays off — pkgdown site, new long-form docs, slide decks, the
`inst/extdata/` dev docs (2 already `.qmd`). The long-form **manual**
(`a3manual.Rmd` + 13 children) is the one deliberate fork (§6.3): keep
as a knitr vignette vs. reposition onto the website as Quarto. - **All 6
load-bearing claims survived adversarial refutation at HIGH
confidence:** (1) R Markdown is NOT deprecated — `rmarkdown` 2.31
(2026-03)/`knitr` 1.51 (2025-12) actively maintained CRAN-critical
infrastructure (Posit: “not going away, no plans for deprecation”; Xie
laid off 2023-12-31 but Posit-funded contractor to keep maintaining
knitr/rmarkdown); the only cost of staying is feature stagnation, not
breakage. (2) Quarto CRAN vignette adds a `SystemRequirements`
Quarto-CLI dep NOT guaranteed on CRAN machines (missing on macOS flavors
2025) + a documented transient “no vignettes” NOTE — bad for an archived
package; maintainer cderv “would not advise” it for CRAN. (3) Realized
Quarto benefit for simple single-language HTML vignettes is narrow (CRAN
engine is `theme:none`/`minimal:true` →
callouts/tabsets/Bootstrap/multi-language disabled; only
cross-references land). (4) Conversion can’t fix timing (same knitr
R-chunk cost + fixed overhead → slightly slower). (5) Precompute on the
existing engine solves timing without Quarto. (6) Migration is
mechanical/reversible; pkgdown supports mixed `.qmd`/`.Rmd` (2.1.0,
2024-07). - **Firsthand inventory (not just the audit agent):** all 4
CRAN vignettes use knitr engines (`knitr::rmarkdown_notangle` ×3;
`a3manual` =
[`knitr::knitr`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/knitr/man/knitr-package.html));
`ColonyManagerTutorial` is `eval=FALSE` (screenshots only);
`a2interactive` runs real analysis; `simulatedKValues` has 3 sims
n=10/100/1000 with `set.seed(1L)` before each (deterministic →
precompute-safe); `a3manual` is a 57-line wrapper over 13
`manual_components/_*.Rmd`. `VignetteBuilder: knitr`, no Quarto dep. The
`vignettes/*.html|.R|.md` are git-ignored stale renders — **no
precompute pattern exists**. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** an
analysis/planning doc = dev-process history → **CHANGELOG only** (S104
entry), no NEWS (\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\], S101 plan
precedent). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** commit ONLY the new analysis doc +
`CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (L98) + `SESSION_NOTES.md`;
exclude `.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`
(untracked, standing keep).

### What Session 103 Did

**Deliverable:** **Phase 2 of the CRAN 2.0.0 submission plan**
(`docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md` §4 Phase 2) — archival
ROOT CAUSE (CRAN “tested elapsed times”). **Owner-scoped to Phase 2a
(tests + native pipe + NEWS); Phase 2b (vignette rebuild timing)
DEFERRED.** **(DONE — Phase 2a; 2b remains.)** Development workstream +
strict TDD. **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17 **Status:** **DONE
(Phase 2a). TDD phase = REFACTOR/mechanical** — every edit is
no-numeric-change (skip guards change only WHAT runs on CRAN; native
pipe is syntactic), so the plan’s RED-first simulation-number rule did
NOT apply (avoided, not triggered; declared every response). **Two
`AskUserQuestion` gates:** a pre-RED **scope** decision (defer vignettes
2b; convert ALL `%>%`) + the **PRE-RED→REFACTOR** gate spelling out
every edit + verification. **Owner steered live** (build-vs-run deps;
`Config/Needs/build`; native `|>` instead of declaring magrittr; NEWS
entry; the harness-misattribution catch; the jargon flag). Right-sized
**SOLO** — measurement is CPU-sensitive (parallel checks would corrupt
timing) and the fix is gated mechanical edits; a Workflow would corrupt
the measurement and add no value. - **Measure-first profile (the cause,
named by data not assumption — overturned the plan’s single-suspect
framing):** **examples** 6.6s total, slowest `countLoops` 1.28s — none
\>5s, none flag (no action). **Tests:** the CRAN-running slow ones are
the **shiny module `testServer` tests** (`modGeneticValue` 4.4s /
`modBreedingGroups` 4.6s / `modInput` 2.7s /
`modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign` 2.2s / `modPedigree_processing` 1.2s;
`skip_if_not_installed("shiny")` only — shiny is an Import, so they
RUN). The raw-slowest files (`fillGroupMembers*`/`groupAddAssign`, ~17s)
are `skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp")` → owner-machine-only, **never on
CRAN**. **Vignettes** ~21s (`ColonyManagerTutorial` 7.8s /
`a2interactive` 7.5s / `simulatedKValues` 5.4s) → **Phase 2b** (the only
iteration-reduction lever — `simulatedKValues` n=1000 gene-drop, 3.68s —
would change displayed kinship numbers = a correctness regression, so it
needs numeric-preserving precompute, a separate effort). - **What
changed (13 files):** **Tests** — file-level
[`testthat::skip_on_cran()`](https://testthat.r-lib.org/reference/skip.html)
added to `test_modGeneticValue.R`, `test_modBreedingGroups.R`,
`test_modBreedingGroups_groupAddAssign.R`, `test_modInput.R`,
`test_modPedigree_processing.R` (removes ~15s CRAN time; CI/local
unaffected). **Native pipe** `%>%`→`|>` —
`vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd` (5; dropped
[`library(magrittr)`](https://magrittr.tidyverse.org)),
`vignettes/ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` (3),
`tests/testthat/test_makeRelationsClasses.R` (2),
`R/makeRelationClassesTable.R` `@examples` (2) + hand-synced
`man/makeRelationClassesTable.Rd` (`\%>\%`→`|>`). **NEWS** — `NEWS.Rmd`
Minor “Code modernization” bullet + re-rendered `NEWS.md`. Plus
`CHANGELOG.md` (S103 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 97), this
handoff. - **Verification:** full core suite via
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
at **`NOT_CRAN=true` → 169 files, 0 failed, 0 errors** (after installing
the missing `shinyBS` Suggest in the dev lib — the earlier “52/69
errors” were 100% environment/harness, NOT regressions); at
**`NOT_CRAN=false` the 5 mod files skip** (affected-file time
14.82s→0.01s). `simulatedKValues.Rmd` renders with native pipe (no
magrittr); `makeRelationClassesTable` example runs; `man/*.Rd` parses;
`NEWS.md` re-rendered with only the new bullet (no reformat); 0 `%>%`
remain in any converted file.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): N/A — STATED, not skipped.** No `R/`
function BODY or app/runtime path changed (the
`makeRelationClassesTable` edit is roxygen-comment-only; skip guards are

**Session 102 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 103): Score 9/10.** S102

a different problem than “restore first”; (b) neither the plan nor the
handoff flagged the `skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp")` test guards (which
make raw timing misleading) or that the package’s test harness needs
`load_all` (not [`library()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/library.html)) to
expose internals. All were caught by the measure-first discipline the
plan itself prescribed.

**Self-assessment (Session 103): 7/10.** Oriented fully, claimed the
session (1B stub before work), measured before concluding, and the
measure-first paid off (corrected the single-suspect assumption;
surfaced the rmsharp-only guards). **Strengths:** (1) **profiled each
surface honestly and re-measured when wrong** — when the owner asked
“why don’t you have shinyBS”, I found my real error (ran tests via
`library()+test_dir`, which hides internals → 52 false
`could not find function` “errors”) and re-ran via `load_all` for
trustworthy numbers; (2) **respected the numeric-change rule** —
deferred the only vignette speed-up that would alter displayed numbers,
scoping to a no-numeric-change fix and gating it; (3) handled the
owner’s live direction cleanly (native `|>` over declaring magrittr;
`Config/Needs/build` for build-only tooling; per-surface dep precision);
(4) **verified thoroughly** (0/0 regression after resolving the env gap,
skip behavior both NOT_CRAN settings, render/example/.Rd/NEWS).
**Weaknesses (honest):** (a) the **harness misstep** — I should have
used `load_all` from the start and not initially blamed missing Suggests
for the 52 errors; the owner caught it (−1+); (b) used **jargon
(“dragon-prone”)** the owner objected to (saved as a memory) (−1); (c)
Phase 2 is **partially delivered** (2a; 2b deferred) — owner-approved,
but not the whole plan-Phase. Two genuine stakeholder corrections (vs
the recent 0-correction streak). I reserve 9–10 for a clean-harness,

**Gotchas:** (1) **renv state is NOT what the prior gotchas said** —
[`renv::restore()`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html)
reports **already-synchronized** (Imports materialized in the cache-path
library); the gap is **Suggests** (explicit snapshot omits them). This
session I installed into the dev lib (NOT the lockfile, so they won’t
survive a fresh
[`renv::restore`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html)):
`testthat, mockery, withr, dplyr, magrittr, kableExtra, png, shinyBS, pkgload, roxygen2`.

authoritative gate). (3) **Phase 2b vignette fix must PRESERVE numbers**

**`RSelenium` is undeclared** in Suggests (e2e tests use it). (5) The
`skip_if_not(user=="rmsharp")` tests run only on the owner’s machine —
an anti-pattern, out of Phase-2 scope. (6) **NEWS.md is generated from
NEWS.Rmd** — edit the `.Rmd`, re-render
(`rmarkdown::render(output_format="github_document")`); never hand-edit
NEWS.md. (7) **This close-out leaves `add-methodology` 3 ahead of
origin** (S101 `60b8c67c` + S102 `a3cf3623` + this S103 commit) — push
when ready (accumulate-then-push). (8) Standing uncommitted: `.DS_Store`
(modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked) — owner’s
keep-call.

CRAN session is an archived-resubmission, not a routine update; the
timing root cause must be MEASURED (profile examples/tests/vignettes
under `--as-cran`), not assumed; `cran-comments.md` must address the
archival (Learning 95). (2) **`NEWS.md` is generated from `NEWS.Rmd`** —
edit the `.Rmd` (plain ASCII) and re-render; never edit `NEWS.md`
directly. (3) **The 1.1.0.9000 NEWS section double-counts changes** —
the rewrite must merge duplicates (plan §6.3 / dragon \#4). (4)
**`.Rbuildignore` is perl-regex — an unbalanced paren even in a comment
aborts `R CMD build`** (anchored, paren-free lines only). (5) **renv
library NOT materialized** (Learning 92) —
[`renv::restore()`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html)
is a prerequisite for the check phases. (6) **A speed-up that changes
simulation/sampling numbers is a correctness regression** — RED-first
(dragon \#2). (7) **No reverse dependencies** — do NOT run `revdepcheck`
(plan §3.5). (8) **The owner performs the CRAN upload + email
confirmation** (Phase 5 STOP — outward-facing). (9) The 2 untracked
files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain
uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (10) **This
close-out leaves `add-methodology` 1 ahead of origin** — push when ready
(accumulate-then-push; S96/S99 pattern).

2026-06-16 **Status:** **DONE.** Operational git/GitHub task (open a PR
on already-committed work), not a code change → **TDD phase N/A** (no

`git fetch origin master add-methodology`. (3) The divergence looked
alarming but was benign —
`git rev-list --left-right --count origin/master...origin/add-methodology`
= **2 / 54**, where the “2 ahead” on master are exactly the prior
**`Merge pull request #43/#41`** nodes
(`git log origin/add-methodology..origin/master` → `c6ad23dd`,
`0363ffe3`), i.e. add-methodology’s own work merged back, NOT
independent master work. (4) **Three-part safety proof (→ Learning
94):**
`git merge-tree --write-tree origin/master origin/add-methodology` →
**exit 0, no conflicts**; `comm -23` of the two file trees → **no
master-only files**; the tip-to-tip diff is an exact **mirror**
(`master`-vs-add-methodology = `+406 / −6001`, the inverse of
add-methodology’s `+6001 / −406` over 114 files) → master is **strictly
behind**, zero unique content. ⇒ merge gains exactly the 54 commits
(S52–S99), loses nothing. - **PR body grounded in canonical sources, not
commit-message memory:** read `NEWS.md` (1.1.0.9000) + the
`CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]` section for the user-facing summary;
categorized the 54 commits (6 `feat` / 11 `fix` / 3 `test` / 33 `docs`).
Drafted to `/tmp/s100_pr_body.md`, confirmed title+body via
`AskUserQuestion` (“Create the PR as drafted”), then created. Verified
post-create: base/head/state/mergeable/commit_count all correct. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]:** opening a PR publishes already-committed,
already-logged work (the 54 commits each carry their own CHANGELOG/NEWS
entries) → not itself a new software change → **no new CHANGELOG/NEWS
entry** (\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\], the S96/S99 push

*explicit* body-edit list (Finding \#5 / Rec \#2) said “strike the 3
now-reached functions, add the 4th S3, note the docfix” — but the real
change-set was **5 strikes + 2 additions**: it undercounted the strikes
and never named `chooseDate` or `setAutoIdFormat`, because it was framed
as a delta-since-S78 while the body lagged to S65. A maximal handoff for
a “keep + update body” candidate flags “the body is at the S65 baseline
— updating to HEAD needs a full recompute-and-reconcile, not just the
since-S78 delta” (exactly Learning 93). Light, fair dock — the recompute
method + disposition table were in hand; I had to apply them one step
more completely than the literal list.

**Self-assessment (Session 98): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full, notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, 14
issues, ghost-check → `02728e92`=S97), reported, STOPPED for the owner’s
task; then **disambiguated candidate (1)’s two-deliverable fork via
`AskUserQuestion`** rather than assuming a close vs a body-update.
Claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline
strengths:** (1) **recompute-don’t-inherit applied to a publication** —
independently reproduced S97’s 39-set and reconciled the WHOLE body
programmatically, which caught two stale entries beyond the handoff’s
list (`chooseDate`, `setAutoIdFormat`), a 42≠39 arithmetic error, and an
S3 grep false-negative; (2) verified every published claim firsthand
before it went public; (3) made the body internally consistent,
self-documenting (the “what changed” block + audit reference), and left
a dated thread comment for the otherwise-silent body edit; (4) produced
**Learning 93** (a genuinely non-obvious meta-lesson: a handoff delta is
anchored to the handoff’s baseline, not the artifact’s). **Scope
discipline (1-and-done):** edited exactly \#37’s body + one comment; did
NOT close \#37, did NOT push, did NOT touch other issues / `.DS_Store` /
the untracked files, did NOT start another candidate. **0 stakeholder
corrections.** Right-sized SOLO; declared TDD N/A + Phase-3E N/A with
reasons. **The −2 (honest):** (a) the subject was deeply audited the

the two extra catches + the arithmetic/consistency fix + making the body
authoritative, non-trivial but on well-trodden ground; (b) I **inherited

column (keep-as-public-API reasons) rather than re-deriving all 39
firsthand — appropriate (re-deriving verified dispositions is the exact
Learning-91 redundancy), but it means the body’s *reachability* is
freshly verified while its *disposition prose* is inherited; I state
that plainly. I reserve 9–10 for a harder delivery.

args (`Map`/`apply`/`do.call`) are false-flagged unused (Learning

untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) +

die). So I recomputed reachability **statically** — `sys.source` all 202
`R/*.R` into a throwaway env +
[`codetools::findGlobals`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/codetools/man/findGlobals.html)
under `Rscript --vanilla` (base lib only; `/tmp/s97_reach2.R`). First
pass used `findGlobals(merge=FALSE)$functions` and FALSE-flagged
`chooseDate` unused; firsthand check found `Map(chooseDate, …)` at
`R/setExit.R:54` (passed as a value → `$variables`, not `$functions`).
`merge=TRUE` (the correct conservative “app uses this” test) reconciled
it with S78. Result: **127 app-used / 39 unused** (35 functions + 4 S3
methods); 202/202 files sourced (100% coverage). - **The delta vs S78,
all verified firsthand:** (1) **\#47 ORIP module** mounted
`appUI.R:181`/`appServer.R:286`, issue **CLOSED** → left the unused set.

via `getPotentialParents.R:42`). (3) **Docfix FIXED** —
`getPedDirectRelatives` `@examples` now invokes itself (was the

**(DONE — docs-only on the `@noRd` internal `fillBins()`; NO `R/`
behavior, NAMESPACE, `man/`, or DESCRIPTION change. Two NEW `test_that`
blocks + a `returnBlock()` helper added to
`tests/testthat/test_fillBins.R`: (1) a doc-completeness test (RED
driver) reading `R/fillBins.R` via
`test_path("..","..","R","fillBins.R")` + `skip_if(!file.exists)`,
scoped to the extracted `@return` block, asserting no `TODO` +
`\bmales\b`/`\bfemales\b`; (2) a behavioral return-contract guard (green
at HEAD, honestly classified). `test_fillBins.R` 11/11; `document()`
no-op (no NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change); `lintr` 0; full
clean-regression 0 failed/0 error.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-16 **Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream under **Strict
TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; gates via `AskUserQuestion`: a
separate pre-RED **test-approach scope** question (doc-completeness +
contract vs doc-completeness only) + **PRE-RED→RED**, **RED→GREEN**,
**GREEN→REFACTOR**; **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit
on `add-methodology`. **\#33 left OPEN pending owner confirmation.** -
**Recompute-don’t-inherit (applied to the issue’s claim):** verified
FIRSTHAND that the issue’s suggested `@return` is accurate before
writing it — `R/fillBins.R:32` returns
`list(males = maleBins, females = femaleBins)`; `maleBins`/`femaleBins`
are built from `integer(0L)` + `nrow(...)` (→ integer vectors, one
element per `seq_along(lowerAges)` bin); the existing values test
confirms integer (`c(0L,0L,2L,1L,0L)`); the caller `R/getMaxAx.R:18`
consumes them as `bins$male`/`bins$female` (R `$` partial-match of the
plural names) = per-bin counts. ✓ - **RED design (the load-bearing
craft):** since `fillBins` is `@noRd` there is **no `man/fillBins.Rd`**,
so S87’s `tools::Rd2ex(man/<fn>.Rd)` pattern
(`test_examples_invoke_documented_fn.R`) is UNAVAILABLE — the doc lives
only in the SOURCE file, read via `test_path` + `skip_if`-installed (RED
in dev, inert under `R CMD check` where `R/*.R` isn’t shipped). **The
false-pass trap I scoped around:** `fillBins.R`’s TITLE already says
`list of two lists \code{males} and \code{females}`, so a whole-file
`grepl("males", ...)` would be GREEN with the TODO still present — the
test extracts ONLY the `@return` block (from the `@return` line to the
next `#' @` tag) and uses `\bword\b` boundaries (so the `males` inside
`females` doesn’t satisfy the `males` assertion). All 3 assertions RED
at HEAD for the right reason; the RED failure output literally printed
the isolated `@return` line, proving the extractor scoped correctly. -
**GREEN:** the issue’s verified
`\describe{\item{males}{...}\item{females}{...}}` wording (matches the
package’s existing `@return` convention — 4 files use `\describe`,
e.g. `R/getPossibleCols.R`), all lines ≤80. **REFACTOR = confirmed
no-op** (idiomatic, lint 0, lines ≤80). - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** a
`@noRd` internal’s docs never render to a user-facing man page →
**CHANGELOG only** (S92 entry), no `NEWS`. **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
commit only the doc/test/notes files; exclude `.DS_Store` + the 2
standing untracked files.

### What Session 90 Did

**Deliverable:** Finalize **\#4** — CLOSED the issue (owner-confirmed;
S89’s fix `8a3e3631` was Phase-3E-verified) **and** added behavioral
regression coverage for the Shiny **upload path** that S89 had covered
only *structurally* (a `deparse(body(modInputServer))` grep). **(DONE —
two NEW test files, test-only; no `R/`/NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION
change. (1) `tests/testthat/test_modInput_incomplete_final_line.R` —
in-process
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
driving `modInputServer`’s `getData` with a TINY no-trailing-newline
upload through read.csv + read.table; asserts the warning does NOT
escape + all 3 records survive QC; FIX-SPECIFIC teeth PROVEN by
un-muffle round-trip (both blocks RED un-muffled, green restored). (2)
`tests/testthat/test-e2e-input-incomplete-final-line.R` — opt-in
`shinytest2` browser E2E uploading a no-trailing-newline
`ExamplePedigree.csv` through both reader paths; asserts the cleaned
studbook flows end-to-end to all 3,694 records in the Pedigree Browser.
testServer 6/6, browser E2E 2/2, full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error,
lines ≤80.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-16 **Status:** **DONE.**
Development workstream (regression backfill of already-shipped behavior)
under **Strict TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; gates via
`AskUserQuestion`: a separate pre-RED **scope** question (both reader
paths) + **PRE-RED→RED**, **RED→GREEN**, **GREEN→REFACTOR**, **+ a
mid-GREEN reframe** question when the investigation changed what was
testable; **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit on
`add-methodology`. **\#4 CLOSED.** - **\#4 closed first** (owner gave
the confirmation S89 deferred): the fix shipped + Phase-3E-verified in
S89; closed with an evidence comment referencing `8a3e3631`, the root
cause, and the surgical muffle. - **THE load-bearing discovery (cost ~6
browser diagnostics + several install/`load_all` round-trips — see
Learning 88):** `read.table`/`read.csv` emit
`"incomplete final line found by readTableHeader"` **only for SMALL
files** — the header/type-detection scan must actually REACH the
unterminated final line; a realistic-size file (3694-row
`ExamplePedigree`) **never triggers it**. AND the warning is a
**console-only artifact**: in the assembled app it never reaches the DOM
or `app$get_logs()`. ⇒ a browser `get_logs` warning-absence assertion is
a TAUTOLOGY (it stayed green even un-muffled — the un-muffle round-trip
exposed it). **Root self-critique:** I chased “the app swallows the
warning” for many cycles before validating the FOUNDATIONAL assumption
(does my fixture even reproduce the warning in a plain `read.csv`? It
didn’t). De-risk the FIXTURE, not just the observation mechanism. -
**Resulting honest division of labor (surfaced to the owner via the
mid-GREEN reframe `AskUserQuestion`):** the warning-suppression teeth
live in the **in-process `testServer`** test (TINY 4-line fixture, where
the warning IS observable via `withCallingHandlers`); the **browser
E2E** owns end-to-end **data integrity** (a no-trailing-newline upload
processes correctly to all records). Both reader paths covered in
each. - **testServer test (normal suite):** drives `getData` with a
`data.frame(name,size,type,datapath)` fileInput value +
`session$setInputs(getData=1)` wrapped in `withCallingHandlers`; asserts
`!any(grepl("incomplete final line", captured))` and
`qcSummary()$records == 3`, `$errors == 0`, for read.csv

shows `of 3,694 entries` (the modInput QC outputs are in SUSPENDED
hidden nested tabs and don’t reliably un-suspend; a top-level consumer
is the robust signal — Learning 88). - **Verification:** testServer
**6/6**; browser E2E **2/2** (real Chrome, INSTALLED app); full
clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (195 files; 5 warnings =
the designed `loadSiteConfig` safety-net logs); all test lines ≤80
(REFACTOR reflowed two 81–82-char lines). **\[news-vs-changelog\]:**

only** (S90 entry). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** commit only the 2 test
files + docs; exclude `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

facts that made a naive”upload a file, assert no warning in the browser”
approach a tautology and forced the testServer pivot + the tiny-fixture
discovery. Genuinely the implementer’s to discover (S89 never
investigated browser-observability), so it’s a light, fair dock — but a
maximal handoff would have flagged “the warning is a console-only
artifact; a browser may not even see it” rather than implying a
straightforward browser assertion. ROI: very high on *what/where*; the
sizing was optimistic.

**Self-assessment (Session 90): 8/10.** Oriented fully, reported,
STOPPED for the owner’s pick; closed \#4 on the owner’s confirmation
with an evidence comment; claimed the session (Phase 1B stub BEFORE
technical work). Used `AskUserQuestion` for the pre-RED scope (both
reader paths) + all three TDD gates + an honest **mid-GREEN reframe**
when the discovery changed what was testable — declared the phase every
response; **0 stakeholder corrections**. **The strength = honest
teeth:** the un-muffle teeth round-trip caught a TOOTHLESS browser
assertion before it shipped (it stayed green un-muffled), and I refused
to ship a test claiming warning-teeth it didn’t have — I surfaced the
finding and split the deliverable into a testServer test that PROVABLY
catches the regression (both blocks RED un-muffled) + a browser E2E that
owns end-to-end. Right-sized SOLO under ultracode (a contained two-file
backfill with interactive gates; a Workflow would be theater AND can’t
pause for the gates). **The −2 (honest):** I burned many cycles on a
wrong hypothesis (“the assembled app swallows the deferred warning”)
before checking the cheapest foundational thing — *does my chosen
fixture reproduce the warning in a plain `read.csv`?* (it didn’t; the
file was too large). A single plain-R fixture check up front would have
saved most of the investigation. That self-critique IS Learning 88:
**de-risk the fixture, not just the observation mechanism; a teeth
round-trip that won’t go RED means a toothless assertion OR a
non-triggering fixture — check the fixture first.**

**Gotchas:** (1) **The `"incomplete final line"` warning fires ONLY for
SMALL files** (≤ ~5 lines — the `readTableHeader` scan must reach the
unterminated final line) AND is a **console-only artifact** (never in
the DOM or `app$get_logs()` from the app). ⇒ it canNOT be teeth-tested
in a browser; a browser `get_logs` absence assertion is a TAUTOLOGY. The
suppression teeth live in the **testServer** test (TINY fixture). (2)
**modInput’s QC outputs are in SUSPENDED hidden nested tabs**
(`dataInput-mainTabs`; `suspendWhenHidden=TRUE`) —
`qcSummaryUI`/`cleanedDataTable`/`downloadCleaned` are empty/`disabled`
until their tab is active, and neither `set_inputs(mainTabs=...)` nor a
JS anchor-click reliably un-suspends them (bslib BS4 + broken shinyBS
JS). Read counts via a **top-level consumer**: `#pedigree-pedigreeTable`
in the Pedigree Browser (DataTables formats the count WITH a comma →
match `"of 3,694 entries"`, not `3694`). (3) **`data-ready=true` is set
server-side** (in the `getData` observer) and does NOT imply the
hidden-tab outputs rendered or that `cleaned` is non-NULL. (4) **Teeth
round-trip recipe (testServer):** un-muffle BOTH `modInput.R` read sites
→
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
→ run the testServer file → both blocks RED →
`git checkout R/modInput.R` → green. **No install needed for
testServer**; the browser E2E needs `devtools::install()` first (drives
the INSTALLED app). (5) **The browser E2E uses the BIG fixture** (3694
rows; warning never fires) = a pure end-to-end data-integrity net; the
**testServer uses TINY fixtures** (warning fires) = the suppression
teeth. Two complementary vehicles BY DESIGN — don’t “consolidate” them.
(6) **`flog.*` writes to a FILE appender** (`~/nprcgenekeepr.log` via
`appServer.R:38-43`), NOT stderr — so
`flog.debug("File read successfully. Rows: N")` does NOT reach
`app$get_logs()`. (7) **\#4 is CLOSED.** (8) The 2 untracked files
(`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain
uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. (10) **Installed package = muffled
HEAD** (reinstalled at the end of this session). **Branch model
unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on
the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

### What Session 89 Did

**Deliverable:** Fix **\#4** — reading an animal list or pedigree file
whose final line lacks a trailing newline emitted
`"incomplete final line found by readTableHeader on '...'"` (reported
from a Shiny text upload, `0.txt`). **(DONE — NEW non-exported `@noRd`
helper `muffleIncompleteFinalLine()` muffles ONLY that warning; wrapped
all 4 read sites; NEW `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R`
7/7 RED→GREEN; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error; lint 0 on the 5
changed `R/` files; no NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change; Phase-3E on
the INSTALLED app confirms the readers no longer emit the warning, rows
preserved, and the muffle is surgical.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-16 **Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream under **Strict
TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; **3 phase gates** via
`AskUserQuestion` (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR) **+ a
separate pre-RED scope question** (all-readers-via-helper vs
named-readers vs app-only); **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out
commit on `add-methodology`. - **Reproduce-first
(recompute-don’t-inherit, applied to the bug):** before designing
anything, wrote a no-trailing-newline CSV and read it — confirmed the
warning is **noise, not data loss** (`read.table`/`read.csv` read every
row including the last; `nrow == 3`). That decided the fix shape:
**suppress the warning**, not recover a row. Also confirmed
`grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed = TRUE)`
matches, so the muffle can target exactly this warning. - **Owner scope
decision (separate pre-RED `AskUserQuestion`): All readers, shared
helper** — root-cause over symptom-only. The same bug exists identically
at 4 sites (`getPedigree.R:30` / `getGenotypes.R:30` `read.table`;
`getFocalAnimalPed.R:44` `read.csv`; `modInput.R:274`+`:279` upload
`readDataFile`). The mechanism (surgical `withCallingHandlers` muffle)
was settled by reproduction, so only scope was the owner’s fork. -
**RED:** NEW `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` (7 tests
/ 12 expectations). Helper teeth: no-newline read → no warning + all
rows; an unrelated `warning("...")` **still propagates** (surgical, not
blanket `suppressWarnings`); returns `expr` unchanged. Integration:
`getPedigree`/`getGenotypes` → no warning + rows preserved;
`getFocalAnimalPed` → no `"incomplete final line"` warning (its
post-read LabKey call returns an errorLst without a DB — the unrelated
config-missing warning is acceptable, so I assert absence of the
SPECIFIC warning); `modInputServer` body references the helper
(structural lock for the server-internal `readDataFile` closure). All 7
RED at HEAD for the right reason (helper missing → 3 errors
`object 'muffleIncompleteFinalLine' not found`; readers warn → fail;
body lacks wrap → fail). The 2 passing expectations at HEAD are the
`nrow == 3` data-preservation guards (data was never lost). - **GREEN:**
NEW `R/muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` — non-exported `@noRd` internal
helper (matches `getRecordStatusIndex`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter`
convention) =
`withCallingHandlers(expr, warning = \(w) if (grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed=TRUE)) invokeRestart("muffleWarning"))`.
No new imports (base fns). Wrapped each read:
`x <- muffleIncompleteFinalLine(<existing read call>)` at all 4 sites.
`document()` → **no** NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change (`@noRd`;
reader signatures unchanged — `git status`-confirmed). **REFACTOR =
confirmed no-op** (helper + wraps minimal/idiomatic/lint-clean; one
roxygen line-length reflow done in GREEN to reach lint 0). -
**Verification:** new file **7/7 green** (12 expectations); full
clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (192 files / 1046 tests /
167 skips; the 5 warnings are S85’s designed `loadSiteConfig` safety-net
`flog.warn` logs — confirmed by the “Could not find center” text);
`lintr` **0 lints** on all 5 changed `R/` files. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]:** user-facing file-reading bug fix → **BOTH**
`NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (new bullet under `1.1.0.9000` → Data input /
quality control; rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY the new bullet —
pandoc’s straight→curly-quote + `--`→en-dash conversion is expected, not
churn) **AND** `CHANGELOG.md` (S89 entry). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
`git add` only source/test/doc/notes; excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2
standing untracked files.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The “incomplete final line” warning was NOISE, not
data loss** — `read.table`/`read.csv` always read the last row
correctly; the fix only suppresses the warning. Do not “fix” it by
appending newlines / normalizing files. (2)
**`muffleIncompleteFinalLine()` is surgical** — it muffles ONLY warnings
whose message contains `"incomplete final line"`; every other warning
still propagates (a malformed-file warning, the LabKey config-missing
warning, etc.). Do NOT replace it with
[`suppressWarnings()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/warning.html). (3) **The
helper is `@noRd` (non-exported)** — reference it in tests as
`nprcgenekeepr:::muffleIncompleteFinalLine`; `document()` does NOT
generate a `man/*.Rd` or a NAMESPACE entry for it (the package’s
convention for internals). (4) **`getFocalAnimalPed` on a no-DB machine
emits TWO warnings** (incomplete-final-line + “configuration file is
missing”); assert absence of the SPECIFIC incomplete-final-line warning,
not “no warnings”. (5) **The `modInput` upload path (`readDataFile`) is
a server-internal closure** — tested STRUCTURALLY
(`deparse(body(modInputServer))` grep), not behaviorally; a `shinytest2`
upload E2E is the heavier behavioral alternative if desired. (6) **\#4
is left OPEN** pending owner confirmation. (7) The 2 untracked files
(`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain
uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (8) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

& `getAncestors`. **(DONE — 3 `R/` `@examples` edits + 3 regenerated

**Gotchas:** (1) **The example-invokes-its-own-fn test reads `man/*.Rd`
via [`tools::Rd2ex`](https://rdrr.io/r/tools/Rd2HTML.html)** — it RUNS
under `load_all`/dev (the project’s standard test invocation) and SKIPS
under an installed package (no `man/`). So it does NOT protect under
`R CMD check`’s installed-pkg test run; the example correctness there is
enforced by `R CMD check`’s own example runner. (2) **For a
roxygen-example fix, “example runs without error” is a WEAK test** —
broken examples often still run (`getPedDirectRelatives`’s buggy one
soft-failed to `NULL`); the discriminating test is STRUCTURAL (example
calls its own fn). (3) **Use `grepl(..., fixed=TRUE)`** for the `<fn>(`
check — `"\\("` triggers “unrecognized escape” depending on quoting, and
`fixed=TRUE` also requires the literal `(` (a call, not a mention). (4)
**`document()` can drift unrelated `.Rd`** under a different roxygen2
version — `git status`-check after and restore any file you didn’t
intend to change; here it cleanly touched only the 3 intended `.Rd`. (5)
**NEWS workflow:** edit `NEWS.Rmd` (source),
`rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd")` → `NEWS.md`, and `diff` against a
pre-render backup to confirm ONLY the new bullet changed (render was
stable here). (6) **`lacy1989Ped` `E` is a FOUNDER** (no ancestors) —
use `F` (sire=D, dam=E → {A,B,D,E}) for a non-trivial `getAncestors`
integration check. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say “Version
1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked
files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain
uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

— `#oripReporting-siteInfo` reports `Center=ONPRC`, the Export ORIP
Report button is in the pane, and
`app$get_download("oripReporting-downloadORIPReport")` yields a
`Category/Metric/Value` CSV with a `Center=ONPRC` Site row
(deterministic — the handler writes the Site section even with no
pedigree loaded); (3) **no config (stock app) → tab ABSENT**
(`navigate_to_tab`→FALSE AND `grepl("oripReporting-", body)`→FALSE); (4)
**SNPRC config → tab ABSENT** (proves the gate keys on `center`, not
mere config presence). - **Config-injecting fixture** (the load-bearing
design move): a LOCAL `build_config_app_dir(center)` writes a temp
`app.R` that
`Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with a complete documented-format .nprcgenekeepr_config>)`
BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` — S84/S85’s HOME-override Phase-3E
recipe **promoted to a reusable test fixture**. The POSITIVE case can’t
use the stock `create_test_app()` app (no config → tab hidden); the
NO-CONFIG negative case rides the stock app for free. The builder reuses
the opt-in gate by calling `create_test_app()` for its **skip
side-effect** (path discarded) — **NO change to `helper-shinytest2.R`**.
Config dir lives INSIDE the app dir so one `unlink(recursive=TRUE)`
(registered AFTER `app$stop()`) cleans both. - **GREEN verification:**
independently confirmed the three gate outcomes in-process FIRST
([`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
parse →
[`shouldShowOripTab()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/shouldShowOripTab.md):
ONPRC→TRUE, SNPRC→FALSE, no-config→FALSE) — Learning 80b applied to a
predicate, so the browser run passed first try;
`devtools::install(quick=TRUE)`; then `NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`
single-file → **9/9 expectations PASS** (real Chrome). - **REFACTOR
(owner-approved):** reflowed 6 over-length lines to ≤80 (split the long
`lkPedColumns` config line into the shipped example config’s multi-line
format, which `getTokenList` parses); re-verified parsing
(`center=ONPRC`, 7 `lkPedColumns` tokens, gate TRUE) + a **fresh 9/9
browser run** (the config line is content the app reads) + full
clean-regression 0/0 + lint clean. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:**
test-only (no `R/` code, no user-facing feature; the ORIP feature landed
in NEWS at S83/S84) → **CHANGELOG only** (S86 entry).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** `git add` only the test file + doc/notes;
excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** The browser E2E run
**IS** the runtime verification (strongest kind): real Chrome via
`chromote`, real reactive graph, real config-driven ONPRC gating, real
CSV download. All 4 blocks pass against the **installed** app — 9/9
expectations — including the ONPRC-gated tab present+navigable,
`#oripReporting-siteInfo` rendering ONPRC, the report CSV’s
`Center=ONPRC` row, and the tab genuinely ABSENT under both no-config
and SNPRC. `shinytest2`+`chromote` are installed here, so this ran for
real (`NOT_CRAN=true` + install-first required — Learning 78).

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ORIP E2E is OPT-IN** (`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`); it
skips cleanly otherwise (4 skips, reason “On CRAN” when `NOT_CRAN`
unset). To run it: `NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` +
`devtools::install()` FIRST (it drives the INSTALLED app). (2) **The
POSITIVE/SNPRC cases boot a config-injecting `app.R`** (HOME-override) —
the stock `create_test_app()` app has NO config so it can only serve the
no-config-NEGATIVE block. The builder reuses the opt-in gate via
`create_test_app()`’s skip side-effect (no shared-helper change). (3)
**The config the fixture writes must have all 7 params** (`getParamDef`
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)s on a missing one when a
file exists); `center` is the only one the GATE reads, but all 7 must be
present to parse. (4) **`getConfigFileName` reads `Sys.getenv("HOME")`**
— that’s the lever; the generated `app.R` must `Sys.setenv(HOME=...)`
BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`, and the HOME path is embedded as
a `normalizePath(winslash="/")` absolute literal. (5) **`.lintr`
excludes `tests/`** — `lintr::lint()` on a test file returns “No lints
found” regardless of content; I still kept all lines ≤80 by hand (the
sibling’s max is 82). (6) The browser E2E run IS the Phase-3E
verification (FM \#24) — there is no separate smoke needed. (7)
CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION
`1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted
(build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

### What Session 85 Did

**Deliverable:** Fix **\#50** — the modular app’s config-loading
observer (`R/appServer.R:58-68`) used `read.table(sep="=")`, which
crashes
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)
on boot whenever a config file in the documented format
(`inst/extdata/example_nprcgenekeepr_config`: comments, blank lines,
multi-line quoted/comma values) is present. **(DONE — NEW exported
[`loadSiteConfig()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/loadSiteConfig.md)
routes config through the tolerant
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
parser wrapped in `tryCatch`; observer collapses to a one-liner; new
tests 8/8; full suite 0 failed/0 error; lint 0; Phase-3E AppDriver boots
the REAL documented config that previously crashed → PASS. \#50 CLOSED
on owner confirmation 2026-06-16.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-15
**Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream under **Strict TDD**. TDD
phase declared every response; **3 phase gates** via `AskUserQuestion`
(PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR) **+ a separate pre-RED approach
question** (single-source-of-truth vs minimal-tryCatch, posed before
RED); **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out commit on
`add-methodology`. - **Pre-RED decision (owner, via `AskUserQuestion`):
Single source of truth** (issue’s suggested fix \#1) over a minimal
`tryCatch`-only patch — parse via the tolerant
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
path so documented-format configs actually LOAD, not merely fail-soft.
**De-risked firsthand:** `shared$config` is passed to
`modInputServer`/`modPedigreeServer` but referenced by NEITHER module
body (`grep -n "config" R/modInput.R R/modPedigree.R` → only
`@param`/signature, default `config = NULL`, ignored), so reshaping it
from a `read.table` data.frame to the
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
named list has zero runtime consumer impact. - **RED:** NEW
`tests/testthat/test_loadSiteConfig.R` (5 tests / 8 expectations): (1)
no-file → `NULL`; (2) documented `example_nprcgenekeepr_config` → list,
`center=="SNPRC"`, `lkPedColumns>=6` \[THE crash case\]; (3) malformed
config (missing `center`) → `NULL`, no throw \[`tryCatch` safety net\];
(4) characterization guard — `read.table(sep="=")` errors on the
documented file \[root-cause lock, green throughout, honestly classified
as NOT a RED driver\]; (5) structural — `deparse(appServer)` contains
`loadSiteConfig`, NOT `read.table`. Confirmed RED: 1/2/3 error
(`could not find function "loadSiteConfig"`), 5 fails (observer still on
`read.table`), 4 passes. Rest of suite untouched (only a new test file
added). - **GREEN (2 edits + `document()`):** NEW exported
`R/loadSiteConfig.R` = `getConfigFileName(Sys.info())` → `NULL` if no
file, else
`tryCatch(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE), error → futile.logger::flog.warn + NULL)`;
rewrote the observer to
`observe({ shared$config <- loadSiteConfig() })`. `document()` →
NAMESPACE export + `man/loadSiteConfig.Rd`. **REFACTOR (owner-chosen
small readability tweak):** added a bidirectional
`@seealso \link{loadSiteConfig}` to `appServer`’s roxygen,
re-`document()`’d; no behavior change, full suite still 0/0. -
**Verification:** new tests 8/8 green; full clean-regression read **0
failed / 0 error** (the 5 warnings are the designed `flog.warn`
safety-net logs from test 3); `lintr` **0 lints** on all 3 changed files
(the `object_usage` warning for the brand-new fn cleared after
`devtools::install()`). - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** user-facing (a
startup-crash bug fix) → **BOTH** `NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (new bug-fix
bullet under `1.1.0.9000`, rendered from SOURCE, diff = ONLY that
bullet) **AND** `CHANGELOG.md` (S85 entry). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
`git add` only source/test/doc/notes; excluded `.DS_Store` + the 2
standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** `AppDriver` boot of
the **installed** app with the **REAL documented
`example_nprcgenekeepr_config` (center=SNPRC) present**
(HOME-override-in-`app.R` recipe; `NOT_CRAN=true`; dev installed first)
— the exact file that crashed boot before this fix. App reached a stable
state (`AppDriver$new` returned, `mainNavbar`=“Home”), navigation to
“Genetic Value Analysis” worked (reactive graph alive), **0**
`read.table`/“did not have 2 elements” crash lines, **0** non-`shinyBS`
error-level logs. **This is the boot S84 had to SIDESTEP with a stripped
single-line config; the documented format now boots clean** — the
designed payoff of S84’s flag-and-file.

**Gotchas:** (1) **\#50 is CLOSED** (owner-confirmed 2026-06-16; fix
`d440e504`, Phase-3E-verified). (2) **`shared$config` is now a
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
named list** (was a `read.table` data.frame) when a config file is
present, else `NULL`. Safe TODAY because both consumer modules ignore
the `config` param — but if a FUTURE change makes a module actually USE
`config`, it now receives the named-list shape, not a data.frame. (3)
**[`loadSiteConfig()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/loadSiteConfig.md)
fails SOFT:** a missing OR malformed config → `NULL` + a
[`futile.logger::flog.warn`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/futile.logger/man/flog.logger.html)
to the `nprcgenekeepr` logger (no R warning/error surfaced to the user).
A partial config (missing one of the 7 required params) also no longer
crashes — `getParamDef`’s [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)
is caught → NULL. (4) **Phase-3E HOME-override recipe (reusable):** temp
`app.R` does `Sys.setenv(HOME=<dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE
`shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`; `AppDriver$new(<appdir>)`;
`NOT_CRAN=true`; install dev first. **The DOCUMENTED
`example_nprcgenekeepr_config` now boots clean** — no longer needs S84’s
stripped single-line workaround. (5) **Test idiom:**
[`withr::local_tempdir()`](https://withr.r-lib.org/reference/with_tempfile.html) +
`withr::local_envvar(c(HOME=tmp))` (withr is available via testthat);
name the config file by
`basename(getConfigFileName(Sys.info())[["configFile"]])` for
OS-correctness. (6) **`object_usage_linter` false-positives a brand-new
package function** (“no visible global function”) until
`devtools::install()`; re-lint after install. (7) CLAUDE.md +
`appUI.R:216` still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) —
stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted
(build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

### What Session 84 Did

**Deliverable:** (1) Owner-confirmed close of **\#47** (ORIP wire-in
accepted), then (2) implement **\#49** — gate the **“ORIP Reporting”**
tab to show **only for ONPRC** — under Strict TDD. **(BOTH DONE — \#47
CLOSED; \#49 = NEW exported predicate + parameterized `appUI` + gated
server mount; new tests 5/5 + 4/4; full suite 0 failed/0 error; lint 0;
Phase-3E AppDriver 3/3 config scenarios PASS. Discovered a pre-existing
config-observer crash → filed NEW \#50, did NOT fix it.)** **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-15 **Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream
under **Strict TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; **3 phase
gates** via `AskUserQuestion` (PRE-RED→RED, RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR)
**+ a separate pre-RED scope/approach question** (the product fork AND
the gating mechanism, posed together before RED); **0 stakeholder
corrections**. One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. -
**Owner-authorized first action — \#47 CLOSED:** the owner’s “1” was the
firsthand confirmation the standing close-rule requires; closed \#47
with a comment referencing S83 commit `6fd16715` + the 13/13 Phase-3E
smoke, noting ONPRC-gating is tracked in \#49. (Not a workstream
deliverable — a clerical close; \#49 is the substantive work.) - **Two
pre-RED decisions (owner, via one `AskUserQuestion`):** (a) **product
fork → Hide unless a real ONPRC config** — show only when an actual
config file exists AND `center=="ONPRC"`; the
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
default fallback (`center="ONPRC"` with no file, true in dev/CI) hides
the tab. (b) **approach → Build-time conditional `tabPanel`** (not
dynamic `insertTab`) —
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
is evaluated ONCE at `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` construction and the
deployment center is fixed per server, so the tab’s presence is a
per-deployment CONSTANT, not a per-session reactive (dynamic `insertTab`
is for DATA-driven tabs like Error List). - **RED:** NEW
`tests/testthat/test_shouldShowOripTab.R` (5 pure-predicate cases) +
rewrote `tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting.R` to INJECT a `siteInfo`
list — tab PRESENT under ONPRC+existing-file, ABSENT under SNPRC /
non-existent-file — plus a `deparse(appServer)` assertion that the
server mount is gated on `shouldShowOripTab`. All failed for the right
reason (undefined function / unused `siteInfo` arg / un-gated mount);
rest of suite unaffected (only test files changed). - **GREEN (3
edits):** NEW exported `R/shouldShowOripTab.R` =
`isTRUE(hasConfigFile) && isTRUE(center == "ONPRC")` (mirrors
`shouldShowChangedColsTab`; `isTRUE(==)` not `identical` →
names/NA/NULL/length-safe); parameterized
`appUI(siteInfo = getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile = FALSE))` with
`if (isTRUE(showOrip)) tabPanel("ORIP Reporting", …)`; gated the
`modORIPReportingServer` mount in `appServer.R` on the same predicate.
`document()` → NAMESPACE export + `man/shouldShowOripTab.Rd` +
`man/appUI.Rd`. **REFACTOR = confirmed no-op** (kept the sibling
inline-call idiom; no helper extraction — would break the appServer
structural test’s intent). - **Verification:** new tests 5/5 + 4/4 (9
expectations); full clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error**;
`lintr` **0 lints** on all 5 changed files (the `object_usage` warning
for the brand-new fn cleared after `devtools::install()`, like its
installed siblings). - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** user-facing
(visible-tab behavior change) → **BOTH** `NEWS.Rmd`→`NEWS.md` (ORIP
bullet augmented with the ONPRC-gating note; rendered from SOURCE, diff
= ONLY that bullet) **AND** `CHANGELOG.md` (S84 entry).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** `git add` only source/test/doc/notes; excluded
`.DS_Store` + the 2 standing untracked files.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS (3/3 config
scenarios).** `AppDriver` boots of the **installed** app, each via a
generated `app.R` that
`Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE
`shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` (controls the real `getSiteInfo`→config
path in-process): **ONPRC config** → ORIP tab present + navigable
(active pane = ORIP), all 5 module outputs register values, 4 siblings
intact; **SNPRC config** → tab ABSENT (UI + server), siblings intact;
**no config** → tab ABSENT, siblings intact. 0 ORIP-namespaced
ERROR-level logs (the 5 `oripReporting` lines are INFO `shiny:value`
registrations; the only errors are the pre-existing app-wide `shinyBS`
noise). **First app boot ever done WITH a config file present → surfaced
pre-existing bug \#50 (below).**

**Discovered (out of scope) → NEW \#50:** the pre-existing
config-loading observer (`appServer.R:63`, from `6457a3a3`, 2025-12-29)
uses `read.table(configFile, header=TRUE, sep="=")`, which **cannot
parse the documented config format** (`example_nprcgenekeepr_config`:
comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted values) and **crashes the
modular app on boot** whenever such a file is present — latent in dev/CI
only because no config file exists there. **NOT fixed** (unrelated to
\#49); sidestepped for the smoke with a stripped single-line `key=value`
config both `read.table` AND `getParamDef` accept; filed **\#50** with
root cause + repro + suggested fix.

**pre-named THIS deliverable as SUGGESTED-NEXT candidate (2):**

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ORIP tab gate is BUILD-TIME** — decided when
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)/`appServer`
first run (per-deployment constant). Correct because the config is
per-server, but it does NOT react to a mid-session config change. (2)
**[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
now takes a `siteInfo` param** (defaulted to
`getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)`) — tests inject it; production
callers (`runModularApp`, `inst/shinytest/app.R`) call
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
with no args (unchanged behavior). (3) **Pre-existing bug \#50:** the
`appServer` config observer’s `read.table(sep="=")` crashes the app on
boot with a DOCUMENTED-format config file. For ANY app-boot-with-config
test, use a stripped single-line `key=value` config (all 7 params:
center, baseUrl, schemaName, folderPath, queryName, lkPedColumns,
mapPedColumns — multi-values SPACE-separated, no quotes/commas/comments)
that both `read.table` and `getParamDef` accept. (4) **Phase-3E
HOME-override recipe:** generate a temp `app.R` doing
`Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE
`shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`, then `AppDriver$new(<that dir>)`
(`NOT_CRAN=true` + install dev first). One AppDriver per config
scenario. (5) **Classify AppDriver logs by LEVEL/text, not namespace
mention** — `shiny:value <ns>-<output>` lines are INFO registrations
(success), not errors; genuine errors are `error`-level minus the
pre-existing app-wide `shinyBS is not defined` noise. (6)
**`object_usage_linter` false-positives a brand-new package function**
(“no visible global function”) until `devtools::install()`; re-lint
after install to confirm clean. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say
“Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2
untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) +
`.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing
keep-call). (9) **Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** —
use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

navigate via `app$set_inputs(mainNavbar="<Tab Title>")`; grep logs for
YOUR namespace (the `shinyBS is not defined` errors are pre-existing
app-wide noise). **SCRIPT GOTCHA:** `app$get_html(".tab-pane.active")`
returns **MULTIPLE** nodes (every nested module tabset has its own
active pane) — collapse with `paste(collapse=" ")` or assert
`app$get_value(input="mainNavbar")`; a length-N vector into
`&&`/`isTRUE` halts the run. (4) **`NEWS.md` is GENERATED from
`NEWS.Rmd`** (`github_document`) — edit the `.Rmd` SOURCE and
`rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd", output_file="NEWS.md")`; verify sync by
diffing a fresh render. **Never hand-edit `NEWS.md`.** (5)
**`siteConfig=NULL` and

functionally identical** (module falls back to the same call) — I passed

animal”/“of 50 entries”/50-row-CSV checks only pass when the feature
actually returns 50; a broken/empty feature renders the empty-state and
fails them (not the body-grepl tautology the helper comments warn
against). **Right-sized SOLO** — a contained single-file test backfill
with interactive TDD gates is not a breadth fan-out; a Workflow would
have been theater (Learning 70/77 right-size heuristic, contained side)
— and that call holds even with ultracode “on”, because the project

more targeted first read could have saved one round-trip; (c) the table
assertion couples to DT’s *“of N entries”* info-text format (mirrors the
existing pedigree e2e test’s *“of 375 entries”*, so an accepted,
idiomatic coupling — but a DT phrasing change would require updating
both).

**Gotchas:** (1) **The new E2E test is OPT-IN** (`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`)
and **drives the INSTALLED package** — to run it: `devtools::install()`
dev code first, then
`NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true Rscript -e '...test_file(...)'`.
Without `NOT_CRAN=true` AppDriver aborts “On CRAN”; without install the
installed copy may be stale (Learning 78). (2) **`shinytest2` +
`chromote` ARE installed here** — the browser E2E genuinely runs; don’t
assume “no Chrome → skip”. (3) **`PP_EXPECTED_CANDIDATES <- 50L` is a
regression lock** — if the fixture or `getPotentialParents` ever changes
the count, this fails by design; re-derive via the pipeline (`read.csv`
→ `runQcStudbook(...)$cleaned` → `setPopulation` →
`getPotentialParents(_, 2.0, 210L)`) and update. Both
`processedPedigree` and `pedigreeData` yield 50 today. (4) **The table
assertion uses DT’s *“of 50 entries”* info text** (mirrors the pedigree
e2e’s *“of 375 entries”*); a DT phrasing change would require updating
it. (5) **`shared$currentPedigree` = the FILTERED `pedigreeData()`**
(`modPedigree.R:344`), auto-populated by the observer at
`appServer.R:252-255` once the studbook is ready — but blocks 3/4 still
visit Pedigree Browser first to faithfully mirror S81’s manual flow and
dodge any output-suspension flakiness. (6) **`ExamplePedigree.csv` is
the right no-`fromCenter` graceful-degradation input** (QC-clean, no
colony-origin field); the populated path needs
`rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv`. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:198` still say
“Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2
untracked files + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted (build-ignored /
owner’s standing keep-call). (9) **Default `gh issue view` errors on
`projectCards`** — use `gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model
unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on
the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-15 **Status:** **DONE.** Verification +
issue-management workstream (**TDD phase = N/A** declared every response
— added an example dataset + diagnosis, no production logic/tests). One
close-out commit on `add-methodology`. No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`,
`.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes. - **The click-through-first call
(owner’s pick) earned its keep.** Provided the step-by-step script (Part
A graceful-degradation on a standard file; Part B populated path on the
new file). The owner ran it live and **caught a real defect in my staged
file** that my headless pre-check had blessed → I fixed and re-verified
against the app’s EXACT ingest path before close. - **NEW
`inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv`** (375 animals =
[`nprcgenekeepr::rhesusPedigree`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/rhesusPedigree.md) +
`fromCenter = TRUE`; unknown parents written as **literal `NA`**
matching `ExamplePedigree.csv`). Verified through the real pipeline (app
reader → `runQcStudbook` → `setPopulation` → `getPotentialParents`): QC
clean, `fromCenter` preserved, **50 animals with candidates**. Owner
chose the `rhesusPedigree` source (vs full-ExamplePedigree /
modify-in-place) via `AskUserQuestion`. - **Defect caught by the
click-through (FM \#24 — replication diverged from the app):** my first
file wrote unknowns as **empty cells** (`write.csv(na="")`); the app’s
text reader (`modInput.R:274` = `read.table(..., fill=TRUE, quote="\"")`
with **default `na.strings="NA"`**) reads `""` as the empty string, so
`""` landed in **both** the sire and dam columns →
`correctParentSex.R:71` `intersect(sires,dams)` flagged *“Subject(s)
listed as both sire and dam”* (the empty offending id = the “cell empty”
the owner saw). My pre-check had used `na.strings=c("","NA")`, silently
converting `""`→NA and masking it. Rewrote with `na="NA"` and
re-verified with the app’s exact reader → clean. - **Diagnosed two
non-defects firsthand from the code (not guesses):** (1) the *“Column
name case changed: fromCenter → fromcenter”* warning is **cosmetic** —
`fixColumnNames.R:20` lowercases all headers (logging the caseChange),
`:61` restores `fromcenter`→`fromCenter`; the cleaned studbook keeps
`fromCenter`, which is exactly what `getPotentialParents`
(case-sensitive `names(ped)=="fromCenter"`) needs. Same noise hits
`recordStatus`/`geographicOrigin`. (2) *“No data available in table”* =
the empty **Errors** tab DT placeholder (no errors). The earlier repeat
of *“both sire and dam”* was a **stale upload** — cleared once the owner
re-read the rewritten file. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]** → **CHANGELOG
only** (verification + a test-support example dataset + issue close =
dev-process history; the *feature* already landed in `NEWS.md` at S80;
no `R/` code changed). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** `git add` only
`inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` + `SESSION_NOTES.md` +
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` + `CHANGELOG.md`; excluded `.DS_Store` and the 2
standing untracked files (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS (owner-run live
click-through).** The strongest possible runtime verification: real
browser, real reactive graph, real file upload. **Part A** (graceful
degradation): `ExamplePedigree.csv` (QC-clean, no `fromCenter`) →

Potential Parents → Find → **50-animal sortable table** (status *“Found
candidate parents for 50 animal(s)…”*) → **CSV downloaded** as
`potential_parents_2026-06-15.csv` (matches

intact.

code**, not by guessing. **Closed \#48 only on owner confirmation**
(standing rule). **Right-sized SOLO** — a contained diagnose-and-stage
with interactive owner round-trips, not a breadth fan-out (a Workflow
would have been theater; Learning 70). **The −2 (honest):** (a) I
**shipped a defective fixture to the owner first** because my pre-flight
used `na.strings=c("","NA")` instead of the app’s default
`na.strings="NA"` — a faithful replication of the app’s reader up front
(FM \#24) would have caught it before the owner saw it; (b) a couple of
round-trips (stale-upload confusion, case-change alarm) were partly
pre-emptable by telling the owner up front to re-select after a file
rewrite and that the case-change warning is cosmetic.

`inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv` (375 animals;
`fromCenter=TRUE`; unknowns as literal `NA`; the Potential Parents
demo/click-through fixture). **CHANGED (docs):** `CHANGELOG.md` (S81
entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (Learning 79 at tail), `SESSION_NOTES.md`

comment posted). **Read firsthand (unchanged):**

`R/appServer.R:244-295` (`shared$currentPedigree` flow), `R/modInput.R`

from studbook), `R/runModularApp.R` (in-namespace
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)/`appServer`).
**Transient (NOT committed):** `/tmp/s81_*.R`.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The shipped pedigree example files are deliberately
error-laden input-error QC fixtures** (jmac csv: 2 hard parent-age
errors; jmac-edited: a “.” in an ID; xlsx: invalid dates;
`ExamplePedigree`: QC-clean but **no `fromCenter`**) — **none reaches
the populated Potential Parents path.** The clean demo file is
**`inst/extdata/rhesusPedigree_fromCenter.csv`**. (2) **Replicate the
app’s EXACT reader when pre-verifying example data:** text path =
`read.table(..., fill=TRUE, quote="\"")` with **default
`na.strings="NA"`** (`modInput.R:274`) — empty cells stay `""` (NOT NA).
**Write unknown parents as literal `NA`** (`write.csv(..., na="NA")`).
Using `na.strings=c("","NA")` in a check masks the empty-cell “both sire
and dam” bug (FM \#24). (3) **The “fromCenter → fromcenter” caseChange
warning is cosmetic** — `fixColumnNames.R:20` lowercases all headers
(logs it), `:61` restores `fromCenter`; the cleaned studbook keeps
`fromCenter`. (4) **`processQcStudbookResult()$hasErrors` does NOT
include the `sireAndDam` category** — the app’s Errors tab renders the
raw `errorLst$sireAndDam` directly; inspect the errorLst components, not
just `hasErrors`, when judging “is it clean.” (5) **Run the dev app with
the new tab via `pkgload::load_all("."); runModularApp()`** (builds from
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)/`appServer`
in-namespace — no reinstall; an older installed copy lacks the tab and
the line-61 restore). (6) **\#48 is CLOSED** (owner-confirmed). (7)
CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:198` still say “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION
`1.1.0.9000`) — stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files + `.DS_Store`
remain uncommitted (build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call). (9)
**Default `gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** — use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

`@export`. - **NEW `tests/testthat/test_modPotentialParents.R`:** 14
tests / 43 expectations — helper (cols, NULL/empty→0-row, multi-animal
flatten, empty-sires→`""`/0, `write.csv` round-trip), UI (4 namespaced
controls + “Find Potential Parents” + default 210 + `shiny.tag`), server
via [`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)

(2 edits):** `appUI.R` —
`tabPanel("Potential Parents", icon("search"), modPotentialParentsUI("potentialParents"))`
after Breeding Groups; `appServer.R` —
`modPotentialParentsServer("potentialParents", pedigree = reactive(shared$currentPedigree))`
after the Breeding Groups module. - **Data seams honored (from \#48):**

(not error); `minParentAge` does NOT flow to `shared` so it stayed a
**server param** default `2.0` (QC value) — owner ratified only the
gestation UI input, so **no second UI input** added (scope held); empty
results → empty-state message. - **Verification:** GREEN
`devtools::check()` **0/0/0**; post-REFACTOR `check()` 0 errors / 0

failed/error** (847 rows); `lintr` clean on both new files; `document()`
regenerated `man/modPotentialParents*.Rd` + NAMESPACE
(`flattenPotentialParents` stayed internal). -

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): PERFORMED — PASS.** Booted the
assembled app headlessly via
[`shinytest2::AppDriver`](https://rstudio.github.io/shinytest2/reference/AppDriver.html)
against `inst/shinytest/app.R` (= `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`).
**Gotchas to reuse:** set `NOT_CRAN=true` (AppDriver aborts “On CRAN”
under `Rscript` otherwise); `devtools::install()` the dev package FIRST
(the e2e app drives the installed copy); the e2e suite is opt-in
(`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`) and skips in `test()`/`check()`. Confirmed: app
boots with full server init (incl. the new module), the tab + all 4
controls mount, default 210 set, navigation to the tab works, existing
tabs (Breeding Groups, Genetic Value Analysis) intact, and the module’s
outputs (`statusMessage`/`resultsTable`/`downloadParents`) register
error-free. The only log “errors” are pre-existing
`shinyBS is not defined` JS reference errors (app-wide, used by other
tabs’ popovers — NOT from this module; separated by grepping the logs
for the `potentialParents-` namespace). **Not done (honest boundary):**
the literal single-chain browser E2E (upload a file → create pedigree →
click → see table → download the CSV) — the compute/table/CSV path is
runtime-verified by `testServer` on a real `fromCenter` rhesus pedigree
(the real reactive graph, not mocks) and the integration mount by
AppDriver; the data-loaded full-browser interaction is split across
those two rather than one E2E.
