phase = N/A** declared every response — same classification as S73/S76).
One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`,
`.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes. - **Method (Workflow under ultracode,
Learning 70 breadth side + new Learning 75):** a verify-and-sharpen
workflow (`wf_b8035a53-5be`) — **8 parallel item-verifiers** (one per
register item, each re-reading the spec’s code claims firsthand against
the working tree and adversarially stress-testing its `[REC]`) → an
adversarial **completeness critic**. The critic earned its keep: it
reduced an **over-asked 8-item register to 4 genuine owner-decisions**
(items 1/2/3 + item 6’s provenance half), classified the rest as
ratifiable corollaries/deferrals, and surfaced **two §7 policy gaps
absent from §13**. - **Two firsthand corrections to the S76 spec (→
Learning 75a):** (a) **item 2’s written `[REC]` is unsound** —
`getPotentialParents.R:46-48`’s inclusive-OR `pUnknown` filter means a
both-unknown infant’s *own* birth-time location is a valid **dam-side**
anchor (only the sire side is anchorless), so the decision flipped from
the spec’s “require ≥1 known parent” to **dam-side colocation only**;
(b) **§10 mis-cited \#36** (“Add chimpanzee-specific age pyramid plot
settings”, a display ticket) as the “make species first-class”
prerequisite — first-class species was un-ticketed, now filed as
**\#46**. - **Owner decisions (via `AskUserQuestion`, 2 staggered rounds
— item 2 deliberately placed in round 2 so it could be informed by item
1’s answer; all ratified as recommended, 0 corrections):** (1)
missing-dam = mother–infant **co-housing model** + required no-default
species-tunable `postnatalCoHousingWindow` + gestation-presence,
strictly **soft-rank** (hard-filter rejected); (2) both-unknown =
**dam-side only** via focal anchor; (3) output = **additive-only
invariant locked, carrier deferred**; (4) **coherent obfuscation** (one
shared per-animal delta + alias FK-remap, built WITH \#28); (5) **v1
single-species**, \#28 ⊥ first-class species; (6) **pull-date provenance
stamp** (full bitemporal deferred); (7+8) **defer flat-file & POSIXct**
(LabKey-only, Date res); **§7 QC policy** (open-start = no overlap;
contradictory rows = QC-warn). - **The outward-facing work:**
ratification comment on **\#28** (`#28-issuecomment-4703716881`, body
`/tmp/s77_ratify28.md` — transient) annotating every item

### What Session 74 Did

**Deliverable:** Implement **\#31** — replace the dam-exclusion “hack”
(fixed ±½-year birth window) in `getPotentialParents` with a principled
gestation-derived window driven by the **existing**
`maxGestationalPeriod` parameter (owner-decided S73: reuse/extend that
param, NO new parameter). The near-term, low-lift sub-task of umbrella
**\#45**. **(DONE — feature shipped; `devtools::check()` 0/0/0; full
suite 0/0; \#31 left OPEN, awaiting owner confirm to close.)** **Started
/ Completed:** 2026-06-13 **Status:** **DONE.** Code session under
strict **TDD: RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped)** — a separate pre-RED
scope/approach decision + all three transition gates (PRE-RED→RED,
RED→GREEN, GREEN→REFACTOR-skipped) posed via `AskUserQuestion`; TDD
phase declared atop **every** response. **0 stakeholder corrections.**
One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. - **The change (ONE rule, one
line of logic):** a female who delivered another offspring within
`maxGestationalPeriod` days of the focal birth cannot have gestated the
focal animal (a female bears one offspring at a time), so she is
excluded as a candidate dam. The `births` window
(`getPotentialParents.R:83-85`) now uses
`pUnknown$birth[i] ± maxGestationalPeriod` instead of `± (dYear / 2L)`
(182.5 d). The `:106` dam exclusion
(`potentialDams[!id %in% births$dam, ]`) and `:92-95` comment block had
the TODO “hack” replaced with a principled explanation; the
`@param maxGestationalPeriod` roxygen (now `~:17-27`) documents it as
the conservative MAX gestation (e.g. 210 for rhesus vs ~165 typical),
its dual sire-retention + dam-exclusion use, and the **intentional**
sire(conception)/dam(birth) exit-check asymmetry. - **Owner decision
(pre-RED, via `AskUserQuestion`):** **minimal principled change** — only
the `births` window; document the exit-check asymmetry as correct (NOT
changed); leave the preferential band untouched (proven a no-op);
recompute fixtures with per-delta justification; keep
`maxGestationalPeriod=210L` in tests. Applied exactly as chosen. -
**Recompute-don’t-inherit (Learning 70/72 applied to a code change →
Learning 73):** in PRE-RED I read issue \#31/#45 +
`getPotentialParents.R` + `test_getPotentialParents.R` firsthand, then
built a **throwaway read-only oracle** (`/tmp/s74_explore.R`, never
touching `R/`) to quantify the behavior change BEFORE writing tests —
every fixture delta derived independently + justified biologically, not
from running the production change. - **TDD trail (RED→GREEN):** RED
edited ONLY `test_getPotentialParents.R` — recomputed exact-set fixtures
(`dams_1` drops `0B7XRI`/`PHCADH`; `dams_4` drops
`1SIP4V`/`DMI0QY`/`HV7LZ3`, each with a justification comment citing the
conflicting-offspring offset) + added 2 driving tests (a hand-verifiable
**synthetic-exclusion** pedigree + a **differential-responsiveness**
test, acceptance criterion \#2). **Confirmed RED: 4 expected failures**
for the right reasons; the 4 unaffected tests passed. GREEN: the
one-line window change + comment + roxygen + `devtools::document()` →
all green. - **Verification:** test file **21/21**; full suite **0
failed / 0 error** (real + total, excl `test-app-`/`test-e2e-`); **lint
0** on both changed files; **`devtools::check()` 0 errors / 0 warnings /
0 NOTES** (cleaner than the S71/S72-predicted spelling NOTE — it did not
fire; my new doc words are all dictionary words, spell-check clean).

### What Session 71 Did

**Deliverable:** Implement GitHub issue **\#44** (full umbrella scope)
and its **\#38** sub-task — configurable auto-generated unknown-ID
format (default `"U%04d"`, **byte-identical back-compat with no
config**) from a single source of truth: exported
[`getAutoIdFormat()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getAutoIdFormat.md)/[`setAutoIdFormat()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/setAutoIdFormat.md) +
an internal `isGeneratedUnknownId()` predicate replacing all 7 detection
literals across 4 files + 2 generators; case-handling reconciled to
case-sensitive. **(DONE — feature shipped; \#44 + \#38 implemented +
`devtools::check()`-green, awaiting owner confirm to close.)** **Started
/ Completed:** 2026-06-13 **Status:** **DONE.** Code session under
strict **TDD: RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped)** — all three transition gates
posed via `AskUserQuestion`, plus a separate pre-RED scope/approach
decision (3 questions). TDD phase declared atop **every** response. **0
stakeholder corrections.** One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. -
**Owner decisions (pre-RED, via `AskUserQuestion`, 3 questions):**
**(Scope)** full \#44; **(Detection)** prefix-only, byte-identical
(`startsWith(id, <literal prefix before first %>)` — preserves today’s
exact matching, incl. the existing over-match of real prefix-IDs);
**(Case rule)** case-sensitive (match generation’s uppercase prefix;
reconciles `reportGV`+`obfuscateId`, which were insensitive). All
applied exactly as chosen. - **Recompute-don’t-inherit (Learning 70
applied to a feature):** re-read the 8 load-bearing files firsthand to
verify S70’s subsystem map against the working tree BEFORE declaring RED
— confirmed the 2 generators + 7 detection sites + the case-divergence,
rather than trusting the handoff. - **Evidence-based decision aid (→
Learning 71b):** grepped the test corpus and found the lowercase
`"u001"` in `test_obfuscateId.R:28` — the exact datum that makes
“reconcile case-sensitivity” a concrete, test-breaking fork. The
case-rule `AskUserQuestion` cited that specific test, so the owner chose
against evidence. - **New code:** `R/autoIdFormat.R` —
[`getAutoIdFormat()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getAutoIdFormat.md)
= `getOption("nprcgenekeepr.autoIdFormat", "U%04d")` +
`setAutoIdFormat(format)` (validates: single non-NA char, non-empty
literal prefix, sprintf-template-consuming-one-integer; returns previous
invisibly) \[both **exported**\]; internal `getAutoIdPrefix()` +
`isGeneratedUnknownId()` (case-sensitive, **NA-preserving like the
`startsWith`/`stri_sub` it replaces** → a true drop-in) \[`@noRd`\]. -
**Threaded through (all 7 literals + 2 generators centralized):**
`addUIds(ped, format = getAutoIdFormat())` mints via
`sprintf(format, …)` (≡ default); `obfuscateId` mints with
`getAutoIdPrefix()` + `size - nchar(prefix)` and guards via the
predicate; `removeAutoGenIds` (×3), `modPedigree.R:290` display filter,
`reportGV.R:137-140` founder counts all route detection through the
single predicate. `removeAutoGenIds`’s “use a function call” TODO
resolved (docstring updated). `qcStudbook` **unchanged** (its
`addUIds(sb)` picks up the option via the default — smallest blast
radius). `modPedigree.R:112` tooltip notes configurability. - **TDD
trail (RED→GREEN):** RED wrote 2 NEW test files (`test_autoIdFormat.R`,
`test_removeAutoGenIds.R`) + edited `test_obfuscateId.R:27-32` to the
case-sensitive contract, and **confirmed red** (new fns undefined →
error; obfuscate test fails vs current insensitive code). GREEN: the new
file + 5 edits + `devtools::document()`. **Only ONE existing test
changed** (`test_obfuscateId`);
`test_addUIds`/`test_qcStudbook`/`test_modPedigree` pass UNCHANGED = the
byte-identical back-compat proof (→ Learning 71a). - **Verification:**
**Full suite 0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip** (985 results; the 5
warnings are pre-existing `test_modPyramid.R` baseline).
**`devtools::check()` exit 0, 1 NOTE** — the `spelling.R` comparison vs
a stale `spelling.Rout.save`; attributed to **pre-existing baseline**
(`spell_check_package` + check word list flag **0** of my identifiers
among 54 words → not introduced/worsened; left as housekeeping, →
Learning 71d). Lint clean under `load_all` (the
[`options()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/options.html) setter carries
`# nolint: undesirable_function_linter` per the `getPyramidPlot.R`
precedent — a public permanent setter). Exhaustive `grep` confirmed **no
remaining auto-ID `"U"` literal** in `R/` (only the unrelated sex-factor
level M/F/U/H). **\[news-vs-changelog\] → BOTH** (user-facing feature):
`NEWS.md` NEW-48 + `CHANGELOG.md` S71 under `[Unreleased]`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **DONE via the test suite.** The
runtime-behavior changes — the `modPedigreeServer` `displayUnknownIds`

[`getAutoIdFormat()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getAutoIdFormat.md)/[`setAutoIdFormat()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/setAutoIdFormat.md);
detection = `getAutoIdPrefix()` (literal before first `%`) +
`startsWith`, **case-SENSITIVE**, NA-preserving. (3) **Prefix-only
detection still over-matches** real IDs starting with the prefix
(e.g. “Ulysses”) — SAME as before (byte-identical, by owner choice).
`removeAutoGenIds`’s TODO is satisfied only in *letter* (centralized
function call); its deeper “real U-prefixed IDs possible” goal is
**NOT** achieved and would require the declined prefix+digits option
(`^prefix[0-9]+$`). (4) **Case reconciliation:**
`reportGV`+`obfuscateId` are now case-SENSITIVE (were insensitive) —
`test_obfuscateId.R:27-32` updated accordingly (lowercase `"u001"` →
real ID); a real lowercase-`"u"` animal ID is now treated as real
everywhere (the intended fix). (5) **Out-of-scope conventions
untouched** — textual `"UNKNOWN"` (`unknown2NA`), `recordStatus`
(`addParents`/`getRecordStatusIndex`); don’t widen into them. (6)
**Pre-existing spelling NOTE** — `devtools::check()` yields 1 NOTE
(`spelling.R` vs stale `spelling.Rout.save`); 0 of my words flagged;
housekeeping candidate, not this session’s regression. (7)
**`obfuscateId` mint uses `size - nchar(prefix)`** — a multi-char
configured prefix needs `size > nchar(prefix)`. (8)
**[`setAutoIdFormat()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/setAutoIdFormat.md)
uses [`options()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/options.html) permanently**
(it IS the public setter) — carries
`# nolint: undesirable_function_linter`; do not “fix” to
[`withr::with_options`](https://withr.r-lib.org/reference/with_options.html).
(9) CLAUDE.md still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) —
stale, carried since S56. (10) The 2 untracked files remain —
build-ignored / owner’s standing keep-call. **Branch model unchanged:**
this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next
sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

### What Session 70 Did

**Deliverable:** Consolidate the ID cluster — GitHub issues **\#26 /
\#32 / \#38** (auto-generated unknown-ID format/detection) — into **one
umbrella design issue**, grounded in a firsthand subsystem map, with a
decided disposition for the three originals. **(DONE — umbrella \#44
created OPEN; \#26 + \#32 CLOSED as duplicates; \#38 kept OPEN + linked.
Open issues 18 → 17.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13 **Status:**
**DONE.** Grooming/design session (no production code or tests; **TDD
phase = N/A** declared every response — same non-code classification as
S57/S61–S67/S69). One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. No `R/`,
`tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes. - **Firsthand
subsystem map (Workflow under ultracode):** ran a 2-phase workflow —
phase 1: 4 parallel facet readers (generation / detection /
callers-config / tests); phase 2: an adversarial **completeness critic**
fed the combined inventory. The critic earned its keep: it overturned
the mappers’ claims that `addUIds` is “the only synthesizer” and that
“no centralized predicate exists,” surfacing that the ID cluster spans
**three independent unknown/auto-gen conventions** coupled only by
ordering in `qcStudbook.R:188→198→199`
(`unknown2NA → addUIds → addParents`): **(1)** textual `"UNKNOWN"`
(`unknown2NA.R`, case-insensitive); **(2)** the `"U%04d"` prefix — **2
producers** (`addUIds.R:47,54` + `obfuscateId.R:38-43`) and **7
detection sites** in 4 files with **inconsistent case-handling**
(`stri_sub`/`startsWith` sensitive vs `grepl("^U", ignore.case=TRUE)`),
**no centralized predicate**; **(3)** `recordStatus="added"`
(`addParents.R:43-61`) with the **already-centralized**
`getRecordStatusIndex()` predicate (the model \#2 should imitate). -
**Verify-before-publish (the load-bearing discipline):** before the
irreversible/outward-facing actions, re-read the **8 load-bearing files
firsthand** (`addUIds`, `removeAutoGenIds`, `addParents`,
`getRecordStatusIndex`, `obfuscateId`, `unknown2NA`, the `qcStudbook`
ordering, the `modPedigree.R:112` tooltip) to confirm the
three-convention thesis + the two-generator claim in source — did NOT
publish subagent findings unverified (→ Learning 70). - **Owner
decisions surfaced via `AskUserQuestion`** (after the owner asked to
clarify the first pass and steered “U default, configurable other
prefix”): **(Configurable)** prefix **+ number format**, default

(textual-`UNKNOWN` + `recordStatus` documented as out-of-scope);

umbrella issue only (no `docs/` note, since scope is narrow). Applied

per \#38 + an internal `isGeneratedUnknownId()` predicate replacing the

**⟢ Carried — Session 68 (implement \#35 descendants, DONE; full detail
below):**

### What Session 68 Did

**Deliverable:** Implement GitHub issue \#35 — include **descendants**
in the Pedigree Browser’s “Trim pedigree” filtering (ancestor-inclusion
was already done per S67). Strict TDD, **Option A (strict lineal)** per
owner. **(DONE — feature shipped; \#35 implemented, awaiting owner
confirm to close.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13 **Status:**
**DONE.** First real **code** session after the S57–S67 non-code run →
genuine **RED→GREEN→REFACTOR(skipped)** with all three phase gates posed
via `AskUserQuestion`, plus a separate pre-RED **Option A/B approach
decision**. **0 stakeholder corrections.** One close-out commit pending
on `add-methodology`. - **The approach (owner-chosen via
`AskUserQuestion`, pre-RED): Option A — strict lineal.** Trimmed
pedigree = focal ∪ transitive ancestors ∪ transitive descendants; **no
collaterals** (siblings/cousins/mates). (Option B = reuse
`getPedDirectRelatives`, which adds collaterals = whole connected
pedigree — declined.) - **New code:** `R/getDescendantPedigree.R` — new
**exported** transitive-offspring closure
(`repeat { getOffspring → break when nothing new }`), the downward
mirror of `getProbandPedigree`. `R/modPedigree.R:292-307` — trim block
now unions the ancestor closure
(`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative=FALSE, addBackParents=FALSE)`)
with the new descendant closure:
`ped <- ped[ped$id %in% union(ancestors$id, descendants$id), ]`. **UI
help text** (`:124-126`) changed from the “include only **relatives**”
over-promise S67 flagged → “the **ancestors and descendants** of the
focal animals”; **docstring** (`:166-167`) updated to match. - **TDD
trail (RED then GREEN):** RED wrote 9 failing assertions and CONFIRMED
red — `test_getDescendantPedigree.R` errored
(`could not find function`) + the 2 new integration tests failed
(`"GC" %in% ped$id` false under ancestors-only). GREEN: created the
helper + union edit + docstring/help-text + `devtools::document()` (man
page + NAMESPACE export) + `NEWS.md` NEW-47. - **The regression catch (→
Learning 68):** the full-suite clean-regression read surfaced a SECOND
stale test in a *different* file — `test_modPedigree.R:419` (“trims
pedigree based on focal animals”) asserted the OLD ancestors-only result
(`nrow==3`, `expect_false("D")`) for focal `{A,C}`; D is a child of
focal A → now a correctly-included descendant. Updated it to the new
contract (`nrow==4`, `{A,B,C,D}` in, half-sib E still out) with
reasoning. I’d only updated the obvious `_processing` file in RED; the
regression read (CLAUDE.md Build/Test/Verify) is the backstop that
caught the one RED missed. - **Verification:** **Full suite 972 tests /
0 failed / 0 error / 159 skip.** Lint clean — the lone
`object_usage_linter` warning (“no visible global function definition
for ‘getDescendantPedigree’”) proven an **install-staleness artifact**:
0 warnings once linted with
[`pkgload::load_all()`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
(resolves against installed namespace; the new fn is dev-only until
reinstall). \[news-vs-changelog\] → BOTH (user-facing feature):
`NEWS.md` NEW-47 + `CHANGELOG.md` S68 under `[Unreleased]`. BACKLOG.md
has no \#35/descendant ref (grep-verified — nothing to prune).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **DONE via
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html).**
The deliverable changes the `modPedigreeServer` `pedigreeData` reactive
— the 2 integration tests in `test_modPedigree_processing.R` (+ the
updated `test_modPedigree.R` trim test) drive that exact reactive
through the real server pipeline (`processedPedigree` → trim union) and
confirm the new behavior is active (descendant `GC` included,
collaterals `C`/`E` excluded). That is the deterministic runtime
verification for a Shiny-module change; no separate interactive
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)
browser launch was needed (testServer exercises the changed code path
directly).

**Gotchas:** (1) **\#35 is IMPLEMENTED but still OPEN** — close it (with
a commit pointer) only on owner confirm; the feature is done +
suite-green, so this is a clean close candidate, not re-verification
work. (2) **`getDescendantPedigree` is the new descendants-only
primitive** — `getProbandPedigree`=ancestors(up),
`getDescendantPedigree`=descendants(down),
`getPedDirectRelatives`=both+collaterals,
`getOffspring`/`getParents`=single-level. (3) **The trim is
strict-lineal by design** — a displayed descendant’s *other parent*
(mate) is intentionally NOT pulled in, so the trimmed table can show an
animal whose `sire`/`dam` isn’t a row; that is the approved Option-A
semantics, not a bug. (4) **`object_usage_linter` will warn on
`getDescendantPedigree` until the package is reinstalled** — it’s an
install-staleness artifact (0 warnings under `load_all`); don’t “fix”
it. (5) **A behavior-change feature can break tests in files you didn’t
touch** — `grep` the test corpus for the changed behavior during RED
(Learning 68); the clean-regression read is the backstop. (6) CLAUDE.md
still says “Version 1.0.8” (DESCRIPTION is `1.1.0.9000`) — stale,
carried since S56. (7) The 2 untracked files remain — build-ignored /
owner’s standing keep-call. **Branch model unchanged:** this close-out
is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR (S51
promoted prior work via PR \#41).

**⟢ Carried — Session 67 (re-scope \#35 to descendants, DONE; full
detail below):** **Task (Session 67 — 2026-06-13):** Execute S66
SUGGESTED-NEXT \#1 — **re-scope GitHub issue \#35** (“Include ancestors
and descendants in pedigree filtering”) to focus on the remaining
**descendants** work, after firsthand-verifying that ancestor-inclusion
is implemented and descendant-inclusion is not. **(DONE — \#35
re-scoped + retitled, kept OPEN.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13
**Status:** **DONE.** Issue-grooming / re-scope session (no production
code or tests written; **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response —
same non-code classification S57/S61–S66 used). One close-out commit on
`add-methodology`. **No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md`
changes.** - **The finding (firsthand — confirmed the handoff
directionally, added the depth it couldn’t):** \#35’s compound
“ancestors AND descendants” ask is **half done**. **Ancestors DONE** —
`R/modPedigree.R:292-302`: when the “Trim pedigree based on focal
animals” checkbox is on, it calls
`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative = FALSE, addBackParents = FALSE)`
→
[`getProbandPedigree()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getProbandPedigree.md)
(`R/getProbandPedigree.R:24-40`), an **upward** `sire`/`dam` closure
(ancestors only). The module docstring already says so
(`R/modPedigree.R:166`: “Optionally trimming to **ancestors**…”).
**Descendants NOT** — neither `trimPedigree` nor `getProbandPedigree`
walks downward;
[`getOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getOffspring.md)
is single-level only. - **Two stale references the inherited framing did
NOT flag (caught firsthand):** (1) the issue’s cited “Current Code”
(lines **246-253**, a `# TODO: Include ancestors and descendants`
placeholder) **no longer exists** — replaced by the live ancestor logic
at 292-302; (2) its “Suggested Implementation” called
`trimPedigree(focalIds(), ped, ancestors = TRUE, descendants = TRUE)` —
**that signature does not exist**
(`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative, addBackParents)`).
Copying the suggested call forward would have handed the implementer a
non-compiling spec (→ Learning 67(a)). - **The semantic fork surfaced
(not silently picked):** “include descendants” has two non-equivalent
implementations — **(A) strict lineal**: add a downward closure
mirroring `getProbandPedigree`’s loop (repeated
[`getOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getOffspring.md)
to closure), union with the ancestor set = focal’s ancestors+descendants
only; **(B) reuse
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)**
(`R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:46-59`), which already loops
[`getParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getParents.md)
AND
[`getOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getOffspring.md)
to closure but thereby also pulls in **collateral** relatives
(sibs/cousins/mates) = the whole connected pedigree, broader than
lineal. The package has the both-directions primitive but **no
transitive descendants-only helper**. - **Applied (owner-chosen via
`AskUserQuestion`, 2 questions):** **FORM = rewrite the body** + retitle
to “Include descendants in pedigree filtering (ancestors already
implemented)” + dated verification comment; **SCOPE = document BOTH
options** as an open design choice (left for the implementation
session). Did `gh issue edit 35 --title … --body-file` +
`gh issue comment 35` (comment `#issuecomment-4699260833`). **Issue kept
OPEN.** - **Verified the edit landed:** title updated + `state: open`
re-read via `gh api`; 5 grep checks on the saved body all PASS (cites
live 292-302; notes the non-existent `ancestors=,descendants=` API; both
Option A + Option B present; references `getPedDirectRelatives`; notes
the stale 246-253 placeholder is gone). Also noted the UI help text
already over-promises (“…include only **relatives** of the focal
animals”, `R/modPedigree.R:125`) while behavior is ancestors-only —
flagged in the body as a label-alignment item. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG only (S67 entry under
`[Unreleased]`; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release
note → NOT `NEWS.md`; **BACKLOG.md has no \#35 / descendants reference**
— verified by grep, nothing to prune). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** explicit
`git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` +
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked:
`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **N/A and stated** — no runtime
behavior changed (edited a GitHub issue + build-ignored docs; no `R/`
code). The runtime facts the re-scope documents ARE verified, though:
the ancestors-done / descendants-not partition was read directly off the
live call chain in the working-tree source (`modPedigree.R` →
`trimPedigree` → `getProbandPedigree`;
`getOffspring`/`getParents`/`getPedDirectRelatives`), not from a stale
install.

proved cleanliness up front (blob-equality on 3 files +
non-overlapping-hunk analysis + 0-marker dry run) and confirmed
keep-both afterward, so the “true merge commit” was a known-clean
outcome, not a hope. **Re-read my own rewritten operating file**
(SESSION_RUNNER §3C, which this merge changed) before close-out per the
prompt’s caution, and routed Learning 66 to PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md (the
post-merge adopter rule), NOT the synced table — the change I merged
governed the close-out it was part of. **Right-sized SOLO** under
ultracode: a single-commit wording merge with explicit grep verification
is mechanical/already-verified-by-design — a Workflow fan-out would be
ceremony (ultracode ≠ orchestrate-everything). **Scope discipline:**
merged + verified + deleted the branch and nothing else; did NOT touch
the 2 untracked artifacts or the stale CLAUDE.md version; did NOT push
(prior sessions’ close-outs don’t push — `add-methodology` is ahead of
origin by 19 now). **Stakeholder corrections: 1, but zero-rework** — the
mid-flight “S65 also closed” note; my analysis had already used live
HEAD (= S65) for merge-base/dry-run, so the correction confirmed
currency rather than requiring a change (captured as Learning 66(c)).
Clean, fully-verified, 1-no-op-correction deliverable → honest 8. Met
the S57–S65 discipline bar.

**⟢ Carried — Session 65 (update \#37, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 65 — 2026-06-12):** Execute S64 SUGGESTED-NEXT \#1 —
**update GitHub issue \#37** (“Exported functions not currently used by
app”): firsthand-verify its 70-function inventory against current code,
strike the now-resolved rows, keep the still-accurate unused inventory.
**(DONE — \#37 UPDATED, kept open.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-12 **Status:** **DONE.** Issue-grooming session (no production
code or tests written; **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response —
same classification S57/S61/S62/S63/S64 used). One close-out commit on
`add-methodology`. **No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md`
changes.** - **The finding — far staler than the handoff anticipated:**
S64 (inheriting the S62 audit) framed \#37 as “strike the resolved
Shiny-module + genotype rows; keep the still-accurate inventory” (≈7
functions). A from-scratch call-graph reachability recomputation found
**45 of the 70 listed functions are now used by the app** — the
inventory was ~64% stale, not 2 clusters. **Verified totals (current
code): 155 exports / 116 used / 39 unused** (the issue claimed 108 / 38
/ 70). - **Method (firsthand, reproducible):**
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html),
seed the call graph at
`{runModularApp, runGeneKeepR, appUI, appServer}`, take the transitive
closure of `codetools::findGlobals(f)$functions ∩ package-functions`;
exported names outside the closure = unused. Produced concrete call
paths for the flipped set (e.g. `rankSubjects`:
`appServer → modGeneticValueServer → reportGV → orderReport → rankSubjects`;
the 4 Shiny modules called directly by `appServer`; the genotype trio
`getGenotypes`/`addGenotype`/`hasGenotype` via `modInputServer`;
`filterKinMatrix/Report/Threshold` via the breeding-group + GV modules).
Confirmed all 22 “still unused” are genuinely unreachable. -
**Adversarial check of the static method’s blind spots:** findGlobals
misses dynamic + S3 dispatch, so I checked both explicitly: the ONLY
string-dispatched call in `R/` is `do.call("rbind", …)` (base), and
**none of the 22 still-unused names appear as a string literal anywhere
in `R/`** → no dynamic invocation the closure missed; the 3 S3 methods
(`print.summary.*`, `summary.nprcgenekeeprErr`) aren’t dispatched on the
app path (the app’s [`summary()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/summary.html)
calls hit `summary.default` on numeric vectors). Classification held:
**45 flipped / 22 unused / 3 S3 = 70.** - **Applied (owner-chosen via
`AskUserQuestion`):** **strikethrough-in-place + dated verification
note** in the body (`gh issue edit 37 --body-file`) — struck the 45
now-used rows (verified: exactly 45 `~~…~~` pairs in the saved body;
`obfuscateId` unstruck, `rankSubjects` struck), corrected the counts +
the 5 “Notable findings”, and **folded in the 17 newer unused exports**
(added since 2026-01-25: the sim-kinship subsystem, `modORIPReporting*`,
`calcFE`/`calcFG`, `makeFounderStatsTable`/`makeGeneticSummaryTable`,
`getAncestors`, `create_wkbk`, `savePlotToFile`, `logModuleEvent`,
`safeExecute`) so the inventory is complete (39 total). Posted a
timeline pointer comment (`#issuecomment-4696756359`) so the silent body
edit is visible to watchers. **Issue kept OPEN** — the 39
genuinely-unused exports remain the actionable surface. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG only (S65 entry under
`[Unreleased]`; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release
note → NOT `NEWS.md`; BACKLOG.md has no \#37 reference — open work lives
in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** explicit
`git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` +
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked:
`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **N/A and stated** — no runtime
behavior changed (edited a GitHub issue + build-ignored docs; no `R/`
code). The runtime fact the issue documents IS verified, though:
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
loaded the package cleanly and the reachability closure is computed
against the working-tree source, so the “used by the app” set reflects
the live call graph, not a stale install.

**⟢ Carried — Session 64 (verify + close \#8, DONE; full detail
below):** **Task (Session 64 — 2026-06-12):** Execute S63 SUGGESTED-NEXT
\#1 — verify issue \#8 (“Improve handling of non-founder animals without
either parents assigned”) firsthand against current code, then **CLOSE
\#8 with a caveat note**. **(DONE — \#8 CLOSED, strengthened caveat.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-12 **Status:** **DONE.**
Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written; **TDD
phase = N/A** declared every response — same classification S57/S61/S63
used closing \#30/#34/#14). One close-out commit on `add-methodology`.
**No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md` changes.** - **The
finding — re-graded from the audit’s footnote-caveat to a STRENGTHENED
caveat (with a reproduction):** \#8’s two proposed solutions are
genuinely implemented, BUT firsthand verification + a direct
reproduction showed the fix is gated on the optional `origin` column and
**silently recreates the original bug in the default (no-`origin`)
case** — plus the issue’s 2021 simulated-kinship thread is orphaned (the
audit never mentioned it). The owner picked the **strengthened-caveat
close** via `AskUserQuestion`. - **What’s implemented (verified
firsthand):** **Solution 1 (origin/“From Center” segregation)** —
ONPRC-born founders with no offspring are split into a `noParentage`
bucket: `R/orderReport.R:31,44-54`
(`is.na(rpt$origin) & totalOffspring==0L & id %in% founders`), placed
last. **Solution 2 (don’t rank them)** — `R/rankSubjects.R:38` sets
`value="Undetermined"`, `:44` sets `rank=NA`. **Live** —
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
→ `orderReport(finalData, ped)` (`R/reportGV.R:146`); `finalData`
carries `origin` only when the source ped does (`R/reportGV.R:119`
`intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped))`). **Green** —
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
runs: `test_orderReport.R` **8/0/0/0**, `test_rankSubjects.R`
**5/0/0/0**. - **The caveats (firsthand, beyond the audit’s
one-liner):** **(1) origin-gating silently no-ops — REPRODUCED.** Whole
`noParentage` block wrapped in `if ("origin" %in% names(rpt))`
(`R/orderReport.R:31`); `origin` is documented optional
(`R/getPossibleCols.R:29`, `R/qcStudbook.R:34`), absent from most
bundled datasets. I built a synthetic ped+rpt (a no-parent founder, 0
offspring, high gu) and ran `orderReport()`: *without* `origin` →
`value="High Value", rank=1` (the exact \#8 bug, no warning); *with*
`origin` (NA for the ONPRC animal) → `"Undetermined"/NA`. **(2) No
regression pin** — `test_orderReport.R:15-26` runs the origin path but
asserts only positional unknown-ID counts; `test_rankSubjects.R` never
exercises `noParentage`; the only “Undetermined” in tests is a comment
in a usually-skipped e2e (`test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:109`).
**(3) Orphaned thread B** — the 2021 simulated-kinship subsystem
(`getPotentialParents`, `createSimKinships`, `kinshipMatricesToKValues`,
`summarizeKinshipValues`, `countKinshipValues`, `addKinshipValueCount`,
`cumulateSimKinships`) is exported but has NO live caller in `R/` (only
tests + `inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R` +
`vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd`); the issue’s “discuss with Matt …
display/use simulated kinship coefficients” design item is unresolved.
All three documented in the close comment as candidate future
enhancements; **none filed** (new-issue creation outside the approved
“close with caveat” scope — S63 lesson; I did not even attempt it this
time). - **Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute workflow
`wf_f37f1b72-b6a`, `Explore` skeptics vs the working tree):** **SPLIT —
1 hold-open + 2 close-with-caveat — reconciled by REPRODUCTION, not
vote.** (i) headline-implemented → `refuted=true`, hold-open (high): the
origin-gating makes the fix illusory in the common case; claimed to have
reproduced a no-parent founder ranked “High Value”. I reconstructed it
myself (the table above) → **upheld**, which is what upgraded the close
from clean→strengthened-caveat. (ii) test-authenticity →
`refuted=false`: no assertion pins the branch → **upheld**, caveat not
blocker. (iii) intent-completeness → `refuted=false`: simulated-kinship
subsystem orphaned (also surfaced the vignette + example file) →
**upheld**, severable. The lone dissenter was the decisive one —
verifying it firsthand beat tallying 2-of-3. - **Closed:**
`gh issue comment 8 --body-file` + `gh issue close 8` with the
strengthened-caveat resolution comment (both solutions implemented + the
reproduced origin-gating failure + the missing test + the orphaned
thread B + why-close-anyway). Verified `state=closed`,
`closed_at=2026-06-12T22:10:27Z`. **\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG
only (S64 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process/close history, not a
user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`; BACKLOG.md has no \#8
reference — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** explicit `git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` +
`CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing
untracked: `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **N/A and stated** — no runtime

firsthand reproduction drove `orderReport()` end-to-end and showed both

**⟢ Carried — Session 63 (verify + close \#14, DONE; full detail
below):** **Task (Session 63 — 2026-06-12):** Execute S62 audit
recommendation \#1 — verify \#14 (“Add ability to provide genotypes for
animals within the pedigree and track them”) is genuinely resolved
against the current code, then **CLOSE issue \#14**. **(DONE — \#14
CLOSED, caveated.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-12 **Status:**
**DONE.** Verify-and-close session (no production code or tests written;
**TDD phase = N/A** declared every response — same classification
S57/S61 used closing \#30/#34). One close-out commit on
`add-methodology`. **No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md`
changes.** - **The finding — re-graded from the audit’s “clean” to
CAVEATED:** \#14’s genotype provide+track **ability is genuinely
implemented and test-pinned**, BUT firsthand verification (deeper than
the audit’s per-issue classifier) surfaced a deliberate limitation that
made it an \#8-grade close-with-caveat rather than a clean close. The
owner picked the **caveated close** via `AskUserQuestion`. - **What’s
implemented (verified firsthand):** **Provide** — the modular app’s
separate-genotype-file mode reads via `getGenotypes`, validates via
`checkGenotypeFile` (degrade-to-NULL), integer-codes + merges via
`addGenotype` (`R/modInput.R:384-396`); same functions exported for
scripting. **Track** — integer `first`/`second` ride the cleaned
studbook into
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
→ `getGVGenotype` (`R/reportGV.R:78`) → `geneDrop(genotype=…)` (`:92`) →
`calcGU`/`calcFG`. **Live** —
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)
→ `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)` → `modInputUI/Server("dataInput")`
(`appUI.R:123`, `appServer.R:104`); module exposes
`cleanedStudbook`/`genotypeData` reactives. **Pinned** — I ran the
genotype tests via
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html):
**278 assertions, 0 fail / 0 err / 0 skip**, incl. the end-to-end
`modInputServer` separate-file test
(`test_modInput_qcStudbook.R:536-545` asserts `hasGenotype(cleaned)`,
integer `first`/`second`, populated `genotypeData()`). The **0 skips**
are load-bearing (the `testServer` tests
`skip_if_not_installed("shiny")`; shiny is installed → they actually
drove the module). - **The caveat (deliberate, owner-authored):**
`commonPedGenoFile` (combined ped+geno file) UI mode does **not**
integer-code string alleles → those genotypes never satisfy
[`hasGenotype()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/hasGenotype.md)
/ reach gene-drop. This is **at parity with the legacy monolith** and
explicitly intentional per the owner’s own commit `c9019d51`: *“neither
app integer-codes string alleles for a combined file, so common-mode
genotypes never reach gene-drop in either.”* Documented in the close
comment; combined-file tracking noted there as a candidate future
enhancement (NOT filed — new-issue creation was outside the approved
“close with caveat” scope; the auto-mode classifier correctly denied my
attempt to `gh issue create`). - **Adversarial close gate (3-lens refute
workflow `wf_9d00a37a-6d0`):** **1 confirmed, 2 refuted — and I
reconciled EACH verdict firsthand.** (i) live-path → `refuted=false`
(matched my read). (ii) intent-completeness → `refuted=true`, REAL →
this surfaced the combined-file caveat → re-graded the close. (iii)
test-authenticity → `refuted=true`, but an **ARTIFACT**: the agent
decompiled the **stale installed binary** (missing the merge block)
instead of the source; my `load_all` run (90/0/0/0) + my read of
`modInput.R:384-396` proved the source is correct and green. Agents
inspect what’s installed; the parent runs `load_all` against the working
tree. - **Closed:** `gh issue close 14` with a resolution comment
(implemented-ability evidence + the parity caveat + the
firsthand/adversarial verification). Verified `state=closed`,
`closed_at=2026-06-12T21:48:24Z`. **\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG
only (S63 entry under `[Unreleased]`; dev-process/close history, not a
user-facing release note → NOT `NEWS.md`; BACKLOG.md has no \#14
reference — open work lives in GitHub Issues, nothing to prune).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** explicit `git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` +
`CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing
untracked: `..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **N/A and stated** — no runtime
behavior changed (closed a GitHub issue + edited build-ignored docs; no
`R/` code). The runtime behavior \#14 concerns IS verified, though: the
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
tests actually drove `modInputServer` end-to-end with the genotype-merge
path (0 skips) — a stronger, cheaper gate than a manual app boot.

outward-facing close). Its gotchas held: `gh issue view` can print empty
(I used `gh api` throughout); the Workflow-`args`-non-array trap (I
avoided it by not passing array args); reports belong under `docs/`.

intent-completeness depth the audit’s right-sized sweep didn’t.

environment (TTY/pager) — use

‖ test-authenticity, each an `Explore` skeptic told to find a reason NOT
to close) → **all `refuted=false`, high confidence, 0 gaps**;

the agents. - **Closed:** `gh issue close 34` with a resolution comment
(placeholder→real integration, commits, firsthand verification, the pin
test, the 3-lens result). Verified `state=closed`,
`closed_at=2026-06-12T20:52:27Z`. **\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG

**Key files (this session):** `.Rbuildignore` (cran-comments
consolidation in the dated-files block near `:10`; new
tooling/audit/notes block appended at EOF, `:81-88`); `CHANGELOG.md`

**Gotchas:** (1) **`.Rbuildignore` is a REGEX file** — every line
incl. `#` comments is a perl regex; an unbalanced paren ABORTS
`R CMD build`. Keep comments balanced/paren-free; re-build after ANY
edit (carried, S59). (2) **zsh `nomatch` ABORTS on an unmatched glob** —
`rm -f <glob>` with no match kills the whole script (even with `-f`);
prefix `unsetopt nomatch 2>/dev/null; setopt null_glob 2>/dev/null`
before any cleanup glob. (3) **Dated cran-comments are now covered by
one `^[0-9]+_cran-comments\.md$`** — do NOT re-add per-date exact lines;
a new dated file is auto-ignored. NOTE: this tight pattern does NOT
dupe-guard (`20250504_cran-comments 2.md` would NOT be ignored) —
intentional (cran-comments aren’t macOS-synced). (4) **Tight vs broad
patterns:**
`^methodology_dashboard\.py$`/`^dashboard\.html$`/`^nprcgenekeepr_notes\.txt$`
are exact (no dupe-guard); the `<NAME>.*` ones (`RECOMMENDED_SKILLS`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT`, `TECH_DEBT_AUDIT`) ARE dupe-guarded — matched to whether
the file is sync-prone. (5) **`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` remains
untracked on disk** (now build-ignored) — owner’s standing keep/remove
call; `..Rcheck/00check.log` is build-ignored check residue. (6)
DESCRIPTION is `1.1.0.9000` (CLAUDE.md’s “1.0.8” is stale) — glob
`nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz`. (7) Verify any `.Rbuildignore` change at the
BUILD level (stage dummy → `R CMD build` → `tar tzf`), NOT via
`devtools::check()` and NOT by eye. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

added an inline NOTE — see the finding below. - **Owner decisions
(`AskUserQuestion`, before editing):** (1) **pattern style = loose
`.*`** (matches the S58 line; over `( [0-9]+| copy)?` targeted form);
(2) **scope = the already-ignored methodology cluster only** (pure
behavior-none — those 7 are already excluded; only their dupe forms get
newly covered). Explicitly DEFERRED adding currently-UNignored docs
(`RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md`, audit files) — that would change tarball
contents + shrink the top-level-files NOTE, a DIFFERENT change. -
**In-flight finding (caught by the build step, fixed, NOT shipped):**
**every line in `.Rbuildignore` is a perl regex — including `#`
comments** (they only “work” because each is a valid regex matching no
real path). My first multi-line comment held an unbalanced `(` →
`R CMD build` aborted with `invalid regular expression`. Fixed to
balanced/paren-free comment lines + an inline NOTE warning future
editors. (Learning 59b.) - **Verification (firsthand, both levels):**
(a) **RED-equivalent regex probe** across all 7 names (`grepl`,
ignore.case, perl — mirrors R CMD build’s case-insensitive relative-path
match): OLD exact patterns MISS every dupe form (the leak); NEW `.*`
form matches `<NAME> 2.md` AND `<NAME> copy.md`, does NOT over-match
`<NAME>.Rmd`/`<NAME>_archive.txt`, canonical names stay excluded — all 7
OK. (b) **Build-equivalent (authoritative):** staged **14 real spaced
dupes** (both forms × 7 names), ran
`R CMD build --no-build-vignettes --no-manual .` (RC=0); the resulting
`nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz` (693 files) contained **zero** of the
7 names as `.md` → all dupes + canonicals excluded, real content
(DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE) present. `trap cleanup EXIT` removed all 14
dupes + the tarball; `git status` clean except the intended mods + the 2
pre-existing untracked (`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).
Did NOT run a full `devtools::check()` — the WARNING is a pure function
of tarball contents (verified directly); S58 established the
dupe→WARNING link firsthand and self-dinged the redundant check. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG only (Session 59 entry under
`[Unreleased]`; dev-process history, not a user-facing release note →
NOT `NEWS.md`). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** no dupe present this session;
explicit `git add` of only `.Rbuildignore` + `SESSION_NOTES.md` +
`CHANGELOG.md` + `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded `..Rcheck/`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test):** **N/A and stated** — no runtime
behavior changed. Edited only `.Rbuildignore` (build config; not
shipped/installed) and docs (CHANGELOG/PROJECT_LEARNINGS/SESSION_NOTES).
No `R/` code → no app boot; the authoritative gate is the clean
`R CMD build` + tarball inspection above.

**Gotchas:** (1) **`.Rbuildignore` is a REGEX file** — every line
incl. `#` “comments” is a perl regex; a comment with an unbalanced `(`
ABORTS `R CMD build` (`invalid regular expression`). Keep comments
balanced/paren-free (there’s now an inline NOTE saying so); re-build
after ANY `.Rbuildignore` edit. (2) This environment’s shell is **zsh**
— `for n in $VAR` does NOT word-split an unquoted scalar (bash does);
use an explicit literal list. (3) The dupe-WARNING class is now covered
for the **8** `.Rbuildignore`-listed methodology `.md` files
(SESSION_NOTES + the 7); it is NOT covered for top-level docs that
aren’t in `.Rbuildignore` at all (`RECOMMENDED_SKILLS.md`, audit files)
— a dupe of THOSE would still WARN; see SUGGESTED-NEXT (1). (4) Verify
any `.Rbuildignore` change at the BUILD level (stage dummy →
`R CMD build` → `tar tzf`), NOT via `devtools::check()` and NOT by eye
(S58 gotcha, still true). (5) DESCRIPTION is `1.1.0.9000` (CLAUDE.md’s
“1.0.8” is stale) — glob `nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz`. **Branch model
unchanged:** this close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on
the next sync/PR (S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

**FALSE** (the leak path); NEW matches all of them TRUE and over-matches
nothing (`CHANGELOG.md`, `SESSION_NOTES_archive.txt` both FALSE —

**Build-equivalent (authoritative for THIS WARNING):** staged a real
empty `SESSION_NOTES 2.md`, ran

the resulting `nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz` → **zero** SESSION_NOTES
entries (693 files total) → the dupe is excluded, so R CMD check’s

`devtools::check()`: the WARNING is a pure function of tarball contents
(verified directly), no R code/metadata affecting check changed, and S57
already established the dupe→WARNING link firsthand — a full check would
repeat S57’s self-flagged over-check ding. Temp dupe + tarball removed;
`git status` clean except the intended mods + 2 pre-existing untracked
(`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`). -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG only (Session 58 entry under

NOT `NEWS.md`). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** no dupe present this session

below):** **Task (Session 56 — started 2026-06-11, completed
2026-06-12):** IMPLEMENT Phase 4 of the issue \#30 plan (§5 Phase 4) —

REFACTORs + 2 RED→GREEN→REFACTOR slices). **(DONE — all 6 removed; only
`makeGeneticDiversityDashboard` remains excluded; `lint_package()` =
0.)** **Started:** 2026-06-11 · **Completed:** 2026-06-12 **Status:**
**DONE.** Two commits on `add-methodology`: **`17e3fa06`**
(`checkRequiredCols` RED→GREEN→REFACTOR) + **`69c8d759`** (5 refactors +
de-exclude). The §4 line-specific-exclusion deliverable of \#30 is now
COMPLETE — every R-file line exclusion is gone except the
deliberately-kept `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard`. -
**`checkRequiredCols.R` (#1, genuine RED→GREEN→REFACTOR):** RED test
pins the owner-chosen robust NA contract
(`c("dam","sex","birth",NA)`→`c("id","sire")`;
`c("id",NA)`→`c("sire","dam","sex","birth")` with reportErrors=TRUE —
errored before with `"missing value where TRUE/FALSE needed"`). GREEN =
`requiredCols[!requiredCols %in% cols]` + delete the `.lintr` entry.
REFACTOR = `@details` documents the intentional robust NA behavior +
`document()`. Non-NA byte-identical; reportErrors=FALSE untouched. - **3
fully behavior-none REFACTORs:** `correctParentSex.R` (if/else inverted
to a guard clause — report body falls through),
`fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R` (`else { if }`→`else if`, inline
`# nolint` deleted), `setExit.R` (`mapply`→`unlist(Map(...))` —
`chooseDate` always length-1). Each fix + de-exclude in the same change
(\[lint-net-zero\]). - **`addSexAndAgeToGroup.R` (#11) — DOWNGRADED from
the plan’s RED slice to a REFACTOR, then ADOPTED-ROBUST:** my firsthand
probe proved `sapply`→`ped$sex[match(ids,ped$id)]` behavior-none on the
documented domain (the `age` vapply already errors identically on any
≠1-row id, so the planned
[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html)-error concern doesn’t
occur). BUT the adversarial pass found it diverges on **empty `ids`**:
old `sapply` dropped the `sex` column (2-col result) → CRASHED the sole
caller `modBreedingGroups.R:438` (`colnames(gp) <- c(<3 names>)`); new
keeps the documented 3-col schema → empty table renders. **Owner ADOPTED
the new behavior** (latent crash-fix + matches `@return`); pinned by an
empty-ids contract test + a happy-path characterization test; `@details`
documents it. - **`create_wkbk.R` (#13) — ACCEPTED cosmetic
divergence:** inner `if(replace)`→`if(!replace)` guard clause. On a
non-logical non-coercible `replace` while the file exists, both error
but the message text differs
(`"argument is not interpretable as logical"`→`"invalid argument type"`);
`replace` is documented logical, coercible values identical. **Owner
ACCEPTED** (out-of-contract; no clean lint-passing rewrite preserves the
message). - **Verification (all firsthand/authoritative):**
`lint_package()` = **0**; the 6 files lint-clean
(`parse_settings=FALSE`); full suite **0 fail / 0 err / 159 skip** (S49
baseline + 5 new passing expectations → zero regression);
`devtools::check()` **0 errors** (1 WARNING + 2 NOTEs — all pre-existing
stray-file/clock-skew, chiefly the macOS `SESSION_NOTES 2.md` dupe; NOT
mine). **Adversarial verification** `wf_168f8dcf-1e5` (6 skeptics, each
told to REFUTE + run R): 4 fully equivalent (6000-iter fuzz / 146 seeded
/ 21 / ~50 inputs), the 2 divergences owner-reviewed. **Phase-3E:**
`addSexAndAgeToGroup`’s runtime path is covered by
`test_modBreedingGroups.R:1015-1122` (the `viewGrp` member view →
`c("Ego ID","Sex","Age in Years")` colnames), green in the full suite —
no app boot needed (pure fn). **Process slip (self-caught):** committed
`17e3fa06` after adding `@details` without re-linting → an 81-char line
landed in it, fixed-forward in `69c8d759`. **\[news-vs-changelog\]** →
CHANGELOG only. **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** explicit `git add` (excluded
`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`, `SESSION_NOTES 2.md`). \#30
stays OPEN (close decision below).

**Session 55 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 56): Score 9/10.** What
helped enormously: S55’s handoff named Phase 4 with the EXACT 6 files +
dispositions (4 verified-safe + 2 slices), the precise per-file fixes,
the plan reference, and the gotchas (the `parse_settings=FALSE` probe —
load-bearing again; \[lint-net-zero\] fix-and-de-exclude; always
reconcile a sub-probe count against the real `lint_package()`; the
`checkRequiredCols` casing already fixed S55; the macOS-dupe WARNING).
Orientation was fast and every named fact held. The −1: S55 (following
the plan) listed BOTH `addSexAndAgeToGroup` AND `checkRequiredCols` as
RED slices, citing for `addSexAndAge` “sapply→vapply changes error
semantics” — but the real situation is subtler:
[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html) is behavior-none on the
documented domain (the age-vapply guard), and the ACTUAL divergence is
on EMPTY ids (a shape/column-drop the plan never mentioned). The “RED
slice” caution was warranted but for a different, unstated reason —
neither S55 nor the plan flagged the empty-ids case (it took the
adversarial pass). Not misleading — S55’s general
verify-first/adversarial discipline is exactly what surfaced it. Also
gotcha \#5 (“none of Phase 4’s files are Shiny runtime”) was slightly
off (`addSexAndAgeToGroup` IS called by `modBreedingGroups`), but the
spirit (unit/module suite is the gate) held. ROI: very high.

**Self-assessment (Session 56): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard, git, issues)
before any work; claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE
technical work. Declared the TDD phase every response; gated the PRE-RED
scope decisions + every PRE-RED→RED / RED→GREEN / GREEN→REFACTOR
transition via `AskUserQuestion` with exact actions spelled out.
**Verify-first was load-bearing twice:** (1) before classifying,
firsthand-probed both “RED” slices — correctly downgrading
`addSexAndAge` to behavior-none on the documented domain and confirming
`checkRequiredCols` genuine RED, AND catching that my own first
`checkRequiredCols` NA probe used the wrong length-case (masking the
divergence) → re-probed to nail the exact trigger; (2) ran a 6-skeptic
adversarial workflow AFTER my firsthand checks — which caught TWO
divergences my probes MISSED (empty-ids column-drop + abuse-input
error-message), then I REPRODUCED both firsthand and TRACED the caller
(proving empty-ids is a latent crash-fix) before surfacing them. Honored
the strict-TDD contract: did NOT silently ship the refutations — paused,
surfaced both via `AskUserQuestion` with caller-traced consequences;
owner chose adopt/accept. AUTHORITATIVE gate (lint=0, suite=baseline,
check=0-errors) run by me, not trusted from agents. Checkpoint-committed
the RED slice before the 5-file batch (SAFEGUARDS ≤5-files-uncommitted +
commit-before-mode-switch). **Stakeholder corrections: 0** (4
`AskUserQuestion` gates + 2 finding-decisions, all answered as posed).
**Dings:** (a) I MISSED the empty-ids divergence in my OWN firsthand
probe (only happy/missing/dup) — the skeptic caught it; my “fully
behavior-preserving” claim for `addSexAndAge` was premature (should have
probed `character(0)` myself). (b) Committed `17e3fa06` without
re-linting after the `@details` add → an 81-char line slipped in
(fixed-forward, but the checkpoint wasn’t clean). Both captured as
Learning 56. Met/exceeded the S47–S55 bar.

before any work; claimed the session with a Phase 1B stub BEFORE
technical work. Phase 1 = clean behavior-none REFACTOR, verified and
committed ALONE as a boundary before the architect-mode residual work
(SAFEGUARDS commit-before-scope-switch). When the owner expanded scope,
honored it as a deliberate expansion (not scope creep) and surfaced the
two genuine author decisions via `AskUserQuestion` per the plan.
Right-sized the orchestration: a per-file editor→adversarial-verifier
workflow for 18 files (the parallel structure + built-in adversarial
verification fit the breadth), with the AUTHORITATIVE verification done
by me, not trusted from agents. **Verified, didn’t assume:** reconciled
the agents’ lint claims against the real `lint_package()` CI command
(caught the spurious `coalesce_linter`), spot-checked EVERY structural
transform myself (switch conversion, sapply→vapply, return removal,
if/else bracing, and TWO logic-bearing modInput string-wraps — chasing
each apparent “truncation” to confirm it was a clean wrap), and ran the
full gate (lint + suite + check + man-consistency + runtime app boot).
**Stakeholder corrections: 0.** Dings: (a) the workflow’s FIRST launch
failed instantly on an args-as-string bug — my scripting error, fixed +
relaunched with 0 agents wasted; (b) I relied on agents for the 150+
edits rather than doing them all myself — defensible given the scale +
adversarial verify + my spot-checks + global verification, but it means
I trust the workflow’s breadth; (c) one editor correctly edited
`inst/shinytest/app.R` (outside `R/`) and flagged a constraint I’d
written too narrowly. Met/exceeded the S47–S53 bar.

**⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = owner decision on \#30 (plan §9 \#4), then (if
continuing) Phase 3 of the plan.** The CI `lint` check is now GREEN
(`lint_package()` = 0), so **\#30’s “many lint remain” is resolved**.
BUT the green relies on **~12 `.lintr` line-exclusions that still
EXIST** (suppressing ~28 lints in the Phase 3/4 files) — the plan wanted
most of those FIXED, not left excluded. So two paths: **(A)** the owner
judges the green lint check sufficient and **closes \#30**, filing the
remaining exclusion cleanup as a follow-up; or **(B) continue the plan:
Phase 3** (behavior-none exclusion files — `checkRequiredCols.R` \[+ the
`.lintr` casing bug `CheckRequiredCols`→`checkRequiredCols`\],
`getLkDirectAncestors.R` \[rename local `source` var\],
`getLkDirectRelatives.R`, `saveDataframesAsFiles.R`,
`convertFromCenter.R`, `fillGroupMembers.R`, `hasGenotype.R`), **then
Phase 4** (behavior-sensitive: `correctParentSex.R`, `create_wkbk.R`,
`fillGroupMembersWithSexRatio.R`, `setExit.R` + the ONE
RED→GREEN→REFACTOR: `addSexAndAgeToGroup.R`
`sapply`→[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html) with a contract
test — plan §4B \#11 / §6 dragon \#4), after which only
`makeGeneticDiversityDashboard` (kept) remains → then close \#30. **Key
files:** the plan (full §4/§5/§6/§8/§9); `.lintr:13-31` (remaining
exclusions); `/tmp/lint_dump.R` + `/tmp/lint_phase1.R` +
`/tmp/wf_residual_lint.mjs` (this session’s reusable lint dump /
per-file lint / the fan-out workflow — TRANSIENT, regenerate if needed).
**Gotchas:** (1) ALWAYS reconcile any agent/sub-probe lint count against
the real `lintr::lint_package()` — a `parse_settings=FALSE` probe can
fire linters NOT in the project tag set (the `coalesce_linter` false
alarm). (2) `implicit_integer` is KEPT ON now — new code must type
literals (`L` for counts, `.0` for reals). (3) After ANY roxygen edit
run `devtools::document()` and commit the regenerated `man/`
(R-CMD-check enforces code/doc match). (4) Phase-3E for Shiny runtime
edits = BOOT the app from `load_all` (the unit suite doesn’t exercise
the mod servers). (5) `inst/shinytest` is NOT `.lintr`-excluded →
`inst/shinytest/app.R` IS linted. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR.

**⟢ Carried — Session 53 (PLAN \#30 lintr cleanup, DONE; full detail
below):** **Task (Session 53 — 2026-06-11):** PLAN the cleanup of issue
\#30 (lintr) — examine each `file = line` entry in `.lintr`’s
`exclusions: list()` (18 R-file line-specific suppressions), identify
the specific suppressed lint at each, classify fix-vs-keep, and write a
plan to `docs/planning/` to fix most/all of them. (DONE — plan
delivered) **Started:** 2026-06-11 **Status:** **DONE. Deliverable =
`docs/planning/issue30-lintr-exclusion-cleanup-plan.md`** (planning
session; NO `R/`/`tests/`/`.lintr` content changed — implementation is
the subsequent sessions, FM \#18/#25). **Evidence base (firsthand):**
`lint_package(parse_settings=FALSE)` bypassing the `.lintr` exclusions =
**41 lints suppressed by the 18 line-excludes + 152 residual = 193 total
in `R/`**; cross-checked by an 18-file parallel examination workflow
(`wf_c7863094-8f1`, 28 agents — one examiner per excluded file proposing
the EXACT fix + behavior-change risk, then an adversarial verifier on
every behavior-affecting fix and every commented-code deletion). **THREE
agent conclusions were corrected** by adversarial verification / my own
reproduction (the value of verify-first): (1) examine said “delete the
all-commented `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`” → verifier caught it
reverses an explicit author won’t-delete decision (NEW-20,
`.Rbuildignore`’d, would trigger namespace fallout \#35) →
**KEEP-EXCLUDE**; (2) examine said `set_seed.R:11` exclusion is stale →
verifier proved removing it alone RE-FIRES the lint (a stray trailing
`#'` that also leaks into `man/set_seed.Rd` fools
`commented_code_linter`) → **FIX = strip the `#'`**; (3) a
`getLkDirectAncestors.R` agent (ran `lint()` with `.lintr` active → the
exclusion hid the lints) wrongly called L26/29/35 stale → my
`parse_settings=FALSE` repro proved `undesirable_function_linter` flags
the local variable named `source` (it flags the SYMBOL shadowing
[`base::source`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/source.html)) → **FIX = rename
the variable**. **Dispositions of the 18 entries:** FIX 15 (~38 lints —
10 behavior-none, 5 low-risk verified-safe), KEEP-EXCLUDE 1
(`makeGeneticDiversityDashboard`), REMOVE-STALE+fix-real 1
(`getPyramidPlot.R = 25:27` suppresses 0 lints; real lints at
L16/38/41-43). **4 config bugs found:** (a) `.lintr` lists
`"R/CheckRequiredCols.R"` wrong-case → misses on case-sensitive CI; (b)
the `getPyramidPlot 25:27` dead entry; (c) the `source` “undesirable
function” hits = local var rename; (d) `commented_code_linter` IS active
via tags — the `#commented_code_linter = NULL` line is a dead no-op
(resolves \#30’s confusion). **Plan phases (vertical slices, 1 session
each):** P1 comment/doc-only + config hygiene · P2 `getPyramidPlot` · P3
behavior-none logic refactors (7 files) · P4 behavior-sensitive (5
files; `addSexAndAgeToGroup` is the ONE RED→GREEN→REFACTOR — exported,
`sapply`→[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html) w/ a contract
test) · P5 optional residual-152. **Phase-3E:** N/A — planning doc, no
runtime behavior changed; the plan’s lint counts/line numbers were all
firsthand-verified (build-equivalent for a plan = the evidence holds,
not a render). **Learning \#53** added (parse_settings=FALSE auditing
trap; line-drift both ways; verify-first). **\[news-vs-changelog\]** →
CHANGELOG only. **Commit:** one `docs:` close-out (the plan +
SESSION_NOTES + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS \#53) on
`add-methodology`, explicit `git add` (\[macos-dupe-scan\]:
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` excluded). \#30
stays OPEN.

**⟢ Carried — Session 52 (fix \#42 pkgdown, DONE; full detail below):**
**Task (Session 52 — 2026-06-11):** FIX issue \#42 — the `pkgdown`
workflow’s Build-site step fails on `master` because
`docs/methodology/` + `docs/planning/` are git-tracked INSIDE pkgdown’s
default `docs/` output dir, so a fresh CI clone’s `docs/` holds those
files + NO `pkgdown.yml` sentinel →
`clean_site()`/`check_dest_is_pkgdown()` refuses to wipe a dir it didn’t
build → exit 1. Deliverable = make `pkgdown` go GREEN on master. (DONE)
**Started:** 2026-06-11 **Status:** **DONE — pkgdown is GREEN on master
(run `27362288625`, commit `c6ad23dd`: Build site SUCCESS + Deploy
SUCCESS); \#42 CLOSED.** Chose **Option 2 (repoint pkgdown), NOT the
issue’s recommended Option 1 (relocate the doc trees)** — surfaced the
override via `AskUserQuestion` because Option 1 conflicts with the
methodology framework’s own `docs/methodology/` convention: the SYNCED
`methodology_dashboard.py:109-110` scores
`docs/methodology`+`docs/methodology/workstreams` (20 of the 98 health
pts) and the SYNCED `SESSION_RUNNER.md` (13 refs) + `SAFEGUARDS.md` (1
ref) cross-link that path — none durably editable in-repo (re-synced
from upstream). **Load-bearing pkgdown internal (verified from installed
pkgdown 2.1.1 source + empirically):** `build_site_github_pages()` does
`as_pkgdown(pkg, override = list(destination = dest_dir))`, so its
`dest_dir="docs"` ARG **overrides** any `_pkgdown.yml destination:` →
setting the yml ALONE does NOT fix CI (owner proposed the yml; I
confirmed it’s necessary-but-insufficient and set BOTH). **Fix (commit
`fcc154e8`):** `.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml` — pass
`dest_dir="pkgdown_site"` to `build_site_github_pages` + deploy
`folder: docs → pkgdown_site`; `_pkgdown.yml` —
`destination: pkgdown_site` (keeps local `build_site()` consistent);
`.gitignore += pkgdown_site/`; `.Rbuildignore += ^pkgdown_site$`. NO
file moves; `docs/methodology`/`docs/planning`/dashboard/synced
cross-refs untouched; gh-pages URL unchanged. NO R/ or tests/ change →
suite byte-identical, R-CMD-check unaffected (stayed green on all 5
platforms on the PR). **Local proof BEFORE pushing:** simulated the
fresh-clone state in a temp package skeleton → `clean_site(dest=docs)`
ERRORS “is non-empty and not built by pkgdown” (reproduces \#42);
`clean_site(dest=pkgdown_site)` = clean no-op. **The fix UNMASKED a
separate pre-existing latent bug** (FM-#24-cousin: clean_site was the
FIRST of two failures): with clean_site resolved the build reached
vignette rendering and failed on `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd:209-229`
chunk `make-errorList-definition-tbl` —
`data.frame(Error=names(getEmptyErrorLst()), Definition=errorDescriptions)`
paired **10 error types vs 9 hardcoded descriptions** (“arguments imply
differing number of rows: 10, 9”). Root cause: the **NEW-45 “no period
in IDs” feature** (commit `5e228bd9`) added the `invalidIdChars` type to
[`getEmptyErrorLst()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getEmptyErrorLst.md)
without updating this vignette table. **Owner chose (via
`AskUserQuestion`) to fix the vignette too** (vs file-separately) →
added the missing `invalidIdChars` description in positional order
(after `duplicateIds`, before `changedCols`) using the package’s
canonical wording, commit `e89975c8`. **Why latent / why R-CMD-check was
green but pkgdown red:** `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` is
`.Rbuildignore`’d (`:36`) — R CMD build/check NEVER builds it (likely
excluded for CRAN tarball-size/time: it reads screenshots from the
also-`.Rbuildignore`’d `vignettes/shiny_app_use/`), but **pkgdown builds
vignettes from the source tree, ignoring `.Rbuildignore`**, so it was
the ONE pkgdown-built vignette R-CMD-check doesn’t cover → the only
place a render bug could hide (the other 3 — a2interactive, a3manual,
simulatedKValues — ARE check-built → green → render fine). **Validation
chain (all firsthand):** PR \#43 pkgdown run `27361729368` (fresh-clone,
pull_request, Deploy skipped) = SUCCESS, all 4 vignettes rendered;
merged PR \#43 `--merge` → master `c6ad23dd`; master push run
`27362288625` = Build site SUCCESS **+ Deploy SUCCESS** (exercises the
new `folder: pkgdown_site` → gh-pages). **\#42 auto-closed on merge**
(PR body “Fixes \#42”) + added a validation comment
(`#42 issuecomment-4682879576`). **Phase-3E = the live master pkgdown
run itself** (real fresh runner, real build+deploy). **Commits on
`add-methodology`:** `fcc154e8` (config fix) + `e89975c8` (vignette
fix) + this close-out (SESSION_NOTES + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS
\#52), explicit `git add` (\[macos-dupe-scan\]:
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` excluded).
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG only.

**Session 51 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 52): Score 9/10.** What
helped: S51’s handoff named \#42 as the suggested next with the EXACT
root cause (`docs/methodology`+`docs/planning` tracked in pkgdown’s
`docs/` → no sentinel → clean_site refuses), the 3 fix options +
Option-1 recommendation, the precise key files (`.gitignore:32-36`,
`_pkgdown.yml` “no destination → defaults to docs/”,
`.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml`), and the gotchas (a) R-CMD-check GREEN
— don’t mistake the pkgdown red for a package-correctness problem, (b)
lint \#30 + codecov independent of \#42. All of (a)/(b) held and saved
triage time. What was missing (the ding, and the reason I diverged from
the recommendation): S51 recommended **Option 1** without weighing that
the methodology framework PINS reference docs to `docs/methodology/`
(synced dashboard scoring + synced SESSION_RUNNER/SAFEGUARDS cross-refs)
— so Option 1 would break things I can’t durably fix; and it didn’t
anticipate the LATENT vignette bug behind the clean_site failure (it
couldn’t — that was invisible until clean_site was resolved). What was
wrong: nothing factual; the Option-1 rec was sound on its face but
superseded by the synced-convention constraint, which I surfaced. ROI:
very high.

**Self-assessment (Session 52): 9/10.** Research before action: oriented
fully (SAFEGUARDS + SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top,
dashboard, git, issues), then read the issue, the sync mechanism (no
`bin/sync` in-repo → external tooling I can’t inspect → makes the
relocate-vs-repoint choice genuinely owner-gated), the full 17-file
reference inventory classified synced-vs-owned, and the pkgdown
internals (read `build_site_github_pages` source AND empirically proved
the `dest_dir` override) BEFORE proposing a fix. Verified, didn’t
assume: proved the fix with a faithful fresh-clone SIMULATION locally
(reproduced the exact \#42 error, showed the fix is a no-op) THEN with a
real fresh-clone CI run, then with the master push (Build+Deploy). Did
NOT silently follow OR silently override the owner’s written Option-1
recommendation — surfaced the synced-convention conflict + the pkgdown
override via `AskUserQuestion` with exact actions spelled out. Honored
the owner’s mid-stream correction (the yml `destination`) precisely —
confirmed it necessary-but-insufficient and set both yml + workflow arg.
When the fix unmasked a SECOND bug, did NOT scope-creep silently —
surfaced it (root-caused to NEW-45, characterized the whack-a-mole
risk + the .Rbuildignore reason it was latent) and let the owner choose
to fix-or-defer. Stakeholder decisions posed: 4 (fix-approach, merge,
vignette-scope, plus honoring the yml correction) — all answered as
posed; 0 corrections. Minor ding: a truly exhaustive pre-push audit
might have rendered all 4 vignettes locally to catch the
ColonyManagerTutorial bug before the first CI cycle (I judged local
render unreliable vs CI’s fresh `local::.` install — defensible, but it
cost one CI round-trip). Met/exceeded the S47–S51 bar.

**⇒ SUGGESTED NEXT = \#30 (lintr cleanup) — the last standing CI red.**
With \#42 closed, the pkgdown workflow is GREEN on master; the ONLY
remaining red across the CI matrix is **lint** (issue \#30,
known/accepted style debt — it failed on PR \#43 as expected). Greening
lint would make the entire master CI matrix green. It’s an
enhancement-labeled cleanup (`lintr` until satisfied), naturally
incremental. Other standing backlog candidates unchanged: **\#34**
(integrate
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
in modInput, bug), **\#38/#32/#26** (configurable auto-gen ID format),
**\#37** (exported-but-unused functions). **Branch model (unchanged from
S51):** the \#42 fix is now ON master via PR \#43 (`c6ad23dd`);
**`add-methodology` remains the historical dev branch** and holds THIS
close-out (master got the fix commits via the merge but NOT these
close-out notes — they live on add-methodology; the next
add-methodology→master sync or PR carries them). Repo left checked out
on `add-methodology`. **Key files (the \#42 fix, for reference):**
`.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml` (`dest_dir`/`folder` at the Build
site + Deploy steps), `_pkgdown.yml` (`destination: pkgdown_site`),
`.gitignore` (`pkgdown_site/`), `.Rbuildignore` (`^pkgdown_site$`),
`vignettes/ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd:209-230` (the error-table chunk).
**Gotchas for the next session:** (a) pkgdown builds ALL vignettes from
source IGNORING `.Rbuildignore` — so a `.Rbuildignore`’d vignette can
break pkgdown while R-CMD-check stays green; if you add/edit error types
in
[`getEmptyErrorLst()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getEmptyErrorLst.md),
update the `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` table in lockstep (positional
pairing). (b) `build_site_github_pages(dest_dir=...)` OVERRIDES
`_pkgdown.yml destination:` — change the workflow ARG, not just the yml,
to move pkgdown’s CI output dir. (c) the local `docs/` still holds the
old Dec-2025 pkgdown build + the tracked methodology/planning; pkgdown
no longer writes there — harmless cruft. (d) `methodology_dashboard.py`,
`SESSION_RUNNER.md`, `SAFEGUARDS.md` are SYNCED (don’t durably edit;
customize via CLAUDE.md).

**R-CMD-check passed on ALL 5 platforms** (macOS, Windows, ubuntu
release/devel/oldrel-1) + test-coverage passed → package correctness
intact. Surfaced the merge decision via `AskUserQuestion`; owner chose
**“merge now, fix pkgdown later”** → logged the pkgdown collision as
**issue \#42** (NOT fixed this session — 1-and-done) and merged.
**Post-merge live validation (the owner-designated gate):**
`workflow_dispatch`-ed `shinytest2` on master → run **27356752221
SUCCESS** (~19 min, \< 30-min timeout). Confirmed FIRSTHAND from the run
log that all **13 per-module groups (fresh `Rscript` each)** reported
`passed>0 failed=0 error=0` (app 7 · boundary 14 · breeding 26 ·
data-ready 32 · error-states 15 · genetic-value 22 · home-nav 15 · input
19 · pedigree 23 · pyramid 12 · settings-about 4 · summary-stats 8 ·
workflow-integration 8 → “All 13 E2E module groups passed.”). **Both S34
live-run watch items RESOLVED on the FIRST run:** (1) renv lib-path
resolution under `RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false` —
`R CMD INSTALL` + every AppDriver subprocess booted
[`library(nprcgenekeepr)`](https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/);
(2) the 23-in-one-process Chrome flake — the 8e-7 per-module
fresh-process grouping produced ZERO transient errors (first
environmental confirmation it holds; per-group isolation means any
future transient reds only its own group + is independently
re-dispatchable). **Closed \#40**
(`gh issue close 40 --reason completed`) with a full validation comment
(`#40 issuecomment-4682226145`). **Phase-3E = the live master
`shinytest2` run itself** (real runner, real Chrome, real workflow —
\#31). Close-out committed on `add-methodology` (the historical dev
branch; SESSION_NOTES + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS \#51) via explicit
`git add` (\[macos-dupe-scan\]:
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` excluded).
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** → CHANGELOG only. Transient (NOT committed):
`/tmp/regression_s51.R`, `/tmp/pr_body_s51.md`, `/tmp/pkgdown_issue.md`,
`/tmp/issue40_close.md`.

**Session 50 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 51): Score 9/10.** What
helped: the handoff named the EXACT sequence
(push→PR→merge→dispatch→watch→close \#40), the two S34 watch items, the

all mapped directly onto the deliverable and held under firsthand

that the FIRST-EVER remote PR CI would expose pre-existing
**pkgdown/lint/codecov** reds — S50’s work was CI-config-only and never

held. ROI: very high.

**Self-assessment (Session 51): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +

merge (SAFEGUARDS “don’t start from a broken state”). Did NOT merge

confirmed both S34 items. Stayed 1-and-done: logged the pkgdown fix as
\#42 instead of scope-creeping it into the promotion. Honesty: stated

flake is gone forever. Stakeholder corrections: 0 (the single decision

didn’t predict it would FAIL until the log). Met/exceeded the S47–S50
bar.

root cause + 3 fix options are in issue \#42; after the fix, re-dispatch
`pkgdown` (`gh workflow run pkgdown.yaml --ref master`) or push and
watch the per-push run. **Key files:** `.gitignore:32-36` (the
`docs/*` + un-ignore rules), `_pkgdown.yml` (no `destination:` →
defaults to `docs/`), `.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml`. **Gotchas:** (a)

package-correctness problem; (b) the shinytest2 flake fix is confirmed
on ONE live run, NOT proven-forever — if a future nightly reds a SINGLE
group, re-dispatch (that’s the per-group isolation working, not
necessarily a regression); (c) lint (#30) + codecov are independent
reds, not caused by \#42.
